sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Height, T1.Weight FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Killed > (SELECT AVG(Killed) FROM perpetrator) EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name, T1.Height, T1.Weight FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Country = 'China'
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Retrieve the name, height, and weight of perpetrators who have killed more than the average number of victims, excluding any from China.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Height, T1.Weight FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Killed > (SELECT AVG(Killed) FROM perpetrator) EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name, T1.Height, T1.Weight FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Country = 'China'
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Fetch the name, height, and weight of perpetrators who have killed more than the average number of people, while excluding any who are from China.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Total_Killed_And_Injured FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN (SELECT Perpetrator_ID, Killed + Injured AS Total_Killed_And_Injured FROM perpetrator) AS T3 ON T2.Perpetrator_ID = T3.Perpetrator_ID WHERE T1.People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM perpetrator WHERE Country = 'South Korea' OR Country = 'China') ORDER BY T3.Total_Killed_And_Injured DESC
result_summary: Fetch the names of perpetrators from either South Korea or China and their total casualty count, ordered from the highest number of casualties to the lowest.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.dept_name FROM student AS T1 JOIN takes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID JOIN teaches AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id AND T2.sec_id = T3.sec_id AND T2.semester = T3.semester AND T2.year = T3.year JOIN instructor AS T4 ON T3.ID = T4.ID WHERE T4.salary > (SELECT AVG(T5.salary) FROM instructor AS T5 WHERE T5.dept_name = T4.dept_name) GROUP BY T1.ID, T1.name, T1.dept_name
result_summary: This query retrieves the unique name and department name of students who are taking a course taught by an instructor whose salary is above their department's average.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.dept_name FROM student AS T1 JOIN takes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID JOIN teaches AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id AND T2.sec_id = T3.sec_id AND T2.semester = T3.semester AND T2.year = T3.year JOIN instructor AS T4 ON T3.ID = T4.ID WHERE T4.salary > (SELECT AVG(T5.salary) FROM instructor AS T5 WHERE T5.dept_name = T4.dept_name) GROUP BY T1.ID, T1.name, T1.dept_name
result_summary: Fetch the names and departments of students who have taken a course from an instructor whose salary is greater than the average salary within that instructor's department.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.dept_name, T3.course_count FROM instructor AS T1 JOIN (SELECT T2.id, COUNT(T2.course_id) AS course_count FROM teaches AS T2 GROUP BY T2.id HAVING COUNT(T2.course_id) > 1) AS T3 ON T1.id = T3.id WHERE T1.dept_name IN (SELECT dept_name FROM department WHERE budget > 200000)
result_summary: Retrieve the name, department, and course count for instructors who teach more than one course and belong to a department with a budget greater than 200,000.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.Country FROM operate_company AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id JOIN airport AS T3 ON T2.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT company_id FROM flight GROUP BY company_id HAVING count(*) >= 1) EXCEPT SELECT T1.name, T3.Country FROM operate_company AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id JOIN airport AS T3 ON T2.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T1.Incorporated_in = 'China'
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Get the name of each operating company and the country of the airport it flies to, for all companies not incorporated in China.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.Country FROM operate_company AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id JOIN airport AS T3 ON T2.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT company_id FROM flight GROUP BY company_id HAVING count(*) >= 1) EXCEPT SELECT T1.name, T3.Country FROM operate_company AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id JOIN airport AS T3 ON T2.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T1.Incorporated_in = 'China'
result_summary: Fetch the name of each operating company and the country of an airport it flies to, excluding any companies that were incorporated in China.

sql: SELECT T3.name FROM operate_company AS T3 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T3.id = T2.company_id JOIN airport AS T1 ON T2.airport_id = T1.id WHERE T1.id IN (SELECT T4.airport_id FROM flight AS T4 GROUP BY T4.airport_id HAVING avg(T4.Altitude) > 3000) EXCEPT SELECT T5.name FROM operate_company AS T5 WHERE T5.Incorporated_in = 'China'
result_summary: Fetch the names of operating companies that fly into airports with an average flight altitude greater than 3000, excluding any companies incorporated in China.

sql: SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T2.instID IN (SELECT T3.instID FROM Inst AS T3 WHERE T3.country = 'USA') EXCEPT SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T2.authOrder = 1
result_summary: Fetch the first and last names of authors from USA institutions, excluding any authors who are listed as the first author on a publication.

sql: SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname, T3.name FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T2.instID = T3.instID WHERE T1.authID IN (SELECT authID FROM Authorship WHERE authOrder = 1 INTERSECT SELECT authID FROM Authorship WHERE authOrder = 2) GROUP BY T1.fname, T1.lname, T3.name
result_summary: Fetch the name and institution of authors who have been listed as both the first and second author.

sql: SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname, T3.name FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T2.instID = T3.instID WHERE T1.authID IN (SELECT authID FROM Authorship WHERE authOrder = 1 INTERSECT SELECT authID FROM Authorship WHERE authOrder = 2) GROUP BY T1.fname, T1.lname, T3.name
result_summary: Retrieve the names and institutions of authors who have served as both the first and second author on publications.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM people AS T2 JOIN body_builder AS T1 ON T2.People_ID = T1.People_ID WHERE T1.Total > (SELECT AVG(Total) FROM body_builder) UNION SELECT T2.Name FROM people AS T2 JOIN body_builder AS T1 ON T2.People_ID = T1.People_ID WHERE T1.Clean_Jerk = 177.5
result_summary: Fetch the names of bodybuilders who either have a total score greater than the average or a Clean and Jerk score of 177.5.
Fetch the names and lifting scores for bodybuilders who have an above-average total lift and a below-average body weight.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM people AS T2 JOIN body_builder AS T1 ON T2.People_ID = T1.People_ID WHERE T1.Total > (SELECT AVG(Total) FROM body_builder) UNION SELECT T2.Name FROM people AS T2 JOIN body_builder AS T1 ON T2.People_ID = T1.People_ID WHERE T1.Clean_Jerk = 177.5
result_summary: Fetch the names of bodybuilders whose total score is above the average or whose clean and jerk score is exactly 177.5.

sql:
SELECT T.Region_name FROM (SELECT T1.Region_id, T1.Region_name, SUM(T3.Number_Deaths) AS total_deaths FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id JOIN storm AS T3 ON T2.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Region_id) AS T WHERE T.total_deaths > (SELECT AVG(Number_Deaths) FROM storm)
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id WHERE T2.Number_city_affected > 10.0
result_summary: Fetch the names of regions where the total storm-related deaths are greater than the average and where more than 10 cities have been affected.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_name, SUM(T3.Number_Deaths) FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id  =  T2.Region_id JOIN storm AS T3 ON T2.Storm_ID  =  T3.Storm_ID WHERE T3.Damage_millions_USD  >  (SELECT avg(Damage_millions_USD) FROM storm) GROUP BY T1.Region_name ORDER BY SUM(T3.Number_Deaths) DESC
result_summary: Fetch the total number of deaths for each region caused by storms with above-average damage, ordered from the region with the most deaths to the least.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 WHERE T1.Region_id IN (SELECT T2.Region_id FROM affected_region AS T2 JOIN storm AS T3 ON T2.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID WHERE T3.Max_speed > 972 INTERSECT SELECT T2.Region_id FROM affected_region AS T2 JOIN storm AS T3 ON T2.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID WHERE T3.Damage_millions_USD > 10.75)
result_summary: Fetch the names of regions that were affected by storms with a maximum speed greater than 972 and were also affected by storms causing damage greater than 10.75 million USD.

sql: SELECT T1.Model, COUNT(T2.Record_ID) FROM aircraft AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Aircraft_ID = T2.Aircraft_ID WHERE T2.Pilot_ID IN (SELECT Pilot_ID FROM pilot WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM pilot)) GROUP BY T1.Model ORDER BY COUNT(T2.Record_ID) DESC
result_summary: Fetch the count of flights for each aircraft model by pilots who are older than the average pilot age, ordered from most to least flights.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID WHERE T2.Aircraft_ID IN (SELECT Aircraft_ID FROM aircraft WHERE Order_Year > 1995) INTERSECT SELECT Pilot_name FROM pilot WHERE Join_Year < 2000
result_summary: Fetch the names of pilots who both joined before the year 2000 and have flown an aircraft ordered after 1995.

sql:
SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T3.Order_Year = 1992
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 WHERE T1.Rank < (SELECT AVG(Rank) FROM pilot)
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Fetch the names of pilots who have a rank below the average and have also flown an aircraft that was ordered in 1992.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Location FROM track AS T1 WHERE T1.Track_ID IN (SELECT T2.Track_ID FROM race AS T2 GROUP BY T2.Track_ID HAVING count(*) >= 2) AND T1.Year_Opened > (SELECT avg(Year_Opened) FROM track)
result_summary: Fetch the name and location of tracks that have hosted two or more races and were opened later than the average opening year of all tracks.

sql:
SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Race_ID)
FROM track AS T1
JOIN race AS T2
  ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID
GROUP BY
  T1.Track_ID
HAVING
  COUNT(T2.Race_ID) > (
    SELECT
      AVG(Race_Count)
    FROM (
      SELECT
        COUNT(Race_ID) AS Race_Count
      FROM race
      GROUP BY
        Track_ID
    ) AS T3
  );
result_summary: Return the names of tracks and the number of races they've hosted, but only for those tracks with a race count higher than the overall average number of races per track.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Location FROM track AS T1 WHERE T1.Track_ID IN (SELECT T2.Track_ID FROM race AS T2 GROUP BY T2.Track_ID HAVING count(*) >= 2) AND T1.Year_Opened > (SELECT avg(Year_Opened) FROM track)
result_summary: Fetch the name and location of tracks that have hosted two or more races and were opened after the average opening year of all tracks.

sql: SELECT T1.supplier_name FROM Suppliers AS T1 JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2 ON T1.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id GROUP BY T1.supplier_id HAVING sum(T2.total_value_purchased) > (SELECT avg(total_value_purchased) FROM Product_Suppliers) INTERSECT SELECT T3.supplier_name FROM Suppliers AS T3 JOIN Supplier_Addresses AS T4 ON T3.supplier_id = T4.supplier_id WHERE T4.address_id IN (SELECT address_id FROM Addresses)
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Fetch the names of suppliers whose total purchased value is greater than the average and who also have a registered address.

sql: SELECT T1.supplier_name FROM Suppliers AS T1 JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2 ON T1.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id GROUP BY T1.supplier_id HAVING sum(T2.total_value_purchased) > (SELECT avg(total_value_purchased) FROM Product_Suppliers) INTERSECT SELECT T3.supplier_name FROM Suppliers AS T3 JOIN Supplier_Addresses AS T4 ON T3.supplier_id = T4.supplier_id WHERE T4.address_id IN (SELECT address_id FROM Addresses)
result_summary: Fetch the names of suppliers who have a registered address and whose total purchased value is greater than the average purchase value across all suppliers.

sql: SELECT T1.supplier_name, sum(T2.total_value_purchased) FROM Suppliers AS T1 JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2 ON T1.supplier_id  =  T2.supplier_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id  =  T3.product_id WHERE T3.product_type_code  =  'Clothes' GROUP BY T1.supplier_name HAVING sum(T2.total_value_purchased)  >  (SELECT sum(T5.total_value_purchased) FROM Suppliers AS T4 JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T5 ON T4.supplier_id  =  T5.supplier_id WHERE T4.supplier_name  =  'Tesco')
result_summary: Fetch the supplier name and total purchased value for suppliers of 'Clothes' whose total value is greater than the total purchased value from the supplier 'Tesco'.

sql: SELECT id, name, City FROM airport WHERE Country = 'United States';
result_summary: Fetch the data containing the ID, name, and city for all airports located in the United States.

sql: SELECT name, Type, Principal_activities FROM operate_company WHERE Incorporated_in = 'China';
result_summary: Fetch the name, type, and principal activities of companies that are incorporated in China.

sql: SELECT f.Vehicle_Flight_number, f.Date, f.Velocity, a.name AS airport_name, a.City FROM flight AS f JOIN airport AS a ON f.airport_id = a.id WHERE a.City = 'Amsterdam';
result_summary: Fetch the vehicle flight number, date, velocity, and airport name for all flights associated with airports in the city of Amsterdam.

sql: SELECT Pilot, COUNT(id) AS number_of_flights, AVG(Velocity) AS average_velocity FROM flight WHERE Pilot = 'Thompson' GROUP BY Pilot;
result_summary: Fetch a summary calculating the total number of flights and average velocity for the pilot named 'Thompson'.

sql: SELECT oc.name, oc.Type, f.Vehicle_Flight_number, f.Date FROM operate_company AS oc JOIN flight AS f ON oc.id = f.company_id WHERE oc.Type = 'Joint Venture';
result_summary: Fetch the company name, type, vehicle flight number, and date for all flights operated by companies that are a 'Joint Venture'.

sql: SELECT oc.name, oc.Group_Equity_Shareholding FROM operate_company oc WHERE oc.Group_Equity_Shareholding > (SELECT AVG(Group_Equity_Shareholding) FROM operate_company WHERE Incorporated_in = 'China') AND oc.Type = 'Joint Venture';
result_summary: This query fetches the names and group equity shareholdings of joint venture companies with an equity shareholding greater than the average for companies incorporated in China.

sql: WITH CompanyFlightCounts AS (SELECT company_id, COUNT(id) AS flight_count FROM flight GROUP BY company_id) SELECT oc.name, cfc.flight_count FROM operate_company oc JOIN CompanyFlightCounts cfc ON oc.id = cfc.company_id ORDER BY cfc.flight_count DESC, oc.name ASC;
result_summary: Retrieve the names of operating companies along with their total flight counts, ordered from most flights to least, and then by company name alphabetically.

sql: SELECT a.Country, a.City, COUNT(f.id) AS total_flights FROM flight f JOIN airport a ON f.airport_id = a.id WHERE f.Altitude > 3000 GROUP BY a.Country, a.City HAVING COUNT(f.id) >= 1 ORDER BY total_flights DESC;
result_summary: List the countries and cities with their total number of flights above 3000 altitude, ordered from the highest number of flights to the lowest.

sql: SELECT f.Vehicle_Flight_number, f.Date, f.Pilot FROM flight f WHERE f.airport_id IN (SELECT id FROM airport WHERE Country = 'Netherlands') AND f.company_id IN (SELECT id FROM operate_company WHERE Principal_activities = 'Cargo airline');
result_summary: Fetch the vehicle flight number, date, and pilot for flights from cargo airlines that took place at an airport in the Netherlands.

sql: SELECT a.name AS airport_name, a.Country, oc.name AS company_name, oc.Type FROM flight f JOIN airport a ON f.airport_id = a.id JOIN operate_company oc ON f.company_id = oc.id WHERE (a.Country = 'Iceland' AND oc.Type = 'Joint Venture') OR f.Altitude = 0.0;
result_summary: Fetch the airport name, country, operating company name, and company type for flights that are either operated by a Joint Venture in Iceland or have an altitude of zero.

sql: SELECT * FROM classroom WHERE building = 'Lamberton';
result_summary: Fetch all data for classrooms located in the Lamberton building.

sql: SELECT dept_name, budget FROM department WHERE dept_name = 'History';
result_summary: Fetch the name and budget for the 'History' department.

sql: SELECT name, dept_name FROM student WHERE tot_cred = 100;
result_summary: Fetch the name and department name of students who have exactly 100 total credits.

sql: SELECT ID, name, salary FROM instructor WHERE dept_name = 'Marketing';
result_summary: Fetch the ID, name, and salary of all instructors from the Marketing department.

sql: SELECT course_id, title FROM course WHERE credits = 4;
result_summary: Fetch the course ID and title for all courses that are worth exactly 4 credits.

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM course AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.dept_name = T2.dept_name WHERE T1.dept_name = 'Biology';
result_summary: Fetch the titles of all courses that belong to the 'Biology' department.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM takes AS T1 JOIN student AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T1.course_id = '401';
result_summary: Fetch the names of all students who are enrolled in the course with the ID '401'.

sql: SELECT T1.building, T1.room_number, T2.capacity FROM section AS T1 JOIN classroom AS T2 ON T1.building = T2.building AND T1.room_number = T2.room_number WHERE T1.semester = 'Fall' AND T1.year = 2010;
result_summary: This query retrieves the building, room number, and capacity for all classrooms that hosted a section during the Fall 2010 semester.

sql: SELECT T1.name AS student_name, T3.name AS advisor_name FROM student AS T1 JOIN advisor AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.s_ID JOIN instructor AS T3 ON T2.i_ID = T3.ID WHERE T1.ID = '24746';
result_summary: Fetch the name of a student and their corresponding advisor's name for the student with the specific ID '24746'.

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.course_id) AS number_of_sections FROM teaches AS T1 JOIN instructor AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID GROUP BY T2.name;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each instructor and the total number of course sections they teach.

sql: SELECT T2.title AS prerequisite_title FROM prereq AS T1 JOIN course AS T2 ON T1.prereq_id = T2.course_id WHERE T1.course_id = '376';
result_summary: Fetch the title of the prerequisite course for the course that has the course ID '376'.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM student AS T1 JOIN advisor AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.s_ID JOIN takes AS T3 ON T1.ID = T3.ID JOIN teaches AS T4 ON T2.i_ID = T4.ID AND T3.course_id = T4.course_id AND T3.sec_id = T4.sec_id AND T3.semester = T4.semester AND T3.year = T4.year;
result_summary: Fetch the names of all students who are taking a course taught by their own academic advisor.

sql: WITH DeptAvgSalary AS (SELECT dept_name, AVG(salary) as avg_sal FROM instructor GROUP BY dept_name) SELECT dept_name FROM DeptAvgSalary WHERE avg_sal = (SELECT MAX(avg_sal) FROM DeptAvgSalary);
result_summary: Fetch the name of the department with the highest average instructor salary.

sql: WITH RankedInstructors AS (SELECT name, dept_name, salary, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY dept_name ORDER BY salary DESC) as salary_rank FROM instructor) SELECT name, dept_name, salary FROM RankedInstructors WHERE salary_rank = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name, department name, and salary of the instructor with the highest salary within each department.

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM course AS T1 LEFT JOIN prereq AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id WHERE T2.prereq_id IS NULL;
result_summary: Fetch the titles of all courses that do not have any prerequisites.

sql: SELECT T2.dept_name, AVG(T4.capacity) AS avg_classroom_capacity FROM course AS T2 JOIN section AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id JOIN classroom AS T4 ON T3.building = T4.building AND T3.room_number = T4.room_number WHERE T2.dept_name = 'Civil Eng.' GROUP BY T2.dept_name;
result_summary: Fetch the department name and the average classroom capacity for the 'Civil Eng.' department.

sql: SELECT name FROM student WHERE ID NOT IN (SELECT T1.ID FROM takes AS T1 JOIN teaches AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id AND T1.sec_id = T2.sec_id AND T1.semester = T2.semester AND T1.year = T2.year JOIN instructor AS T3 ON T2.ID = T3.ID WHERE T3.name = 'McKinnon');
result_summary: Fetch the names of all students who have never taken a course taught by the instructor named 'McKinnon'.

sql: WITH SectionCounts AS (SELECT course_id, semester, year, COUNT(sec_id) AS num_sections FROM section GROUP BY course_id, semester, year) SELECT course_id, semester, year, num_sections FROM SectionCounts ORDER BY num_sections DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: This query finds the single course offering (identified by course, semester, and year) that had the most sections and returns its details along with that section count.

sql: SELECT T1.dept_name, T1.budget, SUM(T2.salary) AS total_salaries FROM department AS T1 JOIN instructor AS T2 ON T1.dept_name = T2.dept_name GROUP BY T1.dept_name, T1.budget HAVING SUM(T2.salary) > T1.budget;
result_summary: Fetch the department name, budget, and total instructor salaries for departments where the total salaries are greater than the department's budget.

sql: SELECT name, tot_cred FROM student WHERE ID NOT IN (SELECT ID FROM takes WHERE semester = 'Spring' AND year = 2007) ORDER BY tot_cred DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name and total credits of the student with the most credits who did not take any course in the Spring 2007 semester.

sql: WITH AdviseeCounts AS (SELECT i_ID, COUNT(s_ID) AS advisee_count FROM advisor GROUP BY i_ID), RankedAdvisors AS (SELECT T2.dept_name, T2.name, T1.advisee_count, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY T2.dept_name ORDER BY T1.advisee_count DESC) AS rnk FROM AdviseeCounts AS T1 JOIN instructor AS T2 ON T1.i_ID = T2.ID) SELECT dept_name, name, advisee_count FROM RankedAdvisors WHERE rnk = 1;
result_summary: Finds the instructor with the most advisees in each department, returning their department name, name, and total advisee count.

sql: SELECT * FROM storm WHERE Name = 'One';
result_summary: Fetch all available information for the storm with the specific name 'One'.

sql: SELECT Region_name, Region_code FROM region WHERE Region_name = 'Albania';
result_summary: Fetch the region name and region code for the specific region of Albania.

sql: SELECT s.Name, s.Max_speed FROM storm AS s JOIN affected_region AS ar ON s.Storm_ID = ar.Storm_ID WHERE ar.Region_id = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name and maximum speed of storms that have affected a specific region with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT r.Region_name, ar.Number_city_affected FROM region AS r JOIN affected_region AS ar ON r.Region_id = ar.Region_id WHERE ar.Storm_ID = 3;
result_summary: Fetch the name of the region and the number of cities affected within it for the storm with an ID of 3.

sql: SELECT s.Name, r.Region_name, ar.Number_city_affected FROM storm AS s JOIN affected_region AS ar ON s.Storm_ID = ar.Storm_ID JOIN region AS r ON ar.Region_id = r.Region_id WHERE s.Name = 'One';
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Fetch the regions and the number of cities affected by the storm specifically named 'One'.

sql: SELECT Name, Damage_millions_USD FROM storm WHERE Damage_millions_USD > (SELECT AVG(Damage_millions_USD) FROM storm);
result_summary: Fetch the name and damage in millions of USD for storms whose damage exceeds the average damage of all storms.

sql: SELECT r.Region_name FROM region AS r WHERE r.Region_id IN (SELECT ar.Region_id FROM affected_region AS ar GROUP BY ar.Region_id HAVING SUM(ar.Number_city_affected) > 20);
result_summary: Fetch the names of regions that have a total of more than 20 affected cities.

sql: WITH StormSummary AS (SELECT Storm_ID, SUM(Number_city_affected) as TotalCitiesAffected FROM affected_region GROUP BY Storm_ID) SELECT s.Name, ss.TotalCitiesAffected FROM storm AS s JOIN StormSummary AS ss ON s.Storm_ID = ss.Storm_ID ORDER BY ss.TotalCitiesAffected DESC;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each storm and the total number of cities it affected, ordered by the total number of cities affected in descending order.

sql: SELECT Name, Dates_active, Number_Deaths FROM storm WHERE Number_Deaths = (SELECT MAX(Number_Deaths) FROM storm);
result_summary: Fetch the name, active dates, and number of deaths for the storm that caused the maximum number of deaths.

sql: SELECT s.Name, s.Number_Deaths, r.Region_name FROM storm AS s JOIN affected_region AS ar ON s.Storm_ID = ar.Storm_ID JOIN region AS r ON ar.Region_id = r.Region_id WHERE s.Number_Deaths > 5;
result_summary: Fetch the name, number of deaths, and the name of the affected region for storms that caused more than 5 deaths.

sql: SELECT name FROM Inst WHERE country = 'USA';
result_summary: Fetch the names of all institutions located in the USA.

sql: SELECT fname, lname FROM Authors WHERE authID = 50;
result_summary: Fetch the first and last name of the author with an ID of 50.

sql: SELECT title FROM Papers WHERE paperID = 201;
result_summary: Fetch the title of a specific paper with a paper ID of 201.

sql: SELECT authID, paperID, authOrder FROM Authorship WHERE instID = 1000;
result_summary: Fetch the author ID, paper ID, and author order for all authorship records associated with institution ID 1000.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT country FROM Inst ORDER BY country;
result_summary: Fetch a distinct list of countries from the 'Inst' table, ordered alphabetically.

sql: SELECT T2.title, T3.name FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Papers AS T2 ON T1.paperID = T2.paperID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T1.instID = T3.instID WHERE T1.authID = 51;
result_summary: Fetch the titles of papers and the names of their associated institutions for the author with an ID of 51.

sql: SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname, COUNT(T1.paperID) AS PaperCount FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID GROUP BY T2.authID, T2.fname, T2.lname ORDER BY PaperCount DESC;
result_summary: This query retrieves the first name, last name, and total paper count for each author, ordered from the most to the least number of papers written.

sql: SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T2.paperID = 200 ORDER BY T2.authOrder;
result_summary: Fetch the first and last names of authors for the paper with ID 200, ordered by their authorship sequence.

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.authID) AS NumberOfAuthors FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Inst AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID GROUP BY T2.name;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each institution and the corresponding count of its unique authors.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.title FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Papers AS T2 ON T1.paperID = T2.paperID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T1.instID = T3.instID WHERE T3.country = 'UK';
result_summary: Fetch the unique titles of papers from authors affiliated with institutions located in the UK.

sql: SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T2.paperID = 201 AND T2.authOrder = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the first and last name of the primary author (author order 1) for the paper with an ID of 201.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.authOrder) FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Inst AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID WHERE T2.name = 'University of Oxford';
result_summary: Fetch the average author order for all authorships from the institution 'University of Oxford'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.fname, T2.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T1.paperID IN (SELECT paperID FROM Authorship WHERE authID = 51) AND T1.authID <> 51;
result_summary: Fetch the distinct first and last names of all authors who co-authored a paper with the author whose ID is 51.

sql: SELECT T2.name FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Inst AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID GROUP BY T2.instID, T2.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.paperID) >= 2;
result_summary: Fetch the names of institutions that have authored at least two distinct papers.

sql: SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID GROUP BY T1.authID, T2.fname, T2.lname HAVING MIN(T1.authOrder) = 1 AND MAX(T1.authOrder) = 1;
result_summary: Fetches the first and last names of authors who are the sole author of all their publications.

sql: WITH AuthorPaperCounts AS (SELECT instID, authID, COUNT(paperID) as paper_count FROM Authorship GROUP BY instID, authID), RankedAuthors AS (SELECT instID, authID, paper_count, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY instID ORDER BY paper_count DESC) as rn FROM AuthorPaperCounts) SELECT T3.name, T2.fname, T2.lname, T1.paper_count FROM RankedAuthors AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T1.instID = T3.instID WHERE T1.rn = 1;
result_summary: This query retrieves the name of each institution, along with the first name, last name, and paper count of its most published author.

sql: SELECT A1.fname, A1.lname, A2.fname, A2.lname, T3.title FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.paperID = T2.paperID AND T1.authID < T2.authID JOIN Authors AS A1 ON T1.authID = A1.authID JOIN Authors AS A2 ON T2.authID = A2.authID JOIN Papers AS T3 ON T1.paperID = T3.paperID WHERE T1.paperID = 200;
result_summary: Fetch the first and last names for all pairs of co-authors who worked on a specific paper, along with the title of that paper.

sql: WITH AuthorCountryPapers AS (SELECT T3.country, T1.authID, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.paperID) AS paper_count FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T1.instID = T3.instID GROUP BY T3.country, T1.authID) SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname, T1.country, T1.paper_count, DENSE_RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY T1.country ORDER BY T1.paper_count DESC) AS country_rank FROM AuthorCountryPapers AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID;
result_summary: This query retrieves the full name, country, and total paper count for authors, assigning a rank to each author within their country based on their number of publications.

sql: SELECT T2.title FROM Authorship AS T1 JOIN Papers AS T2 ON T1.paperID = T2.paperID GROUP BY T1.paperID, T2.title HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T1.instID) > 1;
result_summary: Fetch the titles of papers that have been authored by individuals from more than one distinct institution.

sql: SELECT p.title, COUNT(a.authID) AS author_count, MAX(CASE WHEN au.fname = 'Jeremy' AND au.lname = 'Gibbons' THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END) AS has_specific_author FROM Papers AS p JOIN Authorship AS a ON p.paperID = a.paperID JOIN Authors AS au ON a.authID = au.authID GROUP BY p.paperID, p.title;
result_summary: This query retrieves each paper's title, a total count of its authors, and a flag indicating whether 'Jeremy Gibbons' is one of the authors.

sql: WITH PaperAuthorCount AS (SELECT paperID, COUNT(authID) AS num_authors FROM Authorship GROUP BY paperID) SELECT T2.title, T1.num_authors FROM PaperAuthorCount AS T1 JOIN Papers AS T2 ON T1.paperID = T2.paperID ORDER BY T1.num_authors DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Fetch the title and author count for the paper with the highest number of authors.

sql: SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname, T3.title FROM (SELECT authID, paperID, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY authID ORDER BY paperID DESC) AS rn FROM Authorship) AS T1 JOIN Authors AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Papers AS T3 ON T1.paperID = T3.paperID WHERE T1.rn = 1;
result_summary: This query fetches the first name, last name, and the title of the most recent paper for each author.

sql: SELECT Name, Height, Weight FROM people WHERE "Home Town" = 'Los Angeles, CA';
result_summary: Fetch the name, height, and weight of people whose home town is Los Angeles, CA.

sql: SELECT Location, Date FROM perpetrator WHERE Country = 'South Korea';
result_summary: Fetch the location and date of perpetrators from the country of South Korea.

sql: SELECT * FROM perpetrator WHERE Year = 1938.0;
result_summary: Fetch all available data for perpetrators from events that took place in the year 1938.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Height > 6.5;
result_summary: Fetch the names of people whose height is greater than 6.5.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM perpetrator WHERE Country = 'China';
result_summary: Count the total number of perpetrators whose country is China.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Killed, T1.Injured FROM perpetrator AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Location = 'Uiryeong';
result_summary: Fetch the name of the perpetrator and the number of people killed and injured for the incident that occurred in Uiryeong.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Location FROM perpetrator AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Weight = 220.0;
result_summary: Fetch the name and location of any perpetrator who has a weight of 220.0.

sql: SELECT Country, AVG(Killed) AS Average_Killed, AVG(Injured) AS Average_Injured FROM perpetrator GROUP BY Country;
result_summary: Fetch the average number of people killed and injured, aggregated by country.

sql: SELECT T2."Home Town", COUNT(T1.Perpetrator_ID) AS NumberOfIncidents FROM perpetrator AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T2."Home Town";
result_summary: This query retrieves the total number of incidents, grouped by the perpetrator's home town.

sql: SELECT Name, "Home Town" FROM people ORDER BY Weight DESC LIMIT 5;
result_summary: This query retrieves the names and hometowns of the 5 heaviest people from a list.

sql: SELECT T1.Location, T1.Country, T1.Year FROM perpetrator AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'Ron Baxter';
result_summary: Fetch the location, country, and year of the perpetrator identified as the person named 'Ron Baxter'.

sql: SELECT Year, SUM(Killed) AS TotalKilled, SUM(Injured) AS TotalInjured FROM perpetrator GROUP BY Year HAVING SUM(Killed) > 30;
result_summary: This query retrieves the total number of people killed and injured, aggregated by year, for all years where the total number of deaths exceeded 30.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Killed FROM perpetrator AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Killed > (SELECT AVG(Killed) FROM perpetrator);
result_summary: This query retrieves the names of perpetrators and the number of people they killed for only those who have killed more than the average number of people.

sql: SELECT T1.Country, T2.Name, T1.Killed FROM perpetrator AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Killed = (SELECT MAX(T3.Killed) FROM perpetrator AS T3 WHERE T3.Country = T1.Country);
result_summary: Fetch the name of the perpetrator with the highest kill count for each country, along with their country and the number of people killed.

sql: SELECT p.Name, pr.Location, pr.Year FROM people AS p JOIN perpetrator AS pr ON p.People_ID = pr.People_ID WHERE p.People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM people WHERE Weight > 200.0) AND pr.Year > 1950;
result_summary: Fetch the name, location, and year of perpetrators who weigh over 200 for incidents occurring after the year 1950.

sql: SELECT T2."Home Town", SUM(T1.Killed) AS TotalKilled FROM perpetrator AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Country = 'South Korea' GROUP BY T2."Home Town" ORDER BY TotalKilled DESC;
result_summary: This query lists the home towns of perpetrators from South Korea along with the total number of people they killed, ordered by the total killed in descending order.

sql: WITH IncidentsInTargetCountries AS (SELECT Perpetrator_ID, People_ID, Location, Country, Injured FROM perpetrator WHERE Country = 'Japan' OR Country = 'China') SELECT p.Name, i.Location, i.Country, i.Injured FROM IncidentsInTargetCountries AS i JOIN people AS p ON i.People_ID = p.People_ID WHERE i.Injured > 10;
result_summary: Fetch the name of the person, location, country, and number of injured for incidents that occurred in Japan or China and resulted in more than 10 injuries.

sql: SELECT p.Name, p.Height, p.Weight, p."Home Town" FROM people AS p WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM perpetrator AS pr WHERE pr.People_ID = p.People_ID);
result_summary: Fetch the name, height, weight, and hometown of all people who are not identified as perpetrators.

sql: SELECT Name, "Home Town", (SELECT SUM(Killed) FROM perpetrator WHERE People_ID = p.People_ID) AS TotalKilled FROM people AS p WHERE p."Home Town" = 'Placentia, CA';
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Fetch the name, home town, and total number of people killed for each person whose home town is 'Placentia, CA'.

sql: SELECT p.Name, p.Height, pr.Killed, pr.Injured FROM people p JOIN perpetrator pr ON p.People_ID = pr.People_ID WHERE pr.Killed > (SELECT Killed FROM perpetrator WHERE Location = 'Kaio') AND pr.Injured > (SELECT Injured FROM perpetrator WHERE Location = 'Kaio');
result_summary: Fetch the name, height, and the number of people killed and injured for perpetrators who have both killed and injured more people than the perpetrator located in 'Kaio'.

sql: SELECT Country, Year, SUM(Killed + Injured) AS TotalCasualties FROM perpetrator GROUP BY Country, Year ORDER BY Country, Year;
result_summary: Fetch the total casualties for each country and year, ordered by country and then by year.

sql: SELECT p.Name FROM people AS p JOIN perpetrator AS pr ON p.People_ID = pr.People_ID WHERE pr.Year = 1982.0 UNION SELECT p.Name FROM people AS p JOIN perpetrator AS pr ON p.People_ID = pr.People_ID WHERE pr.Year = 1995.0;
result_summary: Fetch the names of people identified as perpetrators from either the year 1982 or 1995.

sql: SELECT Name, Height, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM perpetrator WHERE People_ID = p.People_ID) as IncidentCount FROM people p WHERE p.Height > 6.3;
result_summary: Retrieve the name, height, and total number of incidents for each person with a height greater than 6.3.

sql: WITH RankedIncidents AS (SELECT Perpetrator_ID, Country, Killed, RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY Country ORDER BY Killed DESC) as rnk FROM perpetrator) SELECT ri.Country, p.Name, ri.Killed FROM RankedIncidents ri JOIN people p ON ri.Perpetrator_ID = p.People_ID WHERE ri.rnk = 1;
result_summary: Find the perpetrator with the most killings in each country, returning their name, country, and the number of people they killed.

sql: SELECT pr.Location, pr.Country, p.Name FROM perpetrator pr LEFT JOIN people p ON pr.People_ID = p.People_ID WHERE pr.Injured < 20 AND pr.Country = 'China';
result_summary: Fetch the location, country, and name of perpetrators from China who have injured fewer than 20 people.

sql: SELECT Name, Birth_Date, Birth_Place FROM people WHERE People_ID = 2;
result_summary: Fetch the name, birth date, and birth place for the specific person with a People ID of 2.

sql: SELECT Body_Builder_ID, Total FROM body_builder WHERE Snatch = 142.5;
result_summary: Fetch the ID and total score of a bodybuilder who has a snatch lift of 142.5.

sql: SELECT * FROM people ORDER BY Height DESC;
result_summary: Fetch all information for people, ordered by their height in descending order.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM body_builder;
result_summary: Fetch the total count of body builders.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Birth_Place FROM people;
result_summary: Fetch a list of distinct birth places from the people records.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Snatch, T2.Clean_Jerk FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Ty Conklin';
result_summary: Fetch the name, snatch, and clean and jerk weights for the bodybuilder named 'Ty Conklin'.

sql: SELECT T1.Birth_Place, AVG(T2.Total) AS Average_Total FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T1.Birth_Place;
result_summary: This query returns the average total for bodybuilders, grouped by each person's birth place.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Weight FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Clean_Jerk = 175.0;
result_summary: Fetch the name and weight of bodybuilders who can Clean & Jerk exactly 175.0.

sql: SELECT MAX(Snatch) AS Max_Snatch, MIN(Clean_Jerk) AS Min_Clean_Jerk FROM body_builder;
result_summary: Find the maximum snatch and minimum clean and jerk values from the body builder data.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people ORDER BY Weight ASC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name of the person with the lowest weight.

sql: SELECT Name, Birth_Date FROM people WHERE Birth_Place = 'Glenview, Illinois' AND Height > 190;
result_summary: Fetch the name and birth date for all people born in Glenview, Illinois who have a height greater than 190.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Height FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID ORDER BY T2.Total DESC;
result_summary: This query fetches the name and height of people who are bodybuilders, ordered by their total score in descending order.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY T2.Total DESC) AS Total_Rank FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Fetch the name, total score, and rank of each bodybuilder, with the rank calculated based on the total score in descending order.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Snatch FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Snatch > (SELECT AVG(Snatch) FROM body_builder);
result_summary: This query fetches the names and snatch scores for bodybuilders whose snatch score is greater than the overall average.

sql: WITH BuilderStats AS (SELECT People_ID, Total FROM body_builder WHERE Total >= 315.0) SELECT p.Name, p.Birth_Place, bs.Total FROM people AS p JOIN BuilderStats AS bs ON p.People_ID = bs.People_ID;
result_summary: Fetch the names, birthplaces, and totals for bodybuilders who have a total of 315.0 or greater.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM people p WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM body_builder bb WHERE bb.People_ID = p.People_ID AND bb.Clean_Jerk = 177.5);
result_summary: Fetch the name and height of any person who is a bodybuilder with a clean and jerk of 177.5.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE Birth_Place = 'Port Huron, Michigan' UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Total = 315.0;
result_summary: Fetch the names of people who were either born in Port Huron, Michigan, or are bodybuilders with a total of 315.0.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Total, T2.Total - (SELECT AVG(Total) FROM body_builder) AS Difference_From_Average FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Fetch the name and total for each bodybuilder, along with the difference between their total and the average total.

sql: SELECT T1.Birth_Place FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Birth_Place = 'Glenview, Illinois' GROUP BY T1.Birth_Place HAVING AVG(T2.Total) = 315.0;
result_summary: Find the birth place of bodybuilders from 'Glenview, Illinois' only if the average 'Total' for that location is exactly 315.0.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, CASE WHEN T1.Weight > 85.0 THEN 'Heavyweight' WHEN T1.Weight BETWEEN 75.0 AND 85.0 THEN 'Middleweight' ELSE 'Lightweight' END AS Weight_Class, T2.Total FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Retrieve the name and total for each bodybuilder, along with a calculated weight class based on their weight.

sql: SELECT p.Name FROM people AS p JOIN (SELECT People_ID FROM people WHERE Birth_Place = 'Anchorage, Alaska' INTERSECT SELECT People_ID FROM body_builder WHERE Clean_Jerk = 177.5) AS T3 ON p.People_ID = T3.People_ID;
result_summary: Fetch the name of a person who was born in Anchorage, Alaska, and is also a bodybuilder with a Clean & Jerk of 177.5.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Birth_Place, T2.Snatch, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY T1.Birth_Place ORDER BY T2.Snatch DESC) as Rank_In_Location FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Fetch the name, birthplace, and snatch weight of bodybuilders, ranking them by their snatch performance within each birth location.

sql: SELECT p1.Name FROM people AS p1 JOIN body_builder AS b1 ON p1.People_ID = b1.People_ID WHERE b1.Total > (SELECT b2.Total FROM body_builder AS b2 JOIN people AS p2 ON b2.People_ID = p2.People_ID WHERE p2.Name = 'Ty Conklin');
result_summary: Fetch the names of bodybuilders who have a total score greater than the score of the bodybuilder named 'Ty Conklin'.

sql: SELECT Name, Weight, (SELECT Total FROM body_builder WHERE People_ID = p.People_ID) AS Lift_Total FROM people p WHERE p.Birth_Place = 'Port Huron, Michigan';
result_summary: Fetch the name, weight, and associated lift total for each person born in Port Huron, Michigan.

sql: WITH AverageLifts AS (SELECT AVG(Snatch) as AvgSnatch, AVG(Clean_Jerk) as AvgCleanJerk FROM body_builder) SELECT p.Name FROM people p JOIN body_builder b ON p.People_ID = b.People_ID, AverageLifts al WHERE b.Snatch > al.AvgSnatch AND b.Clean_Jerk > al.AvgCleanJerk;
result_summary: Fetch the names of bodybuilders whose Snatch and Clean and Jerk lifts are both above the average.

sql: SELECT Name, Location FROM track;
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Fetch the name and location of every track available in the database.

sql: SELECT Name, Class, Date FROM race WHERE Class = 'DP/GT';
result_summary: Fetch the data containing the name, class, and date of races where the class is 'DP/GT'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name AS RaceName, T2.Name AS TrackName FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each race along with the name of its corresponding track.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Date FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Location = 'Fontana, CA';
result_summary: Fetch the name and date of races that took place at a track located in Fontana, CA.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T2.Location, T2.Seating FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Mexico City 250';
result_summary: Fetch the name, location, and seating of the track that hosted the 'Mexico City 250' race.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Race_ID) AS NumberOfRaces FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each track and the total number of races held at that track.

sql: SELECT Name FROM track WHERE Seating = (SELECT MAX(Seating) FROM track);
result_summary: Fetch the name of the track that has the maximum seating capacity.

sql: SELECT Name, Class FROM race WHERE Track_ID IN (SELECT Track_ID FROM track WHERE Year_Opened > 2000.0);
result_summary: Fetch the name and class of races that were held at tracks opened after the year 2000.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T2.Location FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID GROUP BY T2.Name, T2.Location HAVING COUNT(T1.Race_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Retrieve the name and location of every track that has hosted more than one race.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Location FROM track AS T1 WHERE T1.Year_Opened > (SELECT T2.Year_Opened FROM track AS T2 WHERE T2.Name = 'Darlington Raceway') AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM race AS T3 WHERE T3.Track_ID = T1.Track_ID);
result_summary: Fetch the name and location of all tracks that opened after Darlington Raceway and have hosted at least one race.

sql: SELECT Pilot_name, Rank, Age, Nationality FROM pilot WHERE Team = 'Bradley';
result_summary: Fetch the name, rank, age, and nationality of pilots who are on the 'Bradley' team.

sql: SELECT Model, Fleet_Series, Powertrain, Fuel_Propulsion FROM aircraft WHERE Manufacturer = 'Gillig' AND Order_Year = 1992;
result_summary: Fetch the model, fleet series, powertrain, and fuel propulsion for all aircraft manufactured by 'Gillig' that were ordered in the year 1992.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, T1.Age FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T3.Model = 'Phantom (High Floor)';
result_summary: Fetch the name and age of pilots who have flown an aircraft with the model 'Phantom (High Floor)'.

sql: SELECT T1.Manufacturer, T1.Model, T2.Date FROM aircraft AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Aircraft_ID = T2.Aircraft_ID JOIN pilot AS T3 ON T2.Pilot_ID = T3.Pilot_ID WHERE T3.Pilot_name = "Jermaine O'Neal";
result_summary: Fetch the manufacturer, model, and date of the aircraft flown by the pilot named Jermaine O'Neal.

sql: SELECT T1.Rank, COUNT(T2.Record_ID) AS number_of_flights FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID GROUP BY T1.Rank ORDER BY number_of_flights DESC;
result_summary: Fetch the total number of flights for each pilot rank, ordered by the number of flights in descending order.

sql: SELECT Pilot_name, Rank, Age FROM pilot WHERE Pilot_ID NOT IN (SELECT T1.Pilot_ID FROM pilot_record AS T1 JOIN aircraft AS T2 ON T1.Aircraft_ID = T2.Aircraft_ID WHERE T2.Manufacturer = 'Gillig');
result_summary: Fetch the name, rank, and age of pilots who have never flown an aircraft from the manufacturer 'Gillig'.

sql: WITH RankedFlights AS (SELECT T3.Pilot_name, T1.Manufacturer, T2.Date, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY T1.Manufacturer ORDER BY T2.Date DESC) as rn FROM aircraft AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Aircraft_ID = T2.Aircraft_ID JOIN pilot AS T3 ON T2.Pilot_ID = T3.Pilot_ID) SELECT Pilot_name, Manufacturer, Date FROM RankedFlights WHERE rn = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the pilot's name and the date of the most recent flight for each aircraft manufacturer.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Aircraft_ID) AS distinct_aircraft_flown FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T1.Nationality = 'United States' GROUP BY T1.Pilot_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.Aircraft_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name and the count of distinct aircraft flown for United States pilots who have piloted more than one aircraft.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name AS Pilot1_Name, T2.Pilot_name AS Pilot2_Name, T3.Manufacturer, T3.Model FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS pr1 ON T1.Pilot_ID = pr1.Pilot_ID JOIN pilot_record AS pr2 ON pr1.Aircraft_ID = pr2.Aircraft_ID AND pr1.Pilot_ID < pr2.Pilot_ID JOIN pilot AS T2 ON pr2.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON pr1.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID;
result_summary: Fetch pairs of pilots who have flown the same aircraft, showing their names and the aircraft's manufacturer and model.

sql: SELECT T3.Manufacturer, T3.Model FROM aircraft AS T3 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T3.Aircraft_ID = T2.Aircraft_ID JOIN pilot AS T1 ON T2.Pilot_ID = T1.Pilot_ID WHERE T1.Age = (SELECT MAX(Age) FROM pilot);
result_summary: Find the manufacturer and model of the aircraft flown by the oldest pilot.

sql: SELECT staff_id, staff_name, staff_gender FROM Staff WHERE staff_gender = '1';
result_summary: Fetch the ID, name, and gender of all staff members whose gender is '1'.

sql: SELECT product_type_code, COUNT(product_id) AS number_of_products FROM Products GROUP BY product_type_code;
result_summary: Fetch the total number of products for each product type code.

sql: SELECT p.product_name, p.product_price FROM Products AS p JOIN Product_Suppliers AS ps ON p.product_id = ps.product_id JOIN Suppliers AS s ON ps.supplier_id = s.supplier_id WHERE s.supplier_name = 'Tesco';
result_summary: Fetch the name and price of all products supplied by 'Tesco'.

sql: SELECT s.staff_name, sda.job_title_code FROM Staff AS s JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments AS sda ON s.staff_id = sda.staff_id JOIN Departments AS d ON sda.department_id = d.department_id WHERE d.department_name = 'marketing';
result_summary: Fetch the name and job title code of all staff members who work in the marketing department.

sql: SELECT ds.store_name, dsc.dept_store_chain_name FROM Department_Stores AS ds JOIN Department_Store_Chain AS dsc ON ds.dept_store_chain_id = dsc.dept_store_chain_id WHERE dsc.dept_store_chain_name = 'South';
result_summary: Fetch the store name and its corresponding chain name for all department stores that belong to the 'South' chain.

sql: WITH RankedProducts AS (SELECT product_name, product_type_code, product_price, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY product_type_code ORDER BY product_price DESC) as rn FROM Products) SELECT product_name, product_type_code, product_price FROM RankedProducts WHERE rn = 1;
result_summary: This query retrieves the name, type code, and price of the most expensive product within each product type category.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id WHERE T2.order_id IN (SELECT T3.order_id FROM Order_Items AS T3 JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_name = 'black jeans');
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Fetch the customer ID and name for all customers who have ordered the product 'black jeans'.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT C.customer_name, C.customer_email FROM Customers AS C JOIN Customer_Orders AS CO ON C.customer_id = CO.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS OI ON CO.order_id = OI.order_id JOIN Products AS P ON OI.product_id = P.product_id JOIN Product_Suppliers AS PS ON P.product_id = PS.product_id JOIN Suppliers AS S ON PS.supplier_id = S.supplier_id WHERE S.supplier_name = 'Tesco' AND CO.order_status_code = 'Completed';
result_summary: Fetch the unique name and email of customers who have a completed order for a product supplied by 'Tesco'.

sql: SELECT T3.address_id, T3.address_details FROM Addresses AS T3 WHERE T3.address_id IN (SELECT T1.address_id FROM Customer_Addresses AS T1 INTERSECT SELECT T2.address_id FROM Supplier_Addresses AS T2);
result_summary: Fetches the address ID and details for addresses that are common to both customers and suppliers.

sql: SELECT Artist, Famous_Title FROM artist WHERE Age = 34;
result_summary: Fetch the artist and famous title for all artists who are 34 years old.

sql: SELECT Song, Weeks_on_Top FROM volume WHERE Artist_ID = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the song title and the number of weeks it was on top from the volume table for the artist with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT Music_Festival, Category FROM music_festival WHERE Result = 'Awarded';
result_summary: Fetch the name and category of music festivals that have received an award.

sql: SELECT * FROM artist WHERE Famous_Release_date = 'January 21, 2008';
result_summary: Fetch all data for artists who had a famous release on January 21, 2008.

sql: SELECT Volume_Issue, Issue_Date FROM volume WHERE Weeks_on_Top > 1;
result_summary: Fetch the volume issue and issue date for all volumes that remained on top for more than one week.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, T2.Song FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T1.Artist = 'Gorgoroth';
result_summary: Fetch the artist and song for all records where the artist is 'Gorgoroth'.

sql: SELECT T1.Song, T2.Music_Festival FROM volume AS T1 JOIN music_festival AS T2 ON T1.Volume_ID = T2.Volume WHERE T2.Category = 'Best Song';
result_summary: Fetch the song and music festival name for all entries belonging to the 'Best Song' category.

sql: SELECT T2.Artist, COUNT(T1.Volume_ID) FROM volume AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID GROUP BY T2.Artist;
result_summary: Fetch the data containing each artist and the total count of their associated volumes.

sql: SELECT Category, COUNT(ID) FROM music_festival GROUP BY Category;
result_summary: Fetch the total count of music festival entries for each distinct category.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Weeks_on_Top = 1.0;
result_summary: Fetch the names of artists who have been on top for exactly one week.

sql: SELECT T2.Artist FROM volume AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID GROUP BY T2.Artist HAVING COUNT(T1.Song) > 1;
result_summary: Fetch the names of artists who have more than one song.

sql: SELECT T1.Song FROM volume AS T1 JOIN music_festival AS T2 ON T1.Volume_ID = T2.Volume WHERE T2.Result = 'Awarded' AND T2.Category = 'Best Song';
result_summary: Fetch the names of all songs that were awarded in the 'Best Song' category at a music festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, T2.Song, T3.Music_Festival, T3.Result FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T1.Age > 30;
result_summary: Fetch the artist, song, music festival, and result for all artists who are over 30 years old.

sql: SELECT Artist, Famous_Title FROM artist WHERE Artist_ID IN (SELECT Artist_ID FROM volume WHERE Volume_ID IN (SELECT Volume FROM music_festival WHERE Category = 'Best Song' AND Result = 'Nominated'));
result_summary: Fetch the artist's name and a famous title for any artist who has been nominated for 'Best Song' at a music festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, T1.Age FROM artist AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.Artist = T2.Artist WHERE T1.Artist_ID <> T2.Artist_ID;
result_summary: Fetch the name and age of artists who have duplicate names in the database.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, T1.Age, T3.Category FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T3.Result = 'Awarded' ORDER BY T1.Age DESC;
result_summary: Fetch the data containing the artist's name, age, and award category for artists who have won an award, ordered by the artist's age in descending order.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 LEFT JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Volume_ID IS NULL;
result_summary: Fetch the names of all artists who do not have an associated volume.

sql: SELECT Music_Festival FROM music_festival WHERE Volume IN (SELECT Volume_ID FROM volume WHERE Weeks_on_Top = (SELECT MAX(Weeks_on_Top) FROM volume));
result_summary: Fetch the names of music festivals whose associated volume has been on top for the longest duration.

sql: WITH FestivalNominations AS (SELECT Volume, Category FROM music_festival WHERE Result = 'Nominated') SELECT T1.Song, T1.Weeks_on_Top, T3.Category FROM volume AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID JOIN FestivalNominations AS T3 ON T1.Volume_ID = T3.Volume WHERE T2.Artist = 'Ophiolatry';
result_summary: Fetch the song title, weeks on top, and nomination category for all nominated songs by the artist 'Ophiolatry'.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, T2.Song, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY T1.Artist ORDER BY T2.Weeks_on_Top DESC) as RankNum FROM artist as T1 JOIN volume as T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID;
result_summary: Fetch the artist and song, along with a rank calculated for each artist's songs based on the descending number of weeks spent on top.

sql: SELECT Artist FROM artist WHERE Age > 30 UNION SELECT Song FROM volume WHERE Weeks_on_Top > 1;
result_summary: Fetch a combined list of artists who are older than 30 and songs that have been on top for more than one week.

sql: SELECT Artist FROM artist WHERE Artist_ID NOT IN (SELECT T2.Artist_ID FROM music_festival AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Volume = T2.Volume_ID WHERE T1.Music_Festival = '34th England Academy Prize');
result_summary: Fetch the names of all artists who did not perform at the '34th England Academy Prize' music festival.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.Music_Festival FROM music_festival AS T3 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T1.Age = 34 AND T2.Volume_ID = T3.Volume);
result_summary: Fetch the distinct names of music festivals that are associated with an artist who is 34 years old.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, AVG(T2.Weeks_on_Top) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID GROUP BY T1.Artist HAVING AVG(T2.Weeks_on_Top) > 1.5;
result_summary: This query fetches the name and average weeks on top for each artist who has an average of more than 1.5 weeks on top.

sql: SELECT T3.Artist, T3.Famous_Title FROM music_festival AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Volume = T2.Volume_ID JOIN artist AS T3 ON T2.Artist_ID = T3.Artist_ID WHERE T1.Result = 'Nominated' INTERSECT SELECT Artist, Famous_Title FROM artist WHERE Age = 35;
result_summary: Fetches the artist and famous title for all artists who are 35 years old and have also been nominated at a music festival.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, SUM(T1.Killed), AVG(T1.Injured) FROM perpetrator AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T2.Name
result_summary: Returns 5 rows containing each perpetrator's name, the total number of people they killed, and the average number of people they injured.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, SUM(T1.Killed) FROM perpetrator AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Killed > (SELECT AVG(Injured) FROM perpetrator) GROUP BY T2.Name
result_summary: Returns 3 rows containing the name of each perpetrator and the total number of people they killed, filtered to include only those who killed more people than the average number of people injured.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, SUM(T1.Killed) FROM perpetrator AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Killed > (SELECT AVG(Injured) FROM perpetrator) GROUP BY T2.Name
result_summary: Returns 3 rows containing the name of each perpetrator and the total number of people they killed, filtered to only include perpetrators who killed more than the average number of injured people.

sql:
SELECT T1.dept_name, COUNT(T2.ID)
FROM department AS T1
JOIN instructor AS T2
  ON T1.dept_name = T2.dept_name
GROUP BY
  T1.dept_name;
result_summary: Returns 17 rows containing each department's name and the corresponding count of instructors within it.

sql:
SELECT T1.name FROM student AS T1 JOIN takes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.semester = 'Fall' AND T2.year = 2003
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.name FROM student AS T1 JOIN takes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.semester = 'Spring' AND T2.year = 2007
result_summary: Returns 749 rows containing the names of students who took courses in both the Fall 2003 and Spring 2007 semesters.

sql:
SELECT T1.dept_name, AVG(T1.salary)
FROM instructor AS T1
WHERE T1.dept_name IN (
  SELECT T2.dept_name
  FROM course AS T2
  WHERE T2.credits > 3
  GROUP BY T2.dept_name
  HAVING count(*) > 1
)
GROUP BY T1.dept_name;
result_summary: Returns 17 rows containing the department name and the average instructor salary for departments that offer more than one course with over 3 credits.

sql: SELECT T2.name, COUNT(T1.id) FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id = T2.id GROUP BY T2.name
result_summary: Returns 8 rows containing the name of each airport and the total count of flights associated with it.

sql:
SELECT T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id JOIN airport AS T3 ON T1.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T3.name = 'Schiphol Airport'
INTERSECT
SELECT name FROM operate_company WHERE Type = 'Joint Venture'
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the names of 'Joint Venture' companies that operate flights at 'Schiphol Airport'.

sql:
SELECT T1.name FROM airport AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.airport_id WHERE T2.Pilot = 'Thompson'
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.name FROM airport AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.airport_id JOIN operate_company AS T3 ON T2.company_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Type = 'Joint Venture'
result_summary: Returns the names of 2 airports that are both served by pilot 'Thompson' and by a 'Joint Venture' company.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.authID) FROM Inst AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID GROUP BY T1.name
result_summary: Returns 8 rows containing the name of each institution and the corresponding count of its authorships.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.lname, T3.name FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T2.instID = T3.instID WHERE T1.authID IN (SELECT authID FROM Authorship GROUP BY authID HAVING count(*) > 1)
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the last name and institution name for an author who has more than one authorship record.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.fname, T1.lname FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T2.instID = T3.instID WHERE T3.country = 'UK' AND T1.authID IN (SELECT authID FROM Authorship GROUP BY authID HAVING count(*) > 1)
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the first and last name of a distinct author from a UK institution who has more than one publication.

sql:
SELECT T2.Name, T1.Total
FROM body_builder AS T1
JOIN people AS T2
  ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID
WHERE T1.Total > 315.0;
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and total score for a bodybuilder with a total greater than 315.0.

sql:
SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Clean_Jerk = 175.0
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Snatch = 140.0
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of a person who is a bodybuilder with a clean and jerk of 175.0 and a snatch of 140.0.

sql:
SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Clean_Jerk = 175.0
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Snatch = 140.0
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name of the bodybuilder who has a clean and jerk of 175 and a snatch of 140.

sql:
SELECT T1.Region_name, SUM(T3.Damage_millions_USD)
FROM region AS T1
JOIN affected_region AS T2
  ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id
JOIN storm AS T3
  ON T2.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID
GROUP BY
  T1.Region_name;
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing the name of each region and its corresponding total storm damage in millions of USD.

sql:
SELECT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id JOIN storm AS T3 ON T2.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID WHERE T3.Damage_millions_USD = 13.0
EXCEPT
SELECT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id JOIN storm AS T3 ON T2.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID WHERE T3.Number_Deaths = 0
result_summary: Returns 1 row with the name of a region that was affected by a storm causing $13 million in damage, but not by a storm that caused zero deaths.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Storm_ID HAVING count(T2.Region_ID) > (SELECT avg(count_regions) FROM (SELECT count(Region_ID) AS count_regions FROM affected_region GROUP BY Storm_ID) AS T3)
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the name of each storm that affected more regions than the average number of regions affected by a storm.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, COUNT(T2.Aircraft_ID) FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID GROUP BY T1.Pilot_ID
result_summary: Returns 4 rows containing each pilot's name and the total number of aircraft they have piloted.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, T1.Age, T3.Record_Count FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Pilot_ID, COUNT(*) AS Record_Count FROM pilot_record GROUP BY Pilot_ID) AS T3 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T3.Pilot_ID WHERE T3.Record_Count > 1
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name, age, and total record count for any pilot with more than one record.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T3.Manufacturer = 'Gillig' INTERSECT SELECT Pilot_name FROM pilot WHERE Position = 'Center Team'
result_summary: Returns the name of the single pilot who is on the 'Center Team' and has also flown an aircraft manufactured by 'Gillig'.

sql: SELECT T2.name ,  COUNT(T1.race_id) FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.track_id  =  T2.track_id GROUP BY T2.name
result_summary: Returns 6 rows containing the name of each track and the total count of races held at that track.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM track AS T1 JOIN race AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID GROUP BY T1.Track_ID, T1.Name HAVING count(*) > 1 UNION SELECT Name FROM track WHERE Year_Opened < 1997.0
result_summary: Returns 7 rows containing the names of tracks that have either hosted more than one race or were opened before 1997.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T2.Location FROM track AS T2 JOIN race AS T1 ON T2.Track_ID = T1.Track_ID GROUP BY T2.Name, T2.Location HAVING count(*) > 1
result_summary: Returns 1 row containing the name and location of tracks that have hosted more than one race.

sql: SELECT SUM(T1.citation_num) FROM publication AS T1 WHERE T1.pid IN (SELECT T2.pid FROM publication_keyword AS T2 JOIN domain_keyword AS T3 ON T2.kid = T3.kid INTERSECT SELECT pid FROM publication WHERE jid IS NOT NULL)
result_summary: Returns a single row containing the total number of citations for all publications that are associated with a domain keyword and are also published in a journal.

sql:
SELECT
  T1.department_name,
  COUNT(T2.staff_id)
FROM Departments AS T1
JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments AS T2
  ON T1.department_id = T2.department_id
WHERE
  T2.job_title_code = 'Sales Person'
GROUP BY
  T1.department_name;
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the count of staff members with the 'Sales Person' job title for each department.

sql:
SELECT T5.supplier_name FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id JOIN Suppliers AS T5 ON T4.supplier_id = T5.supplier_id WHERE T1.order_status_code = 'Completed'
INTERSECT
SELECT T5.supplier_name FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id JOIN Suppliers AS T5 ON T4.supplier_id = T5.supplier_id WHERE T1.order_status_code = 'New'
result_summary: Returns 2 rows containing the names of suppliers who have supplied products for both 'Completed' and 'New' orders.

sql:
SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Products AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id WHERE T4.product_type_code = 'Clothes'
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.customer_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN Customer_Orders AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id JOIN Order_Items AS T3 ON T2.order_id = T3.order_id JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id JOIN Suppliers AS T5 ON T4.supplier_id = T5.supplier_id WHERE T5.supplier_name = 'Tesco'
result_summary: Returns 3 rows containing the names of customers who have ordered both clothes and products supplied by 'Tesco'.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people;
result_summary: Fetch the names from the people table.

sql: SELECT Location, Country FROM perpetrator;
result_summary: Fetch the location and country of every perpetrator in the dataset.

sql: SELECT Name, "Home Town" FROM people WHERE Height = 6.4 AND Weight = 205.0;
result_summary: Fetch the name and home town for all people with a height of 6.4 and a weight of 205.0.

sql: SELECT * FROM perpetrator WHERE Year = 1982;
result_summary: Fetch all records of perpetrators for incidents that occurred in the year 1982.

sql: SELECT People_ID, Name FROM people WHERE "Home Town" = 'Baton Rouge, LA';
result_summary: Fetch the ID and name of people whose hometown is Baton Rouge, LA.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Location, T2.Country FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Perpetrator_ID = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name, location, and country of the perpetrator with a specific ID of 1.

sql: SELECT Country, SUM(Killed) AS TotalKilled FROM perpetrator GROUP BY Country;
result_summary: Fetches the total number of individuals killed, summed up for each perpetrator's country.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Injured > 15;
result_summary: Fetch the names of perpetrators who have injured more than 15 people.

sql: SELECT Year, COUNT(Perpetrator_ID) AS NumberOfIncidents FROM perpetrator GROUP BY Year ORDER BY Year DESC;
result_summary: This query retrieves the total number of incidents for each year, ordered by year in descending order.

sql: SELECT AVG(Killed) AS AverageKilled FROM perpetrator WHERE Country = 'Japan';
result_summary: Calculate the average number of people killed by perpetrators from Japan.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Injured > (SELECT Injured FROM perpetrator WHERE Location = 'Zhaodong');
result_summary: Fetch the names of perpetrators who have injured more people than the perpetrator located in Zhaodong.

sql: SELECT T1.Country, T1.Location, T2.Name FROM perpetrator AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Killed = (SELECT MAX(Killed) FROM perpetrator AS T3 WHERE T3.Country = T1.Country);
result_summary: Fetch the country, location, and name of perpetrators who have the highest number of killed victims within their respective country.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, T1.Country, T1.Killed, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY T1.Country ORDER BY T1.Killed DESC) AS rank_in_country FROM perpetrator AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Fetch the name, country, and number of people killed by each perpetrator, along with a rank calculated for each perpetrator within their country based on the number of killings in descending order.

sql: WITH Heavy_People AS (SELECT People_ID, Name FROM people WHERE Weight > 200.0) SELECT T2.Date, T2.Location, T1.Name FROM Heavy_People AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID;
result_summary: Fetch the incident date, location, and name for all perpetrators who weigh more than 200.

sql: SELECT CASE WHEN Killed >= 50 THEN 'Massacre' WHEN Killed >= 30 AND Killed < 50 THEN 'High Fatality' ELSE 'Tragedy' END AS Severity_Category, COUNT(*) AS Incident_Count FROM perpetrator GROUP BY Severity_Category;
result_summary: This query counts the number of incidents for each severity category, where the category ('Massacre', 'High Fatality', or 'Tragedy') is determined by the number of people killed.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, (SELECT T2.Killed + T2.Injured FROM perpetrator AS T2 WHERE T2.People_ID = T1.People_ID) AS total_casualties FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM perpetrator);
result_summary: Fetch the name of each perpetrator along with the total number of casualties (killed and injured) they caused.

sql: SELECT Name FROM people WHERE People_ID NOT IN (SELECT People_ID FROM perpetrator WHERE Country = 'South Korea');
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Fetch the names of people who are not perpetrators from South Korea.

sql: SELECT Year, Location, Year - LAG(Year, 1, 0) OVER (ORDER BY Year) AS years_since_previous FROM perpetrator ORDER BY Year;
result_summary: Fetches the year, location, and the number of years since the previous perpetrator event, ordered chronologically by year.

sql: SELECT Name AS item, 'Person' AS type FROM people WHERE "Home Town" = 'Los Angeles, CA' UNION ALL SELECT Location, 'Location' FROM perpetrator WHERE Country = 'China';
result_summary: Fetch a list of items and their types, where the items are either the names of people from Los Angeles, CA, or the locations of perpetrators from China.

sql: SELECT Country, SUM(Injured) AS TotalInjured FROM perpetrator GROUP BY Country HAVING SUM(Injured) > 10;
result_summary: Fetch the total number of injured people grouped by country, including only those countries with more than 10 total injuries.

sql: SELECT * FROM student WHERE dept_name = 'History';
result_summary: Fetch all information for students where the department name is 'History'.

sql: SELECT title, credits FROM course WHERE credits = 4;
result_summary: Fetch the title and credits for all courses that have exactly 4 credits.

sql: SELECT building, room_number FROM classroom WHERE capacity > 25;
result_summary: Fetch the building and room number for all classrooms with a capacity greater than 25.

sql: SELECT dept_name, budget FROM department WHERE building = 'Taylor';
result_summary: Fetch the name and budget of all departments located in the 'Taylor' building.

sql: SELECT day, start_hr, start_min FROM time_slot WHERE time_slot_id = 'A';
result_summary: Fetch the day, starting hour, and starting minute for the specific time slot with an ID of 'A'.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.salary FROM instructor AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.dept_name = T2.dept_name WHERE T2.building = 'Taylor';
result_summary: Fetch the name and salary of instructors whose department is located in the 'Taylor' building.

sql: SELECT T2.title FROM prereq AS T1 JOIN course AS T2 ON T1.prereq_id = T2.course_id WHERE T1.course_id = '376';
result_summary: Fetch the title of the prerequisite course for the course with an ID of '376'.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T2.grade FROM student AS T1 JOIN takes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.course_id = '401' AND T2.semester = 'Fall' AND T2.year = 2003;
result_summary: Fetch the name and grade of students who took course '401' during the Fall semester of 2003.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.name AS advisor_name FROM student AS T1 JOIN advisor AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.s_ID JOIN instructor AS T3 ON T2.i_ID = T3.ID WHERE T1.ID = '24746';
result_summary: Fetch the student's name and their advisor's name for the specific student with ID '24746'.

sql: SELECT T1.course_id, T2.capacity FROM section AS T1 JOIN classroom AS T2 ON T1.building = T2.building AND T1.room_number = T2.room_number WHERE T1.semester = 'Fall' AND T1.year = 2010;
result_summary: Fetch the course ID and classroom capacity for all sections held in the Fall of 2010.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM instructor AS T1 JOIN teaches AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID JOIN section AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id AND T2.sec_id = T3.sec_id AND T2.semester = T3.semester AND T2.year = T3.year JOIN department AS T4 ON T1.dept_name = T4.dept_name WHERE T3.building = 'Lamberton' AND T4.building <> 'Lamberton';
result_summary: Fetch the names of all instructors who teach in the 'Lamberton' building, but whose department is not located in the 'Lamberton' building.

sql: SELECT T1.title FROM course AS T1 JOIN prereq AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id WHERE T2.prereq_id IN (SELECT course_id FROM course WHERE dept_name = 'Mech. Eng.');
result_summary: This query fetches the titles of courses that have a prerequisite course from the 'Mech. Eng.' department.

sql: SELECT dept_name, AVG(salary) FROM instructor GROUP BY dept_name HAVING AVG(salary) > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM instructor);
result_summary: Fetch the name and average salary of departments whose average salary is greater than the overall average salary of all instructors.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM student AS T1 JOIN takes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.semester = 'Fall' AND T2.year = 2010 AND T1.ID NOT IN (SELECT ID FROM takes WHERE semester = 'Spring' AND year = 2010);
result_summary: Fetch the names of students who took a course in the Fall 2010 semester but did not take any courses in the Spring 2010 semester.

sql: SELECT T1.dept_name, T1.name, T1.tot_cred FROM student AS T1 JOIN (SELECT dept_name, MAX(tot_cred) AS max_cred FROM student GROUP BY dept_name) AS T2 ON T1.dept_name = T2.dept_name AND T1.tot_cred = T2.max_cred;
result_summary: This query retrieves the department name, student name, and total credits for the student with the maximum total credits in each department.
