sql: SELECT T1.name FROM student AS T1 JOIN advisor AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.s_ID JOIN takes AS T3 ON T1.ID = T3.ID JOIN teaches AS T4 ON T3.course_id = T4.course_id AND T3.sec_id = T4.sec_id AND T3.semester = T4.semester AND T3.year = T4.year WHERE T2.i_ID = T4.ID;
result_summary: Fetch the data containing the names of all students who are taking a course from an instructor who is also their advisor.

sql: SELECT dept_name FROM course WHERE course_id NOT IN (SELECT course_id FROM section) GROUP BY dept_name;
result_summary: List the unique names of departments that contain courses without any assigned sections.

sql: SELECT T2.name, count(T1.course_id) FROM takes AS T1 JOIN student AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T1.grade = 'A-' GROUP BY T2.name;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each student and the total count of courses for which they received an 'A-' grade.

sql: SELECT dept_name, name, salary, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY dept_name ORDER BY salary DESC) as salary_rank FROM instructor;
result_summary: Fetch the name, salary, and department of each instructor, ranked by salary from highest to lowest within their respective department.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, SUM(T1.Killed) FROM perpetrator AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID GROUP BY T2.Name
result_summary: Fetch the name of each person and the total number of people they killed, grouped by the person's name.

sql:
SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Killed > 31
UNION
SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN perpetrator AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Injured > 30
result_summary: Fetch the names of perpetrators who have either killed more than 31 people or injured more than 30 people.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM perpetrator AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Country IN (SELECT Country FROM perpetrator GROUP BY Country HAVING sum(Killed) > sum(Injured))
result_summary: Fetch the names of perpetrators from countries where the total number of people killed is greater than the total number of people injured.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, SUM(T1.Killed) FROM perpetrator AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Killed > (SELECT AVG(Injured) FROM perpetrator) GROUP BY T2.Name
result_summary: Fetch the name and total number of people killed by each perpetrator, but only for those who have killed more people than the average number of people injured.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, SUM(T1.Killed) FROM perpetrator AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Killed > (SELECT AVG(Injured) FROM perpetrator) GROUP BY T2.Name
result_summary: Fetch the name and total number of people killed by each perpetrator who killed more individuals than the average number of people injured.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM perpetrator AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Country IN (SELECT Country FROM perpetrator GROUP BY Country HAVING sum(Killed) > sum(Injured))
result_summary: Fetch the names of perpetrators from countries where the total number of people killed is greater than the total number of people injured.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, SUM(T1.Killed) FROM perpetrator AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Killed > (SELECT AVG(Injured) FROM perpetrator) GROUP BY T2.Name
result_summary: Find the total number of people killed by each perpetrator, considering only incidents where the number killed exceeds the average number of people injured.

sql:
SELECT T1.dept_name, COUNT(T2.ID)
FROM department AS T1
JOIN instructor AS T2
  ON T1.dept_name = T2.dept_name
GROUP BY
  T1.dept_name;
result_summary: Fetch each department's name and the corresponding number of instructors.

sql:
SELECT T1.dept_name, AVG(T1.salary)
FROM instructor AS T1
WHERE T1.dept_name IN (
  SELECT T2.dept_name
  FROM course AS T2
  WHERE T2.credits > 3
  GROUP BY T2.dept_name
  HAVING count(*) > 1
)
GROUP BY T1.dept_name;
result_summary: Fetch the average instructor salary for each department that offers more than one course with a credit value greater than 3.

sql:
SELECT T1.name FROM student AS T1 JOIN takes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.semester = 'Fall' AND T2.year = 2003
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.name FROM student AS T1 JOIN takes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.semester = 'Spring' AND T2.year = 2007
result_summary: This query fetches the names of students who took a course in the Fall semester of 2003 and also took a course in the Spring semester of 2007.

sql:
SELECT T1.name, T1.salary FROM instructor AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.dept_name = T2.dept_name WHERE T2.building = 'Taylor'
UNION
SELECT T1.name, T1.salary FROM instructor AS T1 JOIN department AS T2 ON T1.dept_name = T2.dept_name WHERE T2.building = 'Chandler'
result_summary: Fetch the names and salaries of all instructors from the 'Taylor' and 'Chandler' buildings.

sql:
SELECT T1.name FROM student AS T1 JOIN takes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.semester = 'Fall' AND T2.year = 2003
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.name FROM student AS T1 JOIN takes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.semester = 'Spring' AND T2.year = 2007
result_summary: This query fetches the names of students who took a course in the Fall 2003 semester and also took a course in the Spring 2007 semester.

sql:
SELECT T1.name FROM student AS T1 JOIN takes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.semester = 'Fall' AND T2.year = 2003
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.name FROM student AS T1 JOIN takes AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.semester = 'Spring' AND T2.year = 2007
result_summary: Fetch the names of students who took a course in both the Fall 2003 semester and the Spring 2007 semester.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM student AS T1 WHERE T1.ID IN (SELECT T2.s_ID FROM advisor AS T2 JOIN instructor AS T3 ON T2.i_ID = T3.ID WHERE T3.dept_name = 'Cybernetics' INTERSECT SELECT ID FROM takes WHERE semester = 'Fall' AND year = 2010)
result_summary: This query retrieves the names of students who are advised by an instructor from the 'Cybernetics' department and also took a course in the Fall 2010 semester.

sql: SELECT T2.name ,  count(*) FROM flight AS T1 JOIN airport AS T2 ON T1.airport_id  =  T2.id GROUP BY T2.name
result_summary: Fetches the name of each airport and the total number of flights associated with it.

sql:
SELECT T2.name FROM flight AS T1 JOIN operate_company AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.id JOIN airport AS T3 ON T1.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T3.name = 'Schiphol Airport'
INTERSECT
SELECT name FROM operate_company WHERE Type = 'Joint Venture'
result_summary: Fetch the names of operating companies that are 'Joint Venture' types and also operate flights at 'Schiphol Airport'.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.name, COUNT(T2.id) FROM operate_company AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id JOIN airport AS T3 ON T2.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T1.name IN (SELECT T1.name FROM operate_company AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id WHERE T2.Velocity > 100 INTERSECT SELECT name FROM operate_company WHERE Incorporated_in = 'China') GROUP BY T1.name, T3.name
result_summary: Fetch the count of flights grouped by operating company and airport for companies that are incorporated in China and have flights with a velocity greater than 100.

sql:
SELECT T1.name FROM airport AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.airport_id JOIN operate_company AS T3 ON T2.company_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Incorporated_in = 'China'
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.name FROM airport AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.airport_id JOIN operate_company AS T3 ON T2.company_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Type = 'Joint Venture'
result_summary: Fetch the names of airports that are served by operating companies that are both incorporated in China and are of the 'Joint Venture' type.

sql:
SELECT T1.name FROM airport AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.airport_id JOIN operate_company AS T3 ON T2.company_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Incorporated_in = 'China'
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.name FROM airport AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.airport_id JOIN operate_company AS T3 ON T2.company_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Type = 'Joint Venture'
result_summary: Fetch the names of airports served by operating companies that are both incorporated in China and are of the 'Joint Venture' type.

sql:
SELECT T1.name FROM airport AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.airport_id JOIN operate_company AS T3 ON T2.company_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Incorporated_in = 'China'
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.name FROM airport AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.airport_id JOIN operate_company AS T3 ON T2.company_id = T3.id WHERE T3.Type = 'Joint Venture'
result_summary: Fetch the names of airports served by flight companies that are both incorporated in China and are a 'Joint Venture' type.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T3.name, COUNT(T2.id) FROM operate_company AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id JOIN airport AS T3 ON T2.airport_id = T3.id WHERE T1.name IN (SELECT T1.name FROM operate_company AS T1 JOIN flight AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.company_id WHERE T2.Velocity > 100 INTERSECT SELECT name FROM operate_company WHERE Incorporated_in = 'China') GROUP BY T1.name, T3.name
result_summary: Fetch the number of flights grouped by operating company and airport, for companies that are both incorporated in China and operate flights with a velocity greater than 100.

sql: SELECT T1.name, COUNT(T2.paperID) FROM Inst AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.instID = T2.instID GROUP BY T1.instID
result_summary: Fetch the name of each institution and the total count of papers associated with it.

sql:
SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T2.authOrder = 1
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T2.authOrder > 1
result_summary: Fetch the first and last names of authors who have been listed as both the first author and a subsequent author on publications.

sql:
SELECT T2.fname, T2.lname
FROM (
  SELECT authID FROM Authorship WHERE authOrder = 1
  INTERSECT
  SELECT authID FROM Authorship WHERE authOrder = 2
) AS T1
JOIN Authors AS T2
  ON T1.authID = T2.authID;
result_summary: Fetch the first and last names of authors who have been listed as both the first and the second author on a publication.

sql:
SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T2.authOrder = 1
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T2.authOrder > 1
result_summary: Fetch the first and last names of authors who have been credited as both a first author and a non-first author on publications.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.lname, T3.name FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T2.instID = T3.instID WHERE T1.authID IN (SELECT authID FROM Authorship GROUP BY authID HAVING count(*) > 1)
result_summary: Fetch the unique last names of authors and the names of their institutions for authors who have authored more than one work.

sql:
SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T2.authOrder = 1
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.fname, T1.lname FROM Authors AS T1 JOIN Authorship AS T2 ON T1.authID = T2.authID WHERE T2.authOrder > 1
result_summary: Fetch the first and last names of authors who have been listed as both the first author and a non-first author on publications.

sql: SELECT T3.name, COUNT(T1.authID) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT instID, authID FROM Authorship WHERE authID IN (SELECT authID FROM Authorship GROUP BY authID HAVING COUNT(paperID) > 1)) AS T1 JOIN Inst AS T3 ON T1.instID = T3.instID GROUP BY T3.name
result_summary: Return a count of authors who have written more than one paper, grouped by their institution's name.

sql: SELECT T2.Birth_Place, COUNT(T1.Body_Builder_ID) FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID  =  T2.People_ID GROUP BY T2.Birth_Place
result_summary: Fetch a count of bodybuilders grouped by their birthplace.

sql:
SELECT T2.Name FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Total = 315.0
INTERSECT
SELECT T2.Name FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Clean_Jerk = 175.0
result_summary: Fetch the names of bodybuilders who have a total lift of 315.0 and a clean and jerk lift of 175.0.

sql:
SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Clean_Jerk = 175.0
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Snatch = 140.0
result_summary: Fetch the names of bodybuilders who can both clean and jerk 175.0 and snatch 140.0.

sql: SELECT T3.Birth_Place, AVG(T3.Snatch), AVG(T3.Clean_Jerk) FROM (SELECT T1.Snatch, T1.Clean_Jerk, T2.Birth_Place FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID) AS T3 GROUP BY T3.Birth_Place
result_summary: Fetch the average snatch and clean & jerk weights for bodybuilders, grouped and listed by their birthplace.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Total > (SELECT avg(Total) FROM body_builder)
result_summary: Fetch the names of bodybuilders whose total score is greater than the average total score of all bodybuilders.

sql:
SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Clean_Jerk = 175.0
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN body_builder AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Snatch = 140.0
result_summary: Find the names of bodybuilders with a clean and jerk of 175.0 and a snatch of 140.0.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM body_builder AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Total > (SELECT avg(Total) FROM body_builder)
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Fetch the names of bodybuilders whose total score is greater than the average total score.

sql: SELECT T1.Region_Name ,  SUM(T3.Damage_millions_USD) FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id  =  T2.Region_id JOIN storm AS T3 ON T2.Storm_ID  =  T3.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Region_Name
result_summary: Fetch the total storm damage in millions of USD, aggregated for each region name.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM affected_region AS T1 JOIN storm AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Storm_ID, T2.Name HAVING count(*) > 1
result_summary: Fetch the names of storms that have impacted more than one region.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Storm_ID HAVING count(T2.Region_ID) > (SELECT avg(count_regions) FROM (SELECT count(Region_ID) AS count_regions FROM affected_region GROUP BY Storm_ID) AS T3)
result_summary: Fetch the names of storms that have affected a number of regions greater than the average number of regions affected per storm.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM storm AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Storm_ID HAVING count(T2.Region_ID) > (SELECT avg(count_regions) FROM (SELECT count(Region_ID) AS count_regions FROM affected_region GROUP BY Storm_ID) AS T3)
result_summary: Fetch the names of storms that have affected a number of regions greater than the average across all storms.

sql:
SELECT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id JOIN storm AS T3 ON T2.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID WHERE T3.Damage_millions_USD > 10.0
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id JOIN storm AS T3 ON T2.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID WHERE T3.Max_speed = 972
result_summary: Fetch the names of regions that were affected by storms causing over 10 million USD in damage and also by storms with a maximum speed of 972.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM affected_region AS T1 JOIN storm AS T2 ON T1.Storm_ID = T2.Storm_ID GROUP BY T1.Storm_ID, T2.Name HAVING count(*) > 1
result_summary: Fetch the names of storms that have affected more than one region.

sql:
SELECT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id JOIN storm AS T3 ON T2.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID WHERE T3.Damage_millions_USD > 10.0
INTERSECT
SELECT T1.Region_name FROM region AS T1 JOIN affected_region AS T2 ON T1.Region_id = T2.Region_id JOIN storm AS T3 ON T2.Storm_ID = T3.Storm_ID WHERE T3.Max_speed = 972
result_summary: Fetch the names of regions affected by storms causing over 10 million USD in damage and also affected by storms with a maximum speed of 972.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name ,  COUNT(*) FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID  =  T2.Pilot_ID GROUP BY T1.Pilot_name
result_summary: This query retrieves the name of each pilot and the corresponding count of their pilot records.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T3.Manufacturer = 'Gillig' INTERSECT SELECT Pilot_name FROM pilot WHERE Position = 'Center Team'
result_summary: Fetch the names of pilots who are part of the 'Center Team' and have also flown an aircraft manufactured by 'Gillig'.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, T1.Age, T3.Record_Count FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Pilot_ID, COUNT(*) AS Record_Count FROM pilot_record GROUP BY Pilot_ID) AS T3 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T3.Pilot_ID WHERE T3.Record_Count > 1
result_summary: Fetch the name, age, and record count for all pilots who have more than one record.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T3.Manufacturer = 'Gillig' INTERSECT SELECT Pilot_name FROM pilot WHERE Position = 'Center Team'
result_summary: Find the names of pilots who are on the 'Center Team' and have also flown an aircraft manufactured by 'Gillig'.

sql:
SELECT T1.Pilot_name FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN pilot_record AS T2 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T2.Pilot_ID JOIN aircraft AS T3 ON T2.Aircraft_ID = T3.Aircraft_ID WHERE T3.Fuel_Propulsion = 'Diesel'
INTERSECT
SELECT Pilot_name FROM pilot WHERE Join_Year > 2000
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Fetch the names of pilots who joined after the year 2000 and have also flown a diesel-powered aircraft.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, T1.Age, T3.Record_Count FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Pilot_ID, COUNT(*) AS Record_Count FROM pilot_record GROUP BY Pilot_ID) AS T3 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T3.Pilot_ID WHERE T3.Record_Count > 1
result_summary: This query retrieves the name, age, and total number of records for all pilots who have more than one record.

sql: SELECT T1.Pilot_name, T1.Age, T3.Record_Count FROM pilot AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Pilot_ID, COUNT(*) AS Record_Count FROM pilot_record GROUP BY Pilot_ID) AS T3 ON T1.Pilot_ID = T3.Pilot_ID WHERE T3.Record_Count > 1
result_summary: Fetch the name, age, and total record count for pilots who have more than one record.

sql: SELECT T2.name ,  COUNT(T1.race_id) FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.track_id  =  T2.track_id GROUP BY T2.name
result_summary: Fetch the name of each track and the total number of races held there.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM track AS T1 JOIN race AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID GROUP BY T1.Track_ID, T1.Name HAVING count(*) > 1 UNION SELECT Name FROM track WHERE Year_Opened < 1997.0
result_summary: Fetch the names of tracks that have hosted more than one race or were opened before 1997.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID  =  T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Seating  >  (SELECT avg(T3.Seating) FROM track AS T3 JOIN race AS T4 ON T3.Track_ID  =  T4.Track_ID)
result_summary: Fetch the names of races held at tracks with a seating capacity greater than the average seating capacity of all tracks.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM race AS T1 JOIN track AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID  =  T2.Track_ID WHERE T2.Seating  >  (SELECT avg(T3.Seating) FROM track AS T3 JOIN race AS T4 ON T3.Track_ID  =  T4.Track_ID)
result_summary: Fetch the names of races held at tracks with a seating capacity greater than the average seating capacity of all tracks that host races.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM track AS T1 JOIN race AS T2 ON T1.Track_ID = T2.Track_ID GROUP BY T1.Track_ID, T1.Name HAVING count(*) > 1 UNION SELECT Name FROM track WHERE Year_Opened < 1997.0
result_summary: Fetch the names of tracks that have either hosted more than one race or were opened before the year 1997.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM track AS T1 EXCEPT SELECT T2.Name FROM track AS T2 JOIN race AS T3 ON T2.Track_ID = T3.Track_ID
result_summary: Fetch the names of all tracks that have not hosted any races.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM track AS T1 EXCEPT SELECT T2.Name FROM track AS T2 JOIN race AS T3 ON T2.Track_ID = T3.Track_ID
result_summary: Fetch the names of all tracks that have not hosted a race.

result_summary: Calculate the total number of citations for all publications that are associated with a domain keyword and also have a journal ID.

result_summary: Calculate the total number of citations for all publications that are associated with a domain keyword and have been published in a journal.

sql: SELECT SUM(T1.citation_num) FROM publication AS T1 WHERE T1.pid IN (SELECT T2.pid FROM publication_keyword AS T2 JOIN domain_keyword AS T3 ON T2.kid = T3.kid INTERSECT SELECT pid FROM publication WHERE jid IS NOT NULL)
result_summary: Fetch the total number of citations for publications that are both associated with a domain keyword and have been published in a journal.

sql:
SELECT T1.supplier_name, COUNT(T3.product_id) AS number_of_products, AVG(T3.product_price) AS average_product_price
FROM Suppliers AS T1
JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2
  ON T1.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id
JOIN Products AS T3
  ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id
GROUP BY
  T1.supplier_name;
result_summary: This query calculates the number of products and the average product price for each supplier.

sql:
SELECT T5.supplier_name FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id JOIN Suppliers AS T5 ON T4.supplier_id = T5.supplier_id WHERE T1.order_status_code = 'Completed'
INTERSECT
SELECT T5.supplier_name FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id JOIN Suppliers AS T5 ON T4.supplier_id = T5.supplier_id WHERE T1.order_status_code = 'New'
result_summary: Find the names of suppliers that have supplied products for both 'New' and 'Completed' customer orders.

sql:
SELECT T1.supplier_name, SUM(T2.total_value_purchased) AS total_value
FROM Suppliers AS T1
JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2
  ON T1.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id
WHERE T2.product_id IN (SELECT T4.product_id FROM Customer_Orders AS T3 JOIN Order_Items AS T4 ON T3.order_id = T4.order_id WHERE T3.order_status_code = 'Completed')
GROUP BY T1.supplier_name;
result_summary: This query returns each supplier's name and the total value of their products that were part of completed customer orders.

sql:
SELECT T5.supplier_name FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id JOIN Suppliers AS T5 ON T4.supplier_id = T5.supplier_id WHERE T1.order_status_code = 'Completed'
INTERSECT
SELECT T5.supplier_name FROM Customer_Orders AS T1 JOIN Order_Items AS T2 ON T1.order_id = T2.order_id JOIN Products AS T3 ON T2.product_id = T3.product_id JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T4 ON T3.product_id = T4.product_id JOIN Suppliers AS T5 ON T4.supplier_id = T5.supplier_id WHERE T1.order_status_code = 'New'
result_summary: Return the names of suppliers that have provided products for both 'Completed' and 'New' orders.

sql: SELECT T1.address_details FROM Addresses AS T1 JOIN Customer_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.address_id = T2.address_id JOIN Supplier_Addresses AS T3 ON T1.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T2.address_id IN (SELECT address_id FROM Supplier_Addresses)
result_summary: Fetch the address details for locations that are used by both customers and suppliers.

sql:
SELECT T1.supplier_name, SUM(T2.total_value_purchased) AS total_value
FROM Suppliers AS T1
JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2
  ON T1.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id
WHERE T2.product_id IN (SELECT T4.product_id FROM Customer_Orders AS T3 JOIN Order_Items AS T4 ON T3.order_id = T4.order_id WHERE T3.order_status_code = 'Completed')
GROUP BY T1.supplier_name;
result_summary: Fetch the total value of products purchased from each supplier, limited to products that have been part of completed customer orders.

sql:
SELECT T1.supplier_name, SUM(T2.total_value_purchased) AS total_value
FROM Suppliers AS T1
JOIN Product_Suppliers AS T2
  ON T1.supplier_id = T2.supplier_id
WHERE T2.product_id IN (SELECT T4.product_id FROM Customer_Orders AS T3 JOIN Order_Items AS T4 ON T3.order_id = T4.order_id WHERE T3.order_status_code = 'Completed')
GROUP BY T1.supplier_name;
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Calculate the total purchase value from each supplier, considering only products that were part of completed customer orders.

sql: SELECT Name, Price FROM phone WHERE Carrier = 'Sprint';
result_summary: Fetch the name and price of all phones available from the carrier 'Sprint'.

sql: SELECT District, Num_of_employees FROM market WHERE Ranking = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the district and number of employees for the market with the top ranking.

sql: SELECT Phone_ID, Memory_in_G FROM phone WHERE Memory_in_G = 32;
result_summary: Fetch the phone ID and memory in gigabytes for all phones that have 32 GB of memory.

sql: SELECT * FROM market WHERE Num_of_shops > 10.0;
result_summary: Fetch all data for markets that have more than 10 shops.

sql: SELECT Market_ID, Phone_ID, Num_of_stock FROM phone_market WHERE Num_of_stock = 2232;
result_summary: Fetch the Market ID, Phone ID, and stock count from phone markets where the number of stock is exactly 2232.

sql: SELECT T1.District, T1.Ranking FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID WHERE T2.Phone_ID = '1';
result_summary: Fetch the district and ranking of markets for the specific phone with an ID of '1'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Price, T3.District FROM phone AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID JOIN market AS T3 ON T2.Market_ID = T3.Market_ID WHERE T3.District = 'British Columbia';
result_summary: Fetch the name, price, and district for all phones sold in the British Columbia district.

sql: SELECT Carrier, AVG(Price) AS average_price FROM phone GROUP BY Carrier;
result_summary: Fetch the data containing each phone carrier and its corresponding average price.

sql: SELECT T1.District, COUNT(T2.Phone_ID) AS phone_model_count FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID GROUP BY T1.District;
result_summary: Fetch the data containing each district and its corresponding count of phone models.

sql: SELECT Carrier, COUNT(*) AS phone_count FROM phone WHERE Memory_in_G = 128 GROUP BY Carrier;
result_summary: Fetch the count of phones for each carrier, specifically for those with 128 GB of memory.

sql: SELECT T1.District, SUM(T2.Num_of_stock) AS total_stock FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID GROUP BY T1.District HAVING SUM(T2.Num_of_stock) > 3000;
result_summary: Fetch each district and its total phone stock, but only for districts where the total stock is greater than 3000.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Price FROM phone AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID JOIN market AS T3 ON T2.Market_ID = T3.Market_ID WHERE T3.Ranking < 5 AND T1.Carrier = 'Sprint';
result_summary: Fetch the name and price of phones with the carrier 'Sprint' that are available in markets with a ranking of less than 5.

sql: SELECT Name FROM phone WHERE Phone_ID NOT IN (SELECT Phone_ID FROM phone_market WHERE Market_ID = 1);
result_summary: Retrieve the names of all phones that are not available in the market with an ID of 1.

sql: WITH MarketStock AS (SELECT Market_ID, SUM(Num_of_stock) as total_stock FROM phone_market GROUP BY Market_ID) SELECT T1.District, T2.total_stock FROM market AS T1 JOIN MarketStock AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID ORDER BY T2.total_stock DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Fetch the district with the highest total phone stock.

sql: SELECT T1.District, T3.Name FROM market AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID JOIN phone AS T3 ON T2.Phone_ID = T3.Phone_ID WHERE T2.Num_of_stock = (SELECT MAX(Num_of_stock) FROM phone_market WHERE Market_ID = T1.Market_ID);
result_summary: Fetch the district of each market and the name of the phone that has the most stock within that market.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Num_of_employees) FROM market AS T1 WHERE T1.Market_ID IN (SELECT Market_ID FROM phone_market WHERE Phone_ID = (SELECT Phone_ID FROM phone WHERE Name = 'IPhone 6s'));
result_summary: Fetch the average number of employees for all markets that sell the 'IPhone 6s'.

sql: SELECT Name, Price FROM phone WHERE Price > (SELECT AVG(Price) FROM phone);
result_summary: Fetch the name and price of phones that have a price greater than the average phone price.

sql: SELECT Name, Carrier FROM phone WHERE Carrier = 'Sprint' UNION SELECT Name, Carrier FROM phone WHERE Memory_in_G = 128;
result_summary: Fetch the name and carrier of phones that either have 'Sprint' as their carrier or have 128 GB of memory.

sql: WITH PhoneRank AS (SELECT Name, Price, Carrier, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY Carrier ORDER BY Price DESC) as rank_num FROM phone) SELECT Name, Price, Carrier FROM PhoneRank WHERE rank_num = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name, price, and carrier of the most expensive phone offered by each distinct carrier.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Price, (SELECT T2.District FROM market AS T2 WHERE T2.Market_ID = T3.Market_ID) AS Market_District FROM phone AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T3 ON T1.Phone_ID = T3.Phone_ID WHERE T1.Carrier = 'TMobile';
result_summary: Fetch the name, price, and market district of all phones where the carrier is 'TMobile'.

sql: SELECT CASE WHEN Price >= 500 THEN 'Premium' WHEN Price < 500 AND Price >= 400 THEN 'Mid-Range' ELSE 'Budget' END AS Price_Category, count(*) FROM phone GROUP BY Price_Category;
result_summary: Fetch a count of all phones from the phone table, grouped into 'Premium', 'Mid-Range', or 'Budget' categories based on their price.

sql: SELECT T1.District, T3.Name FROM market AS T1 CROSS JOIN phone AS T3 LEFT JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Market_ID = T2.Market_ID AND T3.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID WHERE T2.Phone_ID IS NULL AND T1.District = 'New Brunswick';
result_summary: Fetch the district and phone names for all phones not sold in any market located in the 'New Brunswick' district.

sql: SELECT T1.carrier, AVG(T2.Num_of_stock) AS average_stock FROM phone AS T1 JOIN phone_market AS T2 ON T1.Phone_ID = T2.Phone_ID GROUP BY T1.carrier ORDER BY average_stock DESC;
result_summary: Fetch the average phone stock for each carrier, ordered from highest to lowest.

sql: SELECT * FROM cinema WHERE Location = 'County Wicklow';
result_summary: Fetch all available information for cinemas that are located in County Wicklow.

sql: SELECT Title, Original_air_date FROM film WHERE Directed_by = 'Bill Schreiner';
result_summary: Fetch the title and original air date for all films directed by Bill Schreiner.

sql: SELECT Cinema_ID, Film_ID FROM schedule WHERE Price = 12.99;
result_summary: Fetch the cinema and film IDs for all schedules that have a price of 12.99.

sql: SELECT Name, Capacity FROM cinema ORDER BY Capacity DESC;
result_summary: Fetch the data containing the name and capacity of all cinemas, ordered by capacity in descending order.

sql: SELECT Title, Production_code FROM film WHERE Rank_in_series < 28;
result_summary: Fetch the title and production code for films with a series rank of less than 28.

sql: SELECT T2.Title, T3.Price FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T3 ON T1.Cinema_ID  =  T3.Cinema_ID JOIN film AS T2 ON T3.Film_ID  =  T2.Film_ID WHERE T1.Name  =  'Codling';
result_summary: Fetch the title and price for all films being shown at the cinema named 'Codling'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Film_ID) AS NumberOfFilms FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T2.Cinema_ID GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each cinema and the total number of films scheduled for each one.

sql: SELECT AVG(Price) AS AveragePrice FROM schedule WHERE Date = '21 May';
result_summary: Calculate the average price of all events scheduled for May 21st.

sql: SELECT T1.Location, SUM(T2.Show_times_per_day) AS TotalShowtimes FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID  =  T2.Cinema_ID GROUP BY T1.Location;
result_summary: Fetch the total number of daily showtimes for each cinema location.

sql: SELECT T1.Title FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID  =  T2.Film_ID WHERE T2.Show_times_per_day  =  5;
result_summary: Fetch the titles of films that are shown exactly 5 times per day.

sql: SELECT Name, Location FROM cinema WHERE Openning_year BETWEEN 2010 AND 2012;
result_summary: Fetch the name and location of all cinemas that opened between the years 2010 and 2012.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T3.Name FROM film AS T1 INNER JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID INNER JOIN cinema AS T3 ON T2.Cinema_ID = T3.Cinema_ID WHERE T2.Date = '21 Jun';
result_summary: Fetch the titles of films and the names of the cinemas where they are being shown on the date '21 Jun'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Capacity FROM cinema AS T1 WHERE T1.Capacity > (SELECT AVG(Capacity) FROM cinema);
result_summary: Fetch the names and capacities of cinemas that have a capacity greater than the average capacity of all cinemas.

sql: SELECT T2.Title FROM schedule AS T1 JOIN film AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID  =  T2.Film_ID GROUP BY T1.Film_ID, T2.Title HAVING count(DISTINCT T1.Cinema_ID)  =  1;
result_summary: Fetch the titles of all films that are being shown in exactly one cinema.

sql: WITH CinemaRevenue AS (SELECT c.Name, SUM(s.Price * s.Show_times_per_day) AS DailyRevenue FROM cinema c JOIN schedule s ON c.Cinema_ID = s.Cinema_ID GROUP BY c.Name) SELECT Name, DailyRevenue FROM CinemaRevenue ORDER BY DailyRevenue DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: This query finds the name and daily revenue of the single top-earning cinema.

sql: SELECT T1.Title, T3.Name FROM film AS T1 LEFT JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID  =  T2.Film_ID LEFT JOIN cinema AS T3 ON T2.Cinema_ID  =  T3.Cinema_ID;
result_summary: Retrieve the title of each film and the name of the cinema where it is scheduled to be shown.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID  =  T2.Cinema_ID JOIN film AS T3 ON T2.Film_ID  =  T3.Film_ID WHERE T3.Title  =  'The Case of the Mystery Weekend' INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM cinema AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Cinema_ID  =  T2.Cinema_ID JOIN film AS T3 ON T2.Film_ID  =  T3.Film_ID WHERE T3.Title  =  'The Case of the Smart Dummy';
result_summary: Fetch the name of the cinema that is showing both the movie 'The Case of the Mystery Weekend' and 'The Case of the Smart Dummy'.

sql: SELECT T1.Directed_by, COUNT(T2.Cinema_ID) AS Screenings FROM film AS T1 JOIN schedule AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID  =  T2.Film_ID GROUP BY T1.Directed_by ORDER BY Screenings DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name of the director with the most film screenings and the total count of those screenings.

sql: SELECT Title, 'Film' AS Category FROM film UNION ALL SELECT Name, 'Cinema' AS Category FROM cinema;
result_summary: Fetch a combined list of all film titles and cinema names, each labeled with its respective category of 'Film' or 'Cinema'.

sql: SELECT T3.Location FROM cinema AS T3 JOIN schedule AS T4 ON T3.Cinema_ID  =  T4.Cinema_ID GROUP BY T3.Location HAVING AVG(T4.Price)  <  (SELECT AVG(Price) FROM schedule);
result_summary: Fetch the cinema locations where the average schedule price is less than the overall average schedule price across all cinemas.

sql: SELECT c.Name AS Cinema, f.Title AS Film, s.Show_times_per_day, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY c.Name ORDER BY s.Show_times_per_day DESC) AS RankOfShows FROM cinema AS c JOIN schedule AS s ON c.Cinema_ID = s.Cinema_ID JOIN film AS f ON s.Film_ID = f.Film_ID;
result_summary: This query retrieves the cinema name, film title, and daily showtimes, and ranks the films for each cinema from most to least frequently shown per day.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, T1.Date, T2.Title, SUM(T1.Price) OVER (PARTITION BY T3.Name ORDER BY T1.Date) AS CumulativePrice FROM schedule AS T1 JOIN film AS T2 ON T1.Film_ID = T2.Film_ID JOIN cinema AS T3 ON T1.Cinema_ID = T3.Cinema_ID;
result_summary: This query retrieves the cinema name, date, and film title, along with a cumulative sum of prices calculated for each cinema and ordered by date.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT f1.Title, f2.Title FROM schedule s1 JOIN schedule s2 ON s1.Cinema_ID = s2.Cinema_ID AND s1.Film_ID < s2.Film_ID JOIN film f1 ON s1.Film_ID = f1.Film_ID JOIN film f2 ON s2.Film_ID = f2.Film_ID WHERE s1.Cinema_ID = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the titles of all unique pairs of different films playing at the cinema with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT Name, College_Location FROM college WHERE Leader_Name = 'Ousame Tounkara';
result_summary: Fetch the name and college location for the college led by Ousame Tounkara.

sql: SELECT Name, Country FROM member WHERE College_ID = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name and country of members who belong to the college with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT Member_ID, Decoration_Theme FROM round WHERE Rank_in_Round = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the member ID and decoration theme for all members who ranked first in a round.

sql: SELECT Name FROM college;
result_summary: Fetch the names of all colleges.

sql: SELECT * FROM member WHERE Name = 'Jack Nicklaus';
result_summary: Fetch all available details for the member specifically named 'Jack Nicklaus'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name AS MemberName, T2.Name AS CollegeName FROM member AS T1 JOIN college AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID WHERE T2.College_Location = 'Ottawa';
result_summary: Fetch the name of each member and their corresponding college's name for all colleges located in Ottawa.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Decoration_Theme, T2.Rank_in_Round FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Jack Nicklaus';
result_summary: This query fetches the name, decoration theme, and rank in the round for a specific member named 'Jack Nicklaus'.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.Member_ID) AS NumberOfMembers FROM member AS T1 INNER JOIN college AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each college along with the total count of its members.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 LEFT JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Round_ID IS NULL;
result_summary: Fetch the names of all members who have not participated in any round.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Rank_in_Round) AS AverageRank FROM round AS T1 JOIN member AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.Country = 'United States';
result_summary: Calculate the average rank in a round for all members from the United States.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Country FROM member AS T1 JOIN college AS T2 ON T1.College_ID = T2.College_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'B.C. School';
result_summary: Fetch the name and country of members who attended the college named 'B.C. School'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name AS MemberName, T3.Name AS CollegeName, T3.Leader_Name FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID JOIN college AS T3 ON T1.College_ID = T3.College_ID WHERE T2.Decoration_Theme = 'Walk on the Moon' AND T2.Rank_in_Round = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name of the member, their college, and their college's leader for the person who ranked first in the round with the 'Walk on the Moon' theme.

sql: SELECT c.Name FROM college AS c WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM member AS m WHERE m.College_ID = c.College_ID AND m.Country = 'United States');
result_summary: Fetch the names of all colleges that have at least one member from the United States.

sql: WITH MemberRanks AS (SELECT Member_ID, Decoration_Theme, Rank_in_Round, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY Decoration_Theme ORDER BY Rank_in_Round ASC) as rn FROM round) SELECT T2.Name, T1.Decoration_Theme, T1.Rank_in_Round FROM MemberRanks AS T1 JOIN member AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T1.rn = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name, decoration theme, and rank of the top-ranked member for each distinct decoration theme.

sql: SELECT c.Name, c.College_Location FROM college AS c WHERE c.College_ID IN (SELECT m.College_ID FROM member AS m JOIN round AS r ON m.Member_ID = r.Member_ID GROUP BY m.College_ID HAVING MIN(r.Rank_in_Round) = 1);
result_summary: Fetch the name and location of colleges that have had at least one member achieve a first-place rank in any round.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 WHERE T1.College_ID = (SELECT College_ID FROM college WHERE Name = 'Saskatchewan School') INTERSECT SELECT T1.Name FROM member AS T1 WHERE T1.Country = 'United States';
result_summary: This query fetches the names of members who are from the United States and also attended Saskatchewan School.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Country FROM member AS T1 WHERE T1.Member_ID NOT IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM round WHERE Decoration_Theme = 'Soft Dream');
result_summary: Fetch the names and countries of members who have not participated in any round with the 'Soft Dream' decoration theme.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Name AS College FROM member AS T1 JOIN college AS T3 ON T1.College_ID = T3.College_ID WHERE T1.Member_ID IN (SELECT Member_ID FROM round WHERE Rank_in_Round <= 2);
result_summary: Get the name and college of members who have a rank of 2 or less in a round.

sql: SELECT T2.Decoration_Theme, T1.Name AS TopRankedMember FROM member AS T1 JOIN (SELECT Member_ID, Decoration_Theme, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY Decoration_Theme ORDER BY Rank_in_Round) as rnk FROM round) AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID WHERE T2.rnk = 1;
result_summary: Fetches the name of the top-ranked member for each decoration theme based on their rank in the round.

sql: SELECT c.Leader_Name FROM college AS c JOIN member AS m ON c.College_ID = m.College_ID JOIN round AS r ON m.Member_ID = r.Member_ID WHERE r.Decoration_Theme = 'Dark Nights' AND c.College_Location = 'St. Francis Xavier';
result_summary: Fetch the leader's name from the college at St. Francis Xavier for members who participated in a round with the 'Dark Nights' decoration theme.

sql: SELECT Name, Country FROM member EXCEPT SELECT T1.Name, T1.Country FROM member AS T1 JOIN round AS T2 ON T1.Member_ID = T2.Member_ID;
result_summary: Fetch the names and countries of all members who have not played in any rounds.

sql: WITH CollegeParticipation AS (SELECT c.College_ID, c.Name AS CollegeName, COUNT(DISTINCT r.Decoration_Theme) AS UniqueThemes FROM college c JOIN member m ON c.College_ID = m.College_ID JOIN round r ON m.Member_ID = r.Member_ID GROUP BY c.College_ID, c.Name) SELECT CollegeName, UniqueThemes FROM CollegeParticipation WHERE UniqueThemes = (SELECT MAX(UniqueThemes) FROM CollegeParticipation);
result_summary: This query identifies the college or colleges that have used the highest number of distinct decoration themes in rounds their members participated in.

sql: SELECT T1.Name AS MemberName, T3.Name AS CollegeName FROM member AS T1 JOIN college AS T3 ON T1.College_ID = T3.College_ID WHERE T1.Member_ID = (SELECT Member_ID FROM round WHERE Rank_in_Round = 2 AND Decoration_Theme = 'Soft Dream');
result_summary: Fetch the name and college of the member who achieved a rank of 2 in the round with the 'Soft Dream' decoration theme.

sql: SELECT Artist, Famous_Title, Famous_Release_date FROM artist WHERE Artist = 'Gorgoroth';
result_summary: Fetch the artist name, famous title, and famous release date for the specific artist 'Gorgoroth'.

sql: SELECT Song, Weeks_on_Top FROM volume WHERE Volume_Issue = '45:15';
result_summary: Fetch the song and its number of weeks on top for the specific volume issue '45:15'.

sql: SELECT Music_Festival, Category FROM music_festival WHERE Result = 'Awarded';
result_summary: Fetch the music festival and category for all results that were awarded.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Artist FROM artist WHERE Age > 30;
result_summary: Fetch the distinct names of artists whose age is greater than 30.

sql: SELECT Volume_Issue, Issue_Date FROM volume WHERE Weeks_on_Top = 3.0;
result_summary: Fetch the volume issue and issue date for volumes that were on top for exactly three weeks.

sql: SELECT T1.Song, T2.Artist FROM volume AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID  =  T2.Artist_ID;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each song and its corresponding artist by joining the volume and artist tables on their artist ID.

sql: SELECT T1.Category, T2.Song FROM music_festival AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Volume  =  T2.Volume_ID WHERE T1.Result  =  'Nominated';
result_summary: Fetch the category and song from music festivals for entries that have a result of 'Nominated'.

sql: SELECT T2.Artist, count(*) FROM volume AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID  =  T2.Artist_ID GROUP BY T2.Artist;
result_summary: This query fetches each artist and the total count of their associated volumes.

sql: SELECT T1.Music_Festival, T3.Artist FROM music_festival AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Volume  =  T2.Volume_ID JOIN artist AS T3 ON T2.Artist_ID  =  T3.Artist_ID WHERE T1.Result  =  'Awarded';
result_summary: Fetch the names of music festivals and their associated artists for all festivals that have resulted in an award.

sql: SELECT T2.Artist, T1.Song FROM volume AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID  =  T2.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Famous_Release_date  =  'November 2007';
result_summary: Fetch the artist and song titles for artists whose famous release date was in November 2007.

sql: SELECT avg(T1.Age) FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID  =  T2.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Weeks_on_Top > 2;
result_summary: Calculate the average age for artists who have had a musical volume on the top charts for more than two weeks.

sql: SELECT Artist, Famous_Title FROM artist WHERE Artist_ID IN (SELECT Artist_ID FROM volume WHERE Volume_ID IN (SELECT Volume FROM music_festival WHERE Result = 'Awarded'));
result_summary: Fetch the artist's name and their famous title for all artists who have been awarded at a music festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, T1.Age, T2.Song FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID  =  T2.Artist_ID WHERE T2.Volume_ID NOT IN (SELECT Volume FROM music_festival);
result_summary: Retrieve the name, age, and song of artists whose songs have not been featured in a music festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID  =  T2.Artist_ID GROUP BY T1.Artist HAVING count(*)  >  1;
result_summary: Fetch the names of all artists who have more than one volume.

sql: SELECT 'Artist' ,  Artist FROM artist UNION SELECT 'Song' ,  Song FROM volume;
result_summary: Fetch a consolidated list of all artists and songs, labeling each entry as either an 'Artist' or a 'Song'.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 EXCEPT SELECT T3.Artist FROM music_festival AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Volume  =  T2.Volume_ID JOIN artist AS T3 ON T2.Artist_ID  =  T3.Artist_ID WHERE T1.Result  =  'Awarded';
result_summary: Find all artists who have not won an award at a music festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist, T1.Age, T2.Age FROM artist AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.Artist  =  T2.Artist AND T1.Artist_ID != T2.Artist_ID;
result_summary: Fetch the artist name and the ages for all pairs of records that share the same artist name but have a different artist ID.

sql: SELECT T1.Artist FROM artist AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID  =  T2.Artist_ID JOIN music_festival AS T3 ON T2.Volume_ID  =  T3.Volume GROUP BY T1.Artist ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name of the single artist with the most music festival appearances.

sql: WITH AwardedArtists AS (SELECT T3.Artist_ID, T3.Artist FROM music_festival AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Volume  =  T2.Volume_ID JOIN artist AS T3 ON T2.Artist_ID  =  T3.Artist_ID WHERE T1.Result  =  'Awarded') SELECT Artist FROM AwardedArtists;
result_summary: Fetch the names of all artists who received an award at a music festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Music_Festival ,  T1.Category ,  T2.Song ,  T3.Artist FROM music_festival AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Volume  =  T2.Volume_ID JOIN artist AS T3 ON T2.Artist_ID  =  T3.Artist_ID ORDER BY T3.Age DESC;
result_summary: Fetch the music festival, category, song, and artist, ordered by the artist's age in descending order.

sql: SELECT T2.Artist,  T1.Song, T1.Weeks_on_Top FROM volume AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.Artist_ID = T2.Artist_ID WHERE T1.Weeks_on_Top > (SELECT avg(Weeks_on_Top) FROM volume);
result_summary: Fetch the artist, song, and weeks on top for songs that have remained on top for longer than the average number of weeks.

sql: SELECT Artist_ID, Artist, Famous_Title FROM artist INTERSECT SELECT T3.Artist_ID, T3.Artist, T3.Famous_Title FROM music_festival AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Volume = T2.Volume_ID JOIN artist AS T3 ON T2.Artist_ID = T3.Artist_ID WHERE T1.Category = 'Best Song';
result_summary: This query fetches the artist ID, name, and famous title for all artists who have participated in a music festival in the 'Best Song' category.

sql: SELECT T3.Artist, T1.Category, CASE WHEN T3.Age  >  30 THEN 'Veteran' ELSE 'Newcomer' END FROM music_festival AS T1 JOIN volume AS T2 ON T1.Volume  =  T2.Volume_ID JOIN artist AS T3 ON T2.Artist_ID  =  T3.Artist_ID;
result_summary: Fetch the artist's name, their music festival category, and a classification of 'Veteran' or 'Newcomer' based on whether the artist's age is greater than 30.

sql: SELECT Artist, Age, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY Age DESC) FROM artist;
result_summary: Fetch the name and age of all artists, along with a calculated rank based on their age in descending order.

sql: SELECT * FROM Customers WHERE Customer_ID = 214;
result_summary: Fetch the ID and name for the specific customer with a Customer_ID of 214.

sql: SELECT Service_name FROM Services WHERE Service_ID = 6;
result_summary: Fetch the name of the service from the services table where the service ID is 6.

sql: SELECT Policy_ID, Customer_Phone FROM Available_Policies WHERE policy_type_code = 'Property Insurance';
result_summary: Fetch the policy ID and customer phone number for all available policies with a policy type of 'Property Insurance'.

sql: SELECT * FROM Claims WHERE FNOL_ID = 532;
result_summary: Fetch the claim details associated with the specific First Notice of Loss (FNOL) ID of 532.

sql: SELECT Settlement_ID, Claim_ID FROM Settlements WHERE Settlement_Amount < 2000;
result_summary: Fetch the settlement ID and claim ID for all settlements where the settlement amount is less than 2000.

sql: SELECT C.Customer_name, CP.Policy_ID, CP.Date_Opened, CP.Date_Closed FROM Customers AS C JOIN Customers_Policies AS CP ON C.Customer_ID = CP.Customer_ID WHERE C.Customer_name = 'Ellsworth Paucek';
result_summary: Fetch the name, policy ID, and policy open and close dates for all policies belonging to the customer named 'Ellsworth Paucek'.

sql: SELECT S.Service_name, COUNT(F.FNOL_ID) AS Notification_Count FROM Services AS S JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS F ON S.Service_ID = F.Service_ID GROUP BY S.Service_name;
result_summary: Fetch the total number of 'First Notification of Loss' events grouped by each service name.

sql: SELECT C.Customer_name, AP.policy_type_code, AP.Customer_Phone FROM Customers AS C JOIN Customers_Policies AS CP ON C.Customer_ID = CP.Customer_ID JOIN Available_Policies AS AP ON CP.Policy_ID = AP.Policy_ID WHERE C.Customer_ID = 214;
result_summary: Fetch the name, policy type, and phone number for a specific customer with an ID of 214 by looking up their associated policies.

sql: SELECT C.Customer_name, CL.Claim_ID, CL.Effective_Date FROM Customers AS C JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS FNOL ON C.Customer_ID = FNOL.Customer_ID JOIN Claims AS CL ON FNOL.FNOL_ID = CL.FNOL_ID WHERE FNOL.FNOL_ID = 532;
result_summary: Fetch the customer's name, claim ID, and the claim's effective date for the specific First Notification of Loss record with an ID of 532.

sql: SELECT policy_type_code, COUNT(Policy_ID) as number_of_policies FROM Available_Policies GROUP BY policy_type_code;
result_summary: Retrieve the total number of policies grouped by each distinct policy type code.

sql: SELECT F.FNOL_ID, S.Service_name FROM First_Notification_of_Loss AS F JOIN Services AS S ON F.Service_ID = S.Service_ID WHERE S.Service_name = 'Change a policy';
result_summary: Fetch the first notification of loss ID and the service name for all records specifically related to the 'Change a policy' service.

sql: SELECT CL.Claim_ID, S.Settlement_ID, S.Effective_Date, S.Settlement_Amount FROM Claims AS CL JOIN Settlements AS S ON CL.Claim_ID = S.Claim_ID;
result_summary: Fetch the data containing the claim ID, settlement ID, effective date, and settlement amount by joining the claims and settlements tables on the claim ID.

sql: SELECT C.Customer_name, S.Service_name, AP.policy_type_code FROM Customers AS C JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS FNOL ON C.Customer_ID = FNOL.Customer_ID JOIN Services AS S ON FNOL.Service_ID = S.Service_ID JOIN Available_Policies AS AP ON FNOL.Policy_ID = AP.Policy_ID WHERE C.Customer_name = 'Ellsworth Paucek';
result_summary: Fetch the customer's name, the service's name, and the policy type code for a specific customer named 'Ellsworth Paucek'.

sql: WITH CustomerPolicyCounts AS (SELECT Customer_ID, COUNT(Policy_ID) AS PolicyCount FROM Customers_Policies GROUP BY Customer_ID) SELECT C.Customer_name, CPC.PolicyCount FROM Customers C JOIN CustomerPolicyCounts CPC ON C.Customer_ID = CPC.Customer_ID WHERE CPC.PolicyCount > 1;
result_summary: This query fetches the names and policy counts for all customers who have more than one policy.

sql: SELECT C.Customer_name, CP.Policy_ID, CP.Date_Opened, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY C.Customer_ID ORDER BY CP.Date_Opened) AS PolicyRank FROM Customers AS C JOIN Customers_Policies AS CP ON C.Customer_ID = CP.Customer_ID;
result_summary: Fetch customer names and their policy details, ranking each customer's policies chronologically by the date they were opened.

sql: SELECT AP.policy_type_code, AVG(S.Settlement_Amount) as Average_Settlement FROM Available_Policies AS AP JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss AS FNOL ON AP.Policy_ID = FNOL.Policy_ID JOIN Claims AS C ON FNOL.FNOL_ID = C.FNOL_ID JOIN Settlements AS S ON C.Claim_ID = S.Claim_ID GROUP BY AP.policy_type_code;
result_summary: Calculates the average claim settlement amount for each policy type by joining policy, loss, claim, and settlement data.

sql: SELECT Customer_name FROM Customers WHERE Customer_ID IN (SELECT Customer_ID FROM First_Notification_of_Loss WHERE Service_ID = (SELECT Service_ID FROM Services WHERE Service_name = 'Change a policy'));
result_summary: Fetches the names of customers who have submitted a first notification of loss for the 'Change a policy' service.

sql: SELECT C.Customer_name, COUNT(DISTINCT CL.Claim_ID) AS NumberOfClaims FROM Customers C LEFT JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss FNOL ON C.Customer_ID = FNOL.Customer_ID LEFT JOIN Claims CL ON FNOL.FNOL_ID = CL.FNOL_ID GROUP BY C.Customer_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT CL.Claim_ID) = 0;
result_summary: Fetch the name and claim count for all customers who have filed exactly zero claims.

sql: SELECT Policy_ID FROM Customers_Policies WHERE Customer_ID = 214 INTERSECT SELECT Policy_ID FROM First_Notification_of_Loss WHERE Customer_ID = 214;
result_summary: Find the common policy ID for customer 214 that appears in both the customer policies records and the first notification of loss records.

sql: SELECT C.Customer_name, COUNT(CASE WHEN AP.policy_type_code = 'Property Insurance' THEN 1 END) AS PropertyPolicies, COUNT(CASE WHEN AP.policy_type_code = 'Life Insurance' THEN 1 END) AS LifePolicies FROM Customers C JOIN Customers_Policies CP ON C.Customer_ID = CP.Customer_ID JOIN Available_Policies AP ON CP.Policy_ID = AP.Policy_ID GROUP BY C.Customer_name;
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Retrieve each customer's name along with the total count of their property insurance and life insurance policies.

sql: SELECT CP.Customer_ID, CP.Policy_ID, CP.Date_Opened, LAG(CP.Date_Opened, 1, '1900-01-01') OVER (PARTITION BY CP.Customer_ID ORDER BY CP.Date_Opened) AS PreviousPolicyDate FROM Customers_Policies CP;
result_summary: Fetch customer policy data, including the customer and policy IDs, the policy's opening date, and the opening date of the same customer's chronologically previous policy.

sql: SELECT C.Customer_name FROM Customers C WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Customers_Policies CP JOIN Available_Policies AP ON CP.Policy_ID = AP.Policy_ID WHERE CP.Customer_ID = C.Customer_ID AND AP.policy_type_code = 'Property Insurance');
result_summary: Fetch the names of all customers who have a 'Property Insurance' policy.

sql: SELECT Customer_ID FROM Customers_Policies EXCEPT SELECT Customer_ID FROM First_Notification_of_Loss;
result_summary: Retrieve the IDs of all customers who have a policy but have never filed a claim.

sql: SELECT Customer_name FROM Customers UNION ALL SELECT Service_name FROM Services;
result_summary: Fetch a single combined list containing the names of all customers and all services.

sql: SELECT C.Customer_name FROM Customers C JOIN First_Notification_of_Loss FNOL ON C.Customer_ID = FNOL.Customer_ID JOIN Claims CL ON FNOL.FNOL_ID = CL.FNOL_ID JOIN Settlements S ON CL.Claim_ID = S.Claim_ID WHERE S.Settlement_Amount = (SELECT MAX(Settlement_Amount) FROM Settlements);
result_summary: Find the name of the customer who received the highest settlement amount.

sql: SELECT g_name, rating FROM genre WHERE most_popular_in LIKE '%Sylhet%';
result_summary: Fetch the name and rating of genres that are most popular in any location containing 'Sylhet'.

sql: SELECT f_id, artist_name, file_size FROM files WHERE formats = 'mp4';
result_summary: Fetch the file ID, artist name, and file size for all files that are in 'mp4' format.

sql: SELECT T1.song_name, T1.languages, T1.resolution FROM song AS T1 JOIN artist AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name WHERE T2.gender = 'Male';
result_summary: Fetch the song name, languages, and resolution for all songs performed by male artists.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, T2.g_name FROM artist AS T1 JOIN genre AS T2 ON T1.preferred_genre = T2.g_name WHERE T1.country = 'Bangladesh';
result_summary: Fetch the name of each artist from Bangladesh and their corresponding genre name.

sql: SELECT artist_name, COUNT(song_name) AS NumberOfSongs FROM song WHERE country = 'Bangladesh' GROUP BY artist_name;
result_summary: Fetch the total number of songs for each artist from Bangladesh.

sql: SELECT song_name, releasedate FROM song WHERE artist_name IN (SELECT artist_name FROM artist WHERE preferred_genre = 'tagore');
result_summary: Fetch the name and release date for all songs by artists whose preferred genre is 'tagore'.

sql: SELECT T1.song_name, T1.genre_is, T1.rating FROM song AS T1 WHERE T1.rating = (SELECT MAX(T2.rating) FROM song AS T2 WHERE T2.genre_is = T1.genre_is);
result_summary: Fetch the name, genre, and rating of songs that have the maximum rating within their respective genre.

sql: WITH ArtistSongRank AS (SELECT artist_name, song_name, releasedate, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY artist_name ORDER BY releasedate ASC) as rn FROM song) SELECT artist_name, song_name, releasedate FROM ArtistSongRank WHERE rn = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the artist name, song name, and release date of the earliest song released by each artist.

sql: SELECT T1.country, AVG(T2.rating) as average_rating FROM artist AS T1 JOIN song AS T2 ON T1.artist_name = T2.artist_name JOIN files AS T3 ON T2.f_id = T3.f_id WHERE T3.formats = 'mp4' GROUP BY T1.country;
result_summary: Fetch the average song rating for each country, calculated only from songs that have an 'mp4' file format.

sql: SELECT T1.artist_name, T2.rating AS genre_rating FROM artist AS T1 JOIN genre AS T2 ON T1.preferred_genre = T2.g_name WHERE T1.artist_name IN (SELECT artist_name FROM artist WHERE country = 'India' UNION SELECT artist_name FROM song WHERE resolution = 320);
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Fetch the artist's name and the rating of their preferred genre for all artists who are either from India or have a song with a resolution of 320.

sql: SELECT course_id, course_name, course_description FROM Courses WHERE course_name = 'statistics';
result_summary: Fetch the course ID, name, and description for the course specifically named 'statistics'.

sql: SELECT first_name, middle_name, last_name, email_address FROM People WHERE last_name = 'Hartmann';
result_summary: Fetch the first name, middle name, last name, and email address for all people with the last name 'Hartmann'.

sql: SELECT line_1, city, state_province_county, country FROM Addresses WHERE city = 'Linnealand';
result_summary: Fetch the address line 1, city, state/province/county, and country for all addresses located in the city of Linnealand.

sql: SELECT candidate_id, candidate_details FROM Candidates WHERE candidate_details = 'Jane';
result_summary: Fetch the candidate ID and details for the specific candidate whose details are 'Jane'.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS passed_assessments FROM Candidate_Assessments WHERE asessment_outcome_code = 'Pass';
result_summary: Fetch the total count of candidate assessments where the assessment outcome was a 'Pass'.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.student_details FROM People AS T1 JOIN Students AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.student_details = 'Martin';
result_summary: Fetch the first name, last name, and student details from people who are students, specifically where the student detail is 'Martin'.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T3.course_name, T2.registration_date FROM People AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id WHERE T1.person_id = 121;
result_summary: Fetch the first name, last name, course names, and registration dates for all courses registered by the student with person ID 121.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T3.line_1, T3.city, T3.country FROM People AS T1 JOIN People_Addresses AS T2 ON T1.person_id = T2.person_id JOIN Addresses AS T3 ON T2.address_id = T3.address_id WHERE T1.first_name = 'Shannon' AND T1.last_name = 'Senger';
result_summary: Fetch the first name, last name, and address details for a specific person named Shannon Senger.

sql: SELECT T1.course_name, T2.date_of_attendance FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Attendance AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id WHERE T2.student_id = 111 AND T1.course_name = 'statistics';
result_summary: Retrieve the course name and date of attendance for the student with ID 111 specifically for the 'statistics' course.

sql: SELECT T1.candidate_details, T2.qualification, T2.assessment_date FROM Candidates AS T1 JOIN Candidate_Assessments AS T2 ON T1.candidate_id = T2.candidate_id WHERE T2.asessment_outcome_code = 'Fail';
result_summary: Fetch the details, qualification, and assessment date for candidates who have failed their assessment.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name, COUNT(T1.course_id) AS number_of_registered_courses FROM Student_Course_Registrations AS T1 JOIN People AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.person_id GROUP BY T1.student_id, T2.first_name, T2.last_name ORDER BY number_of_registered_courses DESC;
result_summary: Retrieve each student's first and last name and the number of courses they are registered for, sorted in descending order by the course count.

sql: SELECT T1.student_id, T1.student_details, T2.date_of_attendance FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Attendance AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.course_id = '303';
result_summary: Fetch the student ID, student details, and date of attendance for all students who attended the course with an ID of '303'.

sql: WITH StudentAttendanceCount AS (SELECT student_id, COUNT(course_id) AS attendance_count FROM Student_Course_Attendance GROUP BY student_id) SELECT p.first_name, p.last_name, a.city, COALESCE(sac.attendance_count, 0) AS total_attendance FROM People AS p JOIN People_Addresses AS pa ON p.person_id = pa.person_id JOIN Addresses AS a ON pa.address_id = a.address_id LEFT JOIN StudentAttendanceCount AS sac ON p.person_id = sac.student_id WHERE pa.date_to > '2018-03-01' AND p.person_id IN (SELECT student_id FROM Students);
result_summary: Fetch the name, city, and total course attendance for students with a valid address after March 1, 2018.

sql: SELECT p.first_name, p.last_name, scr.registration_date, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY scr.registration_date ASC) as registration_rank FROM People AS p JOIN Student_Course_Registrations AS scr ON p.person_id = scr.student_id JOIN Courses AS c ON scr.course_id = c.course_id WHERE c.course_name = 'statistics';
result_summary: Fetch the full name and registration date for students in the 'statistics' course, ranked by their registration date from earliest to latest.

sql: SELECT c.course_name, p.first_name, p.last_name FROM Student_Course_Registrations AS scr_outer JOIN People AS p ON scr_outer.student_id = p.person_id JOIN Courses AS c ON scr_outer.course_id = c.course_id WHERE scr_outer.registration_date = (SELECT MAX(scr_inner.registration_date) FROM Student_Course_Registrations AS scr_inner WHERE scr_inner.course_id = scr_outer.course_id);
result_summary: This query fetches the name of each course along with the first and last name of the student who most recently registered for it.

sql: SELECT c.course_name, COUNT(scr.student_id) AS student_count FROM Student_Course_Registrations AS scr JOIN Courses AS c ON scr.course_id = c.course_id WHERE scr.registration_date >= '2008-11-01' AND scr.registration_date < '2008-12-01' GROUP BY c.course_name HAVING COUNT(scr.student_id) >= 1;
result_summary: Fetch each course's name and the total count of students who registered for it during November 2008, for courses with at least one registration.

sql: SELECT p.person_id, p.first_name, p.last_name, (SELECT MAX(pa.date_from) FROM People_Addresses pa WHERE pa.person_id = p.person_id) AS most_recent_move_in_date FROM People AS p WHERE p.person_id = 111;
result_summary: Fetch the ID, first name, last name, and the most recent move-in date for the person with a specific ID of 111.

sql: WITH StudentRegistrationCount AS (SELECT student_id, COUNT(course_id) as num_courses FROM Student_Course_Registrations GROUP BY student_id) SELECT p.first_name, p.last_name, src.num_courses FROM StudentRegistrationCount src JOIN People p ON src.student_id = p.person_id WHERE src.num_courses > (SELECT AVG(num_courses) FROM StudentRegistrationCount);
result_summary: Fetch the first name, last name, and number of registered courses for students who are enrolled in more courses than the average student.

sql: SELECT T1.person_id FROM People AS T1 WHERE T1.person_id IN (SELECT student_id FROM Students) INTERSECT SELECT T2.candidate_id FROM Candidate_Assessments AS T2 WHERE T2.asessment_outcome_code = 'Pass';
result_summary: This query retrieves the person IDs for all students who have also passed their candidate assessment.

sql: SELECT qualification, COUNT(CASE WHEN asessment_outcome_code = 'Pass' THEN 1 END) AS pass_count, COUNT(CASE WHEN asessment_outcome_code = 'Fail' THEN 1 END) AS fail_count, COUNT(*) as total_assessments FROM Candidate_Assessments GROUP BY qualification ORDER BY qualification;
result_summary: This query retrieves the count of passed, failed, and total assessments for each qualification, ordered by the qualification name.

sql: WITH StatisticsStudents AS (SELECT scr.student_id FROM Student_Course_Registrations scr WHERE scr.course_id = '301'), PassedCandidates AS (SELECT ca.candidate_id FROM Candidate_Assessments ca WHERE ca.asessment_outcome_code = 'Pass') SELECT p.first_name, p.last_name, a.city, a.state_province_county FROM People p JOIN StatisticsStudents ss ON p.person_id = ss.student_id JOIN PassedCandidates pc ON p.person_id = pc.candidate_id JOIN People_Addresses pa ON p.person_id = pa.person_id JOIN Addresses a ON pa.address_id = a.address_id WHERE pa.date_to > '2018-01-01';
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Fetch the name and address of people who are students registered for the statistics course and have passed an assessment, using only address information that is valid after January 1st, 2018.

sql: SELECT company_id, company_name, company_address FROM Third_Party_Companies WHERE company_type = 'Maintenance Contractor';
result_summary: Fetch the company ID, name, and address for all third-party companies identified as 'Maintenance Contractor'.

sql: SELECT part_id, part_name, chargeable_amount FROM Parts WHERE chargeable_yn = '1';
result_summary: Fetch the ID, name, and chargeable amount for all parts that are chargeable.

sql: SELECT skill_code, skill_description FROM Skills WHERE skill_code = 'ELEC';
result_summary: Fetch the skill code and skill description from the Skills table for the specific skill with the code 'ELEC'.

sql: SELECT staff_id, staff_name FROM Staff WHERE other_staff_details = 'Manager';
result_summary: Fetch the ID and name of staff who are identified as managers.

sql: SELECT part_fault_id, fault_description FROM Part_Faults WHERE fault_short_name = 'PW';
result_summary: Fetch the part fault ID and fault description from the part faults table for all faults with the short name 'PW'.

sql: SELECT T1.asset_id, T1.asset_details, T2.company_name AS supplier_name FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T2 ON T1.supplier_company_id = T2.company_id WHERE T2.company_name = 'McDermott Group';
result_summary: Fetch the ID, details, and supplier name for all assets provided by the supplier 'McDermott Group'.

sql: SELECT T1.asset_details, T1.asset_acquired_date, T2.contract_end_date FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Maintenance_Contracts AS T2 ON T1.maintenance_contract_id = T2.maintenance_contract_id WHERE T1.asset_id = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the details, acquired date, and maintenance contract end date for a specific asset with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T2.part_name FROM Asset_Parts AS T1 JOIN Parts AS T2 ON T1.part_id = T2.part_id WHERE T1.asset_id = 3;
result_summary: Retrieve the names of all parts belonging to the asset with an ID of 3.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.company_id WHERE T2.company_name = 'Schuppe-Carroll';
result_summary: Fetch the first and last names of maintenance engineers who work for the third-party company named 'Schuppe-Carroll'.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name, T3.skill_description FROM Engineer_Skills AS T1 JOIN Maintenance_Engineers AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id JOIN Skills AS T3 ON T1.skill_id = T3.skill_id WHERE T3.skill_description = 'Electrical';
result_summary: Fetch the first name, last name, and skill description for all maintenance engineers who have the 'Electrical' skill.

sql: SELECT T1.fault_log_entry_datetime, T1.fault_description, T2.staff_name FROM Fault_Log AS T1 JOIN Staff AS T2 ON T1.recorded_by_staff_id = T2.staff_id WHERE T2.staff_id = 14;
result_summary: Fetch the datetime, description, and recording staff member's name for all fault log entries filed by the staff member with an ID of 14.

sql: SELECT T1.asset_details, T1.asset_make, T1.asset_model FROM Assets AS T1 WHERE T1.asset_id IN (SELECT T2.asset_id FROM Fault_Log AS T2 WHERE T2.fault_description = 'system error');
result_summary: Fetch the details, make, and model for all assets that have a logged fault description of 'system error'.

sql: WITH CompanyEngineerCounts AS (SELECT T2.company_name, COUNT(T1.engineer_id) AS number_of_engineers FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.company_id GROUP BY T2.company_name) SELECT company_name, number_of_engineers FROM CompanyEngineerCounts ORDER BY number_of_engineers DESC;
result_summary: Fetch a list of third-party companies and their total number of maintenance engineers, ordered from the company with the most engineers to the least.

sql: SELECT SUM(T4.chargeable_amount) AS total_chargeable_value FROM Third_Party_Companies AS T1 JOIN Assets AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.supplier_company_id JOIN Asset_Parts AS T3 ON T2.asset_id = T3.asset_id JOIN Parts AS T4 ON T3.part_id = T4.part_id WHERE T1.company_name = 'McDermott Group' AND T4.chargeable_yn = '1';
result_summary: Fetch the total chargeable value of all parts supplied by the 'McDermott Group' that are marked as chargeable.

sql: SELECT a.asset_id, a.asset_details, (SELECT MAX(fl.fault_log_entry_datetime) FROM Fault_Log fl WHERE fl.asset_id = a.asset_id) AS last_fault_date FROM Assets a WHERE a.asset_id = 3;
result_summary: Fetch the details and the most recent fault date for the specific asset with an ID of 3.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name, T2.company_name, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY T2.company_name ORDER BY T1.engineer_id) AS rank_in_company FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.company_id;
result_summary: This query retrieves the first name, last name, and company name of maintenance engineers, assigning a rank to each engineer within their company based on their engineer ID.

sql: SELECT T4.first_name, T4.last_name, T3.visit_start_datetime FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Fault_Log AS T2 ON T1.asset_id = T2.asset_id JOIN Engineer_Visits AS T3 ON T2.fault_log_entry_id = T3.fault_log_entry_id JOIN Maintenance_Engineers AS T4 ON T3.engineer_id = T4.engineer_id WHERE T1.asset_details = 'dell laptop3' AND T2.fault_description = 'system error';
result_summary: Fetch the first name, last name, and visit start datetime of maintenance engineers who attended to a 'system error' fault on a 'dell laptop3' asset.

sql: SELECT T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Engineer_Skills AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id JOIN Skills AS T3 ON T2.skill_id = T3.skill_id WHERE T3.skill_description IN ('Electrical', 'Mechanical') GROUP BY T1.engineer_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT T3.skill_id) = 2;
result_summary: Fetch the first and last names of maintenance engineers who possess both 'Electrical' and 'Mechanical' skills.

sql: SELECT T2.skill_description, COUNT(T1.part_fault_id) AS times_required FROM Skills_Required_To_Fix AS T1 JOIN Skills AS T2 ON T1.skill_id = T2.skill_id GROUP BY T2.skill_description ORDER BY times_required DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Fetch the description and count for the single most frequently required skill needed to fix a fault.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.staff_name FROM Staff AS T1 JOIN Fault_Log AS T2 ON T1.staff_id = T2.recorded_by_staff_id JOIN Fault_Log_Parts AS T3 ON T2.fault_log_entry_id = T3.fault_log_entry_id JOIN Part_Faults AS T4 ON T3.part_fault_id = T4.part_fault_id WHERE T4.fault_short_name = 'TW';
result_summary: Fetch the unique names of staff who have recorded a part fault with the short name 'TW'.

sql: SELECT T1.asset_id, T1.asset_details, T1.asset_acquired_date FROM Assets AS T1 LEFT JOIN Fault_Log AS T2 ON T1.asset_id = T2.asset_id WHERE T2.fault_log_entry_id IS NULL;
result_summary: Fetch the asset ID, details, and acquired date for all assets that have never had a fault recorded in the fault log.

sql: WITH CompanyEngineers AS (SELECT T1.engineer_id FROM Maintenance_Engineers AS T1 JOIN Third_Party_Companies AS T2 ON T1.company_id = T2.company_id WHERE T2.company_name = 'Langworth-Funk') SELECT T5.first_name, T5.last_name, T1.asset_details, T3.visit_start_datetime FROM Assets AS T1 JOIN Fault_Log AS T2 ON T1.asset_id = T2.asset_id JOIN Engineer_Visits AS T3 ON T2.fault_log_entry_id = T3.fault_log_entry_id JOIN CompanyEngineers AS T4 ON T3.engineer_id = T4.engineer_id JOIN Maintenance_Engineers AS T5 ON T4.engineer_id = T5.engineer_id;
result_summary: This query retrieves the full name of an engineer, the details of a faulty asset, and the visit's start time for all maintenance visits conducted by engineers from the 'Langworth-Funk' company.

sql: SELECT staff_name AS name, 'Staff' AS role FROM Staff WHERE gender = '1' UNION ALL SELECT first_name AS name, 'Engineer' AS role FROM Maintenance_Engineers WHERE company_id = 2;
result_summary: Retrieve a combined list of names and roles for all staff with gender '1' and all maintenance engineers from company ID 2.

sql: SELECT T2.first_name, T2.last_name, COUNT(CASE WHEN T1.fault_status = 'Waiting' THEN 1 END) AS waiting_visits, COUNT(CASE WHEN T1.fault_status = 'Return' THEN 1 END) AS return_visits FROM Engineer_Visits AS T1 JOIN Maintenance_Engineers AS T2 ON T1.engineer_id = T2.engineer_id WHERE T1.engineer_id = 15 GROUP BY T2.first_name, T2.last_name;
result_summary: Retrieve the first name and last name for the maintenance engineer with ID 15, along with the total number of their visits that have a 'Waiting' and 'Return' fault status.

sql: SELECT product, pages_per_minute_color, max_page_size FROM product WHERE interface = 'USB 2.0';
result_summary: Fetch the product name, color pages-per-minute, and maximum page size for all products that have a USB 2.0 interface.

sql: SELECT Store_Name, Type, Ranking FROM store ORDER BY Ranking ASC;
result_summary: This query retrieves the name, type, and ranking of all stores, ordered by their ranking in ascending order.

sql: SELECT District_name, Headquartered_City FROM district WHERE City_Population > 100000;
result_summary: Fetch the district name and its headquartered city for districts where the city's population is greater than 100,000.

sql: SELECT count(*) AS total_products FROM product;
result_summary: Fetch the total count of all products.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT Type FROM store;
result_summary: Fetch a distinct list of all store types.

sql: SELECT p.product, p.dimensions FROM product AS p JOIN store_product AS sp ON p.product_id = sp.product_id JOIN store AS s ON sp.store_id = s.store_id WHERE s.Store_Name = 'Miramichi';
result_summary: Fetch the product name and dimensions for all products available at the 'Miramichi' store.

sql: SELECT s.Store_Name FROM store AS s JOIN store_district AS sd ON s.Store_ID = sd.Store_ID WHERE sd.District_ID = 11;
result_summary: This query fetches the name of the store that belongs to the district with an ID of 11.

sql: SELECT s.Store_Name, COUNT(sp.Product_ID) AS number_of_products FROM store AS s JOIN store_product AS sp ON s.Store_ID = sp.Store_ID GROUP BY s.Store_Name ORDER BY number_of_products DESC;
result_summary: This query retrieves each store's name and its total product count, sorted in descending order by the number of products.

sql: SELECT s.Store_Name, d.District_name, d.Headquartered_City FROM store AS s JOIN store_district AS sd ON s.Store_ID = sd.Store_ID JOIN district AS d ON sd.District_ID = d.District_ID WHERE s.Ranking = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name of the top-ranked store, along with its district name and the district's headquartered city.

sql: SELECT s.Type, AVG(s.Area_size) as average_area FROM store AS s WHERE s.Type = 'Village Store' GROUP BY s.Type;
result_summary: Calculate the average area size for all stores that are of the 'Village Store' type.

sql: SELECT d.District_name, COUNT(sd.Store_ID) AS number_of_stores FROM district AS d JOIN store_district AS sd ON d.District_ID = sd.District_ID GROUP BY d.District_name;
