result_summary: Fetch the name of each district along with the corresponding count of stores located within it.

sql: SELECT p.product FROM product AS p JOIN store_product AS sp ON p.product_id = sp.product_id JOIN store AS s ON sp.store_id = s.store_id WHERE s.Type = 'City Mall';
result_summary: This query retrieves the names of all products available in stores that are of the 'City Mall' type.

sql: SELECT product, dpi, pages_per_minute_color FROM product WHERE product_id NOT IN (SELECT Product_ID FROM store_product WHERE Store_ID = 1);
result_summary: Fetch the name, DPI, and color pages per minute for all products that are not sold at the store with an ID of 1.

sql: WITH StoreRankingByDistrict AS (SELECT s.Store_Name, d.District_name, s.Ranking, RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY d.District_name ORDER BY s.Ranking ASC, s.Area_size DESC) as district_rank FROM store s JOIN store_district sd ON s.Store_ID = sd.Store_ID JOIN district d ON sd.District_ID = d.District_ID) SELECT Store_Name, District_name, district_rank FROM StoreRankingByDistrict;
result_summary: This query ranks stores within each district based on their original ranking and area size, returning the store name, district name, and the calculated rank.

sql: SELECT d.District_name, d.City_Population FROM district d WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM store_district sd JOIN store s ON sd.Store_ID = s.Store_ID WHERE sd.District_ID = d.District_ID AND s.Type = 'City Mall');
result_summary: Fetch the name and city population for districts that contain at least one store of the 'City Mall' type.

sql: SELECT p.product FROM product p WHERE p.product_id IN (SELECT sp.Product_ID FROM store_product sp WHERE sp.Store_ID = 1 INTERSECT SELECT sp.Product_ID FROM store_product sp WHERE sp.Store_ID = 2);
result_summary: Fetch the names of products that are available in both store ID 1 and store ID 2.

sql: SELECT T.Type, AVG(T.Ranking) AS avg_ranking, SUM(T.product_count) AS total_products_in_type FROM (SELECT s.Store_ID, s.Type, s.Ranking, COUNT(sp.Product_ID) AS product_count FROM store s LEFT JOIN store_product sp ON s.Store_ID = sp.Store_ID GROUP BY s.Store_ID, s.Type, s.Ranking) AS T GROUP BY T.Type;
result_summary: Fetch the type, average ranking, and total number of products for each store type.

sql: SELECT d.District_name, SUM(CASE WHEN s.Ranking = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS top_tier_stores, SUM(CASE WHEN s.Ranking = 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS mid_tier_stores, SUM(CASE WHEN s.Ranking > 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS other_stores FROM store s JOIN store_district sd ON s.Store_ID = sd.Store_ID JOIN district d ON sd.District_ID = d.District_ID GROUP BY d.District_name;
result_summary: This query retrieves the count of stores for each district, broken down into top-tier, mid-tier, and other categories based on their ranking.

sql: SELECT District_name, Headquartered_City FROM district d WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM store_district sd WHERE sd.District_ID = d.District_ID);
result_summary: Fetch the names of districts and their headquarter cities for all districts that do not have an associated store.

sql: SELECT product, pages_per_minute_color, LEAD(product, 1, 'N/A') OVER (ORDER BY pages_per_minute_color ASC) as next_fastest_product FROM product WHERE max_page_size = 'A4' AND dpi = 600;
result_summary: Fetch the product name, its color pages-per-minute, and the name of the next fastest product for all products that support A4 paper and have a 600 DPI resolution, ordered by speed.

sql: SELECT s.Store_Name, 'High Ranking Store' as category FROM store s WHERE s.Ranking = 1 UNION ALL SELECT d.District_name, 'Large Population City' as category FROM district d WHERE d.City_Population > 500000;
result_summary: Fetches a combined list of top-ranked stores and districts from cities with a population over 500,000, assigning a descriptive category to each.

sql: WITH TargetDistrict AS (SELECT District_ID FROM store_district WHERE Store_ID = 1) SELECT s.Store_Name, s.Type, s.Area_size FROM store s JOIN store_district sd ON s.Store_ID = sd.Store_ID WHERE sd.District_ID = (SELECT District_ID FROM TargetDistrict) AND s.Store_ID != 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name, type, and area size of other stores that are located in the same district as a specific target store.

sql: WITH HighDPIProducts AS (SELECT product_id, product FROM product WHERE dpi = 600.0), RogersvilleStore AS (SELECT Store_ID FROM store WHERE Store_Name = 'Rogersville') SELECT hdp.product FROM HighDPIProducts hdp JOIN store_product sp ON hdp.product_id = sp.product_id WHERE sp.Store_ID IN (SELECT Store_ID FROM RogersvilleStore);
result_summary: Fetch the names of all products that have a DPI of 600 and are sold in the 'Rogersville' store.

sql: SELECT Type, AVG(Area_size) as avg_area FROM store GROUP BY Type HAVING AVG(Area_size) > (SELECT AVG(Area_size) FROM store);
result_summary: Retrieve the store type and its average area size for types that have an average area greater than the overall average of all stores.

sql: SELECT s.Store_Name, p.product FROM store s CROSS JOIN product p WHERE s.Store_Name = 'Rogersville' AND p.product_id NOT IN (SELECT sp.Product_ID FROM store_product sp WHERE sp.Store_ID = s.Store_ID);
result_summary: Fetch the name of the 'Rogersville' store and the names of all products that are not sold in that specific store.

sql: SELECT owner_id, first_name, last_name, street, zip_code FROM Owners WHERE city = 'Lake Tia';
result_summary: Retrieve the owner ID, first name, last name, street, and zip code for all owners residing in the city of Lake Tia.

sql: SELECT dog_id, name, age, gender FROM Dogs WHERE breed_code = 'BUL';
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Fetch the ID, name, age, and gender for all dogs with the breed code 'BUL'.

sql: SELECT professional_id, first_name, last_name, email_address FROM Professionals WHERE role_code = 'Employee';
result_summary: Fetch the professional ID, first name, last name, and email address for all professionals whose role is 'Employee'.

sql: SELECT charge_id, charge_amount FROM Charges WHERE charge_type = 'Daily Accommodation';
result_summary: Fetch the charge ID and charge amount for all charges that are for 'Daily Accommodation'.

sql: SELECT treatment_type_code, treatment_type_description FROM Treatment_Types WHERE treatment_type_code = 'VAC';
result_summary: Fetch the treatment type code and description for the specific treatment type corresponding to 'Vaccination'.

sql: SELECT d.name AS dog_name, o.first_name, o.last_name FROM Dogs AS d JOIN Owners AS o ON d.owner_id = o.owner_id WHERE o.owner_id = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name of a dog and the first and last name of its owner for a specific owner with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT t.date_of_treatment, tt.treatment_type_description, t.cost_of_treatment FROM Treatments AS t JOIN Treatment_Types AS tt ON t.treatment_type_code = tt.treatment_type_code WHERE t.dog_id = 14;
result_summary: Fetch the date, description, and cost for all treatments administered to the dog with an ID of 14.

sql: SELECT d.name AS dog_name, s.size_description FROM Dogs AS d JOIN Sizes AS s ON d.size_code = s.size_code WHERE d.size_code = 'LGE';
result_summary: Fetch the names and size descriptions for all dogs classified as large.

sql: SELECT p.first_name, p.last_name, SUM(t.cost_of_treatment) AS total_treatment_cost FROM Professionals AS p JOIN Treatments AS t ON p.professional_id = t.professional_id GROUP BY p.professional_id, p.first_name, p.last_name;
result_summary: Fetch the first and last name for each professional, along with the total cost of treatments they have administered.

sql: SELECT d.name, b.breed_name FROM Dogs AS d JOIN Breeds AS b ON d.breed_code = b.breed_code WHERE b.breed_name = 'Husky';
result_summary: Fetch the names of all dogs that belong to the 'Husky' breed.

sql: SELECT o.first_name, o.last_name, o.email_address FROM Owners AS o JOIN Dogs AS d ON o.owner_id = d.owner_id WHERE d.abandoned_yn = '1' AND o.owner_id = 3;
result_summary: Fetch the first name, last name, and email address for the specific owner with ID 3 who has abandoned their dog.

sql: SELECT b.breed_name, COUNT(d.dog_id) AS number_of_dogs FROM Breeds AS b LEFT JOIN Dogs AS d ON b.breed_code = d.breed_code GROUP BY b.breed_name ORDER BY number_of_dogs DESC;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each dog breed and the total number of dogs for that breed, ordered by the number of dogs in descending order.

sql: SELECT o.first_name, o.last_name, d.name as dog_name, p.first_name as professional_first_name FROM Owners AS o JOIN Dogs AS d ON o.owner_id = d.owner_id JOIN Treatments AS t ON d.dog_id = t.dog_id JOIN Professionals AS p ON t.professional_id = p.professional_id WHERE d.breed_code = 'ESK' AND t.treatment_type_code = 'WALK';
result_summary: This query fetches the first and last name of owners, the name of their dog, and the first name of the professional for all dogs of the 'ESK' breed that have received a 'WALK' treatment.

sql: SELECT d.name, d.age FROM Dogs d WHERE d.dog_id IN (SELECT t.dog_id FROM Treatments t GROUP BY t.dog_id HAVING COUNT(t.treatment_id) > 1);
result_summary: Fetch the name and age of dogs that have received more than one treatment.

sql: WITH BreedCosts AS (SELECT d.breed_code, AVG(t.cost_of_treatment) AS avg_cost FROM Dogs d JOIN Treatments t ON d.dog_id = t.dog_id GROUP BY d.breed_code) SELECT b.breed_name, bc.avg_cost FROM BreedCosts bc JOIN Breeds b ON bc.breed_code = b.breed_code ORDER BY bc.avg_cost DESC;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each dog breed and its corresponding average cost of treatment, ordered from the highest average cost to the lowest.

sql: SELECT d.name AS dog_name, tt.treatment_type_description, t.cost_of_treatment, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY d.dog_id ORDER BY t.cost_of_treatment DESC) AS cost_rank FROM Dogs d JOIN Treatments t ON d.dog_id = t.dog_id JOIN Treatment_Types tt ON t.treatment_type_code = tt.treatment_type_code WHERE d.dog_id = 14;
result_summary: Fetches all treatments for a specific dog, ranking them by cost in descending order.

sql: SELECT p.first_name, p.last_name FROM Professionals p WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Treatments t JOIN Dogs d ON t.dog_id = d.dog_id JOIN Breeds b ON d.breed_code = b.breed_code WHERE t.professional_id = p.professional_id AND b.breed_name = 'Husky');
result_summary: Fetch the first and last names of all professionals who have ever administered a treatment to a Husky.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name, 'Owner' AS contact_type, city, state FROM Owners WHERE state = 'Wisconsin' UNION ALL SELECT first_name, last_name, 'Professional' AS contact_type, city, state FROM Professionals WHERE state = 'Wisconsin';
result_summary: Fetch a combined list of contacts from Wisconsin, including their first name, last name, and city, categorized by their type as either 'Owner' or 'Professional'.

sql: SELECT b.breed_name, TotalCost FROM Breeds b JOIN (SELECT d.breed_code, SUM(t.cost_of_treatment) AS TotalCost FROM Dogs d JOIN Treatments t ON d.dog_id = t.dog_id GROUP BY d.breed_code) AS BreedTotalCosts ON b.breed_code = BreedTotalCosts.breed_code ORDER BY TotalCost DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Fetches the name and total cost for the single dog breed with the highest cumulative cost of treatments.

sql: SELECT d1.name AS dog1_name, d2.name AS dog2_name, o.first_name AS owner_first_name FROM Dogs d1 JOIN Dogs d2 ON d1.owner_id = d2.owner_id AND d1.dog_id < d2.dog_id JOIN Owners o ON d1.owner_id = o.owner_id;
result_summary: Fetch the names for every unique pair of dogs that belong to the same owner, along with that owner's first name.

sql: SELECT owner_id, first_name, last_name FROM Owners WHERE owner_id NOT IN (SELECT d.owner_id FROM Dogs d WHERE d.breed_code = 'ESK');
result_summary: Fetch the ID, first name, and last name of every owner who does not own a dog with the breed code 'ESK'.

sql: SELECT CASE WHEN weight < 2 THEN 'Under 2kg' WHEN weight >= 2 AND weight < 8 THEN '2-8kg' ELSE 'Over 8kg' END AS weight_category, COUNT(*) AS dog_count FROM Dogs GROUP BY weight_category;
result_summary: Fetch a count of dogs grouped by calculated weight categories ('Under 2kg', '2-8kg', 'Over 8kg').

sql: SELECT p.first_name, p.last_name FROM Professionals p WHERE p.professional_id NOT IN (SELECT t.professional_id FROM Treatments t WHERE t.treatment_type_code = 'VAC');
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Retrieve the first and last names of all professionals who have not performed a treatment of type 'VAC'.

sql: SELECT o.first_name, o.last_name, SUM(t.cost_of_treatment) as total_spent FROM Owners o JOIN Dogs d ON o.owner_id = d.owner_id JOIN Treatments t ON d.dog_id = t.dog_id GROUP BY o.owner_id, o.first_name, o.last_name ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Find the name and total treatment cost for the single dog owner who has spent the most money.

sql: SELECT ID, name, Time FROM swimmer WHERE Nationality = 'Poland';
result_summary: Retrieve the ID, name, and recorded time for all swimmers with the nationality of 'Poland'.

sql: SELECT name, City, Capacity FROM stadium WHERE Opening_year = 2005;
result_summary: Fetch the name, city, and capacity of all stadiums that opened in the year 2005.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.Year, T2.name AS StadiumName FROM event AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.Stadium_ID = T2.ID WHERE T1.Name = 'FINA';
result_summary: Retrieve the name, year, and stadium name for the event named 'FINA'.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T2.Nationality, T1.Result FROM record AS T1 JOIN swimmer AS T2 ON T1.Swimmer_ID = T2.ID WHERE T1.Result = 'Loss';
result_summary: Fetch the name and nationality of swimmers for all records where the result was a loss.

sql: SELECT Country, COUNT(*) AS NumberOfStadiums FROM stadium GROUP BY Country;
result_summary: Fetch the count of stadiums for each country.

sql: SELECT T1.name AS SwimmerName, T3.Name AS EventName, T4.name AS StadiumName FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.ID JOIN stadium AS T4 ON T3.Stadium_ID = T4.ID WHERE T4.Country = 'Russia';
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Retrieve the names of swimmers, their corresponding events, and the stadium names for all events held in Russia.

sql: SELECT name, Nationality FROM swimmer WHERE ID NOT IN (SELECT Swimmer_ID FROM record WHERE Result = 'Win');
result_summary: Retrieve the name and nationality for swimmers who have no recorded wins.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID WHERE T2.Event_ID IN (SELECT ID FROM event WHERE Stadium_ID IN (SELECT ID FROM stadium WHERE Capacity > 70000));
result_summary: Fetch the unique names of swimmers who competed in an event held at a stadium with a capacity greater than 70,000.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.ID WHERE T3.Name = 'FINA' EXCEPT SELECT T1.name FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.ID WHERE T3.Name = 'Pacific';
result_summary: Fetch the names of swimmers who have participated in a 'FINA' event but not in a 'Pacific' event.

sql: SELECT T1.Nationality, MIN(T3.Year) AS FirstParticipationYear FROM swimmer AS T1 JOIN record AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.Swimmer_ID JOIN event AS T3 ON T2.Event_ID = T3.ID WHERE T1.Nationality = 'Australia' GROUP BY T1.Nationality;
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
This query retrieves the earliest participation year for swimmers from Australia.

sql: SELECT Name, Nationality, Graduation_College FROM people WHERE Nationality = 'United States';
result_summary: Fetch the name, nationality, and graduation college for all people from the United States.

sql: SELECT Name, Headquarters, Industry FROM company WHERE Industry = 'Banking';
result_summary: Fetch the name, headquarters, and industry for all companies that are in the banking industry.

sql: SELECT * FROM employment WHERE Year_working = 2;
result_summary: Fetch all employment data for individuals who have been working for exactly 2 years.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM people WHERE Graduation_College = 'Iowa';
result_summary: Fetch the name and age of people who graduated from the college 'Iowa'.

sql: SELECT Name, Sales_in_Billion, Profits_in_Billion FROM company WHERE Headquarters = 'USA' AND Sales_in_Billion > 150;
result_summary: Fetch the name, sales, and profits for all companies headquartered in the USA with sales greater than 150 billion.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Year_working FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID WHERE T2.Year_working > 2;
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Fetch the name and years of employment for people who have worked for more than 2 years.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Industry FROM company AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID WHERE T2.People_ID = 2;
result_summary: Fetch the name and industry of the company that employs the person with an ID of 2.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN company AS T3 ON T2.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Tom Sheehey';
result_summary: Fetch the person's name and their company's name for the employee named 'Tom Sheehey'.

sql: SELECT T2.Name, COUNT(T1.People_ID) AS NumberOfEmployees FROM employment AS T1 JOIN company AS T2 ON T1.Company_ID = T2.Company_ID GROUP BY T2.Name;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each company and the total count of its employees.

sql: SELECT T3.Industry, AVG(T1.Age) AS AverageEmployeeAge FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN company AS T3 ON T2.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID GROUP BY T3.Industry;
result_summary: Fetch the average age of employees for each company industry.

sql: SELECT Name, Age FROM people WHERE People_ID IN (SELECT People_ID FROM employment WHERE Company_ID = 13);
result_summary: Fetch the name and age for all people who are employed by the company with an ID of 13.

sql: WITH CompanyRank AS (SELECT Name, Industry, Profits_in_Billion, RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY Industry ORDER BY Profits_in_Billion DESC) as RankNum FROM company) SELECT Name, Industry, Profits_in_Billion FROM CompanyRank WHERE RankNum = 1;
result_summary: This query fetches the name, industry, and profits for the single most profitable company within each industry category.

sql: SELECT T1.Nationality FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN company AS T3 ON T2.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID GROUP BY T1.Nationality ORDER BY AVG(T3.Market_Value_in_Billion) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Fetch the single nationality corresponding to the highest average market value of the companies where its people are employed.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, (SELECT T2.Name FROM people AS T2 JOIN employment AS T3 ON T2.People_ID = T3.People_ID WHERE T3.Company_ID = T1.Company_ID ORDER BY T3.Year_working DESC LIMIT 1) AS LongestTenuredEmployee FROM company AS T1;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each company and the name of its employee with the longest tenure.

sql: SELECT Name FROM company AS T1 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM employment AS T2 JOIN people AS T3 ON T2.People_ID = T3.People_ID WHERE T2.Company_ID = T1.Company_ID AND T3.Nationality = 'United Kindom');
result_summary: Fetch the names of all companies that do not have any employees from the United Kingdom.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 WHERE T1.Graduation_College = 'Virginia' UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN company AS T3 ON T2.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID WHERE T3.Industry = 'Banking';
result_summary: Fetch the names of people who either graduated from Virginia college or work in the banking industry.

sql: SELECT Name, Industry FROM company WHERE Headquarters = 'USA' EXCEPT SELECT Name, Industry FROM company WHERE Industry = 'Oil and gas';
result_summary: Fetch the name and industry of all companies headquartered in the USA, excluding any from the 'Oil and gas' industry.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Name, T1.Graduation_College FROM people AS T1 JOIN people AS T2 ON T1.Graduation_College = T2.Graduation_College AND T1.People_ID < T2.People_ID WHERE T1.Graduation_College = 'Northeastern';
result_summary: Fetch all unique pairs of people's names who both graduated from Northeastern, along with their shared college name.

sql: WITH IndustryAssets AS (SELECT Industry, SUM(Assets_in_Billion) AS TotalAssets FROM company GROUP BY Industry), IndustryTenure AS (SELECT c.Industry, AVG(e.Year_working) AS AvgTenure FROM company c JOIN employment e ON c.Company_ID = e.Company_ID GROUP BY c.Industry) SELECT ia.Industry, ia.TotalAssets, it.AvgTenure FROM IndustryAssets ia JOIN IndustryTenure it ON ia.Industry = it.Industry WHERE ia.TotalAssets > 700 AND it.AvgTenure > 1;
result_summary: Retrieve the industry, total assets, and average employee tenure for industries with more than 700 billion in assets and an average tenure greater than one year.

sql: SELECT CASE WHEN T3.Market_Value_in_Billion > 400 THEN 'High Value' WHEN T3.Market_Value_in_Billion > 200 THEN 'Mid Value' ELSE 'Low Value' END AS Market_Category, COUNT(T1.People_ID) AS Employee_Count FROM people AS T1 JOIN employment AS T2 ON T1.People_ID = T2.People_ID JOIN company AS T3 ON T2.Company_ID = T3.Company_ID GROUP BY Market_Category;
result_summary: Retrieve the total count of employees grouped by their company's market value category, which is defined as 'High', 'Mid', or 'Low Value'.

sql: SELECT * FROM Channels WHERE Other_Details = '145';
result_summary: Fetch all information for the channel where the 'Other_Details' value is '145'.

sql: SELECT * FROM Finances WHERE Other_Details = 'Mutual';
result_summary: Fetch all data for finances where the 'Other_Details' are specified as 'Mutual'.

sql: SELECT E.Event_Type_Code, A.address_details FROM Events AS E JOIN Addresses AS A ON E.Address_ID = A.Address_ID WHERE E.Event_ID = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the event type code and address details for a single, specific event with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT A.Other_Details, AIE.Event_ID FROM Assets AS A JOIN Assets_in_Events AS AIE ON A.Asset_ID = AIE.Asset_ID WHERE A.Other_Details = 'Transportation Cars';
result_summary: Retrieve the event IDs and other details for all assets that are specifically described as 'Transportation Cars'.

sql: SELECT P.Product_Name, P.Product_Price FROM Products_in_Events AS PIE JOIN Products AS P ON PIE.Product_ID = P.Product_ID WHERE PIE.Event_ID = 8;
result_summary: This query retrieves the names and prices of all products associated with event ID 8.

sql: SELECT P_org.Party_Details AS Organizer, E.Event_ID, P_part.Party_Details AS Participant FROM Events AS E JOIN Parties_in_Events AS PIE_org ON E.Event_ID = PIE_org.Event_ID JOIN Parties AS P_org ON PIE_org.Party_ID = P_org.Party_ID JOIN Parties_in_Events AS PIE_part ON E.Event_ID = PIE_part.Event_ID JOIN Parties AS P_part ON PIE_part.Party_ID = P_part.Party_ID WHERE P_org.Party_Details = 'European Free Alliance' AND PIE_org.Role_Code = 'Organizer' AND PIE_part.Role_Code = 'Participant' AND P_org.Party_ID <> P_part.Party_ID;
result_summary: Fetch the event ID and the names of the participants for all events organized by the 'European Free Alliance'.

sql: SELECT L.Other_Details AS Location_Name, COUNT(A.Document_ID) AS Document_Count FROM Locations AS L JOIN Events AS E ON L.Location_ID = E.Location_ID JOIN Agreements AS A ON E.Event_ID = A.Event_ID WHERE E.Event_Type_Code = 'Trade Show' GROUP BY L.Other_Details;
result_summary: Fetch the total count of documents for each location where a 'Trade Show' event was held.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT P.Product_Name, P.Product_Price FROM Products AS P JOIN Products_in_Events AS PIE ON P.Product_ID = PIE.Product_ID JOIN Events AS E ON PIE.Event_ID = E.Event_ID JOIN Parties_in_Events AS PaIE ON E.Event_ID = PaIE.Event_ID JOIN Parties AS Pa ON PaIE.Party_ID = Pa.Party_ID WHERE Pa.Party_Details = 'European People''s Party' AND PaIE.Role_Code = 'Participant' ORDER BY P.Product_Price DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Find the names and prices of the top three most expensive products associated with events where the 'European People's Party' was a participant, ordered by price in descending order.

sql: WITH Bad_Finance_Events AS (SELECT E.Event_ID FROM Events AS E JOIN Finances AS F ON E.Finance_ID = F.Finance_ID WHERE F.Other_Details = 'Bad') SELECT A.Other_Details AS Asset_Details, BFE.Event_ID FROM Bad_Finance_Events AS BFE JOIN Assets_in_Events AS AIE ON BFE.Event_ID = AIE.Event_ID JOIN Assets AS A ON AIE.Asset_ID = A.Asset_ID;
result_summary: Fetch the asset details and event IDs for all assets associated with events that have bad financial records.

sql: SELECT E.Event_ID, E.Event_Type_Code FROM Events AS E WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Products_in_Events AS PIE JOIN Products AS P ON PIE.Product_ID = P.Product_ID WHERE PIE.Event_ID = E.Event_ID AND P.Product_Type_Code = 'Books');
result_summary: Fetch the event ID and event type code for all events that do not contain any products of the type 'Books'.

sql: SELECT * FROM city WHERE Status = 'Village';
result_summary: Fetch all available information for cities that are classified as villages.

sql: SELECT Year, Total_Horses, Working_Horses FROM farm WHERE Year = 1927;
result_summary: Fetch the year, total number of horses, and number of working horses from farm records for the year 1927.

sql: SELECT Theme, Hosts FROM farm_competition WHERE Year = 2006;
result_summary: Fetch the theme and hosts for the farm competition that took place in the year 2006.

sql: SELECT Competition_ID, Farm_ID FROM competition_record WHERE Rank = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the competition and farm IDs for all competition records that have a rank of 1.

sql: SELECT Official_Name, Population FROM city WHERE Official_Name = 'Grand Falls/Grand-Sault';
result_summary: Fetch the official name and population for the city specifically named 'Grand Falls/Grand-Sault'.

sql: SELECT c.Official_Name, c.Population FROM city AS c JOIN farm_competition AS fc ON c.City_ID = fc.Host_city_ID WHERE fc.Theme = 'Carnival M is back!';
result_summary: Fetch the official name and population of cities that hosted a farm competition with the theme 'Carnival M is back!'.

sql: SELECT fc.Theme, cr.Rank FROM farm_competition AS fc JOIN competition_record AS cr ON fc.Competition_ID = cr.Competition_ID WHERE cr.Farm_ID = 2;
result_summary: This query retrieves the theme and rank of competitions for the farm with an ID of 2.

sql: SELECT f.Year, f.Total_Cattle, f.Oxen FROM farm AS f JOIN competition_record AS cr ON f.Farm_ID = cr.Farm_ID WHERE cr.Rank = 3;
result_summary: This query retrieves the year, total number of cattle, and number of oxen for farms that achieved a rank of 3 in a competition.

sql: SELECT Status, COUNT(*) AS number_of_cities FROM city GROUP BY Status;
result_summary: Fetch the total count of cities, grouped by their respective status.

sql: SELECT AVG(Pigs) AS average_pigs, MAX(Sheep_and_Goats) AS max_sheep_and_goats FROM farm;
result_summary: Fetch a single record containing the average number of pigs and the maximum number of sheep and goats from the farm table.

sql: SELECT c.Official_Name, count(fc.Competition_ID) AS competitions_hosted FROM city AS c LEFT JOIN farm_competition AS fc ON c.City_ID = fc.Host_city_ID GROUP BY c.Official_Name;
result_summary: Fetch the official name of each city along with the total count of competitions it has hosted.

sql: SELECT cr.Farm_ID, cr.Rank FROM competition_record AS cr JOIN farm_competition AS fc ON cr.Competition_ID = fc.Competition_ID WHERE fc.Hosts LIKE '%Alicia Keys%';
result_summary: Fetch the farm ID and rank from competition records for any competition hosted by someone whose name contains 'Alicia Keys'.

sql: SELECT c.Official_Name, fc.Year, f.Year, f.Total_Horses FROM city AS c JOIN farm_competition AS fc ON c.City_ID = fc.Host_city_ID JOIN competition_record AS cr ON fc.Competition_ID = cr.Competition_ID JOIN farm AS f ON cr.Farm_ID = f.Farm_ID WHERE cr.Rank = 1 AND c.Official_Name = 'Grand Falls/Grand-Sault';
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
This query fetches the official name, competition year, farm year, and total horses for the winning farm (rank 1) in a competition hosted by the city of 'Grand Falls/Grand-Sault'.

sql: SELECT Farm_ID, Year, Total_Cattle FROM farm WHERE Farm_ID IN (SELECT Farm_ID FROM competition_record WHERE Competition_ID = (SELECT Competition_ID FROM farm_competition WHERE Theme = 'MTV Asia Aid'));
result_summary: Fetch the farm ID, year, and total number of cattle for all farms that participated in the farm competition with the theme 'MTV Asia Aid'.

sql: SELECT c.Official_Name, (SELECT MAX(fc.Year) FROM farm_competition AS fc WHERE fc.Host_city_ID = c.City_ID) AS latest_competition_year FROM city AS c WHERE c.City_ID IN (1, 2, 3);
result_summary: Fetch the official name of cities with IDs 1, 2, and 3, along with the latest year a farm competition was hosted in each city.

sql: WITH FarmPerformance AS (SELECT cr.Competition_ID, cr.Farm_ID, f.Total_Cattle, RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY cr.Competition_ID ORDER BY f.Total_Cattle DESC) as cattle_rank FROM competition_record AS cr JOIN farm AS f ON cr.Farm_ID = f.Farm_ID) SELECT fp.Competition_ID, fp.Farm_ID, fp.cattle_rank FROM FarmPerformance AS fp WHERE fp.cattle_rank = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the competition ID and the farm ID for the top-ranked farm in each competition, where the ranking is based on having the most cattle.

sql: SELECT fc1.Theme AS Competition1, fc2.Theme AS Competition2, c.Official_Name AS HostCity FROM farm_competition AS fc1 JOIN farm_competition AS fc2 ON fc1.Host_city_ID = fc2.Host_city_ID AND fc1.Competition_ID < fc2.Competition_ID JOIN city AS c ON fc1.Host_city_ID = c.City_ID;
result_summary: Fetch pairs of farm competition themes that were held in the same host city, along with the name of that city.

sql: SELECT c.Official_Name, COUNT(fc.Competition_ID) as competition_count FROM city AS c JOIN farm_competition AS fc ON c.City_ID = fc.Host_city_ID GROUP BY c.Official_Name HAVING COUNT(fc.Competition_ID) >= 1;
result_summary: Fetch the official name of each city and a count of the competitions it has hosted, limited to cities that have hosted one or more competitions.

sql: SELECT Farm_ID, 'Won a competition' AS Achievement FROM competition_record WHERE Rank = 1 UNION SELECT Farm_ID, 'High cattle count' AS Achievement FROM farm WHERE Total_Cattle > 8500;
result_summary: Fetch the farm ID and a corresponding achievement for farms that either ranked first in a competition or have more than 8500 cattle.

sql: SELECT Farm_ID FROM competition_record WHERE Competition_ID = 1 INTERSECT SELECT Farm_ID FROM farm WHERE Pigs > 4400;
result_summary: Fetch the ID of any farm that participated in competition 1 and also has more than 4400 pigs.

sql: SELECT fc.Theme, AVG(c.Population) AS avg_host_city_population, AVG(cr.Rank) AS avg_farm_rank FROM farm_competition AS fc JOIN city AS c ON fc.Host_city_ID = c.City_ID JOIN competition_record AS cr ON fc.Competition_ID = cr.Competition_ID GROUP BY fc.Theme;
result_summary: This query retrieves the average host city population and average farm rank, grouped by each farm competition theme.

sql: SELECT fc.Theme, fc.Year, (SELECT f.Total_Horses FROM farm AS f JOIN competition_record AS cr ON f.Farm_ID = cr.Farm_ID WHERE cr.Competition_ID = fc.Competition_ID AND cr.Rank = 1) AS winning_farm_horses FROM farm_competition AS fc WHERE fc.Competition_ID = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the theme, year, and the total number of horses from the winning farm for the competition with an ID of 1.

sql: WITH PerthAndoverCompetitions AS (SELECT fc.Competition_ID FROM farm_competition AS fc JOIN city AS c ON fc.Host_city_ID = c.City_ID WHERE c.Official_Name = 'Perth-Andover') SELECT pac.Competition_ID, cr.Farm_ID, cr.Rank, f.Pigs FROM PerthAndoverCompetitions AS pac JOIN competition_record AS cr ON pac.Competition_ID = cr.Competition_ID JOIN farm AS f ON cr.Farm_ID = f.Farm_ID;
result_summary: Fetch the competition ID, farm ID, rank, and number of pigs for each participating farm in competitions held in the city of Perth-Andover.

sql: SELECT cr.Farm_ID, f.Year, f.Sheep_and_Goats, cr.Rank, DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY cr.Competition_ID ORDER BY f.Sheep_and_Goats DESC) AS livestock_rank FROM competition_record AS cr JOIN farm AS f ON cr.Farm_ID = f.Farm_ID WHERE cr.Competition_ID = 1;
result_summary: This query retrieves farm records for competition ID 1, including the year and sheep/goat count, and then ranks each farm based on its total number of livestock in descending order.

sql: SELECT Code, Name, Founder FROM Manufacturers WHERE Headquarter = 'Tokyo';
result_summary: Fetch the code, name, and founder of manufacturers headquartered in Tokyo.

sql: SELECT Name, Price FROM Products WHERE Price < 200;
result_summary: This query retrieves the name and price for all products that cost less than 200.

sql: SELECT p.Name AS ProductName, p.Price, m.Name AS ManufacturerName FROM Products AS p JOIN Manufacturers AS m ON p.Manufacturer = m.Code;
result_summary: Retrieve the name and price of all products, along with the name of their corresponding manufacturer.

sql: SELECT m.Name, COUNT(p.Code) AS NumberOfProducts FROM Manufacturers AS m LEFT JOIN Products AS p ON m.Code = p.Manufacturer GROUP BY m.Name;
result_summary: Fetch each manufacturer's name along with the total count of products they produce.

sql: SELECT Headquarter, SUM(Revenue) AS TotalRevenue FROM Manufacturers GROUP BY Headquarter HAVING SUM(Revenue) > 50.0;
result_summary: Fetch the total revenue for each manufacturer's headquarters, including only those with a total revenue greater than 50.

sql: SELECT Name, Price FROM Products WHERE Price > (SELECT AVG(Price) FROM Products);
result_summary: Fetch the name and price of products whose price is above the average product price.

sql: SELECT m.Name, m.Headquarter FROM Manufacturers AS m WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Products AS p WHERE p.Manufacturer = m.Code AND p.Price > 150);
result_summary: Fetch the name and headquarter of manufacturers that have at least one product with a price greater than 150.

sql: WITH ProductCounts AS (SELECT Manufacturer, COUNT(*) as ProductCount FROM Products GROUP BY Manufacturer) SELECT m.Name, pc.ProductCount FROM Manufacturers m JOIN ProductCounts pc ON m.Code = pc.Manufacturer WHERE pc.ProductCount > 0;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each manufacturer and the count of their products, for manufacturers with at least one product.

sql: SELECT p.Name, p.Price, m.Name FROM Products p JOIN Manufacturers m ON p.Manufacturer = m.Code WHERE m.Revenue = (SELECT MIN(Revenue) FROM Manufacturers);
result_summary: Fetch the name and price of products, along with their manufacturer's name, for all products made by the manufacturer with the lowest revenue.

sql: SELECT Name, Revenue FROM Manufacturers WHERE Headquarter = 'Los Angeles' UNION SELECT Name, Revenue FROM Manufacturers WHERE Founder = 'Andy';
result_summary: Fetch the name and revenue of manufacturers who are headquartered in Los Angeles or founded by 'Andy'.

sql: SELECT uid, Airline, Abbreviation FROM airlines WHERE Country = 'USA';
result_summary: Fetch the unique ID, name, and abbreviation of all airlines that are located in the USA.

sql: SELECT AirportCode, AirportName, Country FROM airports WHERE TRIM(City) = 'Aberdeen';
result_summary: Fetch the airport code, airport name, and country for all airports located in the city of Aberdeen.

sql: SELECT f.FlightNo, f.SourceAirport, f.DestAirport FROM flights AS f JOIN airlines AS a ON f.Airline = a.uid WHERE a.Airline = 'United Airlines';
result_summary: Fetch the flight number, source airport, and destination airport for all flights operated by United Airlines.

sql: SELECT al.Airline, f.FlightNo FROM flights AS f JOIN airlines AS al ON f.Airline = al.uid JOIN airports AS ap ON TRIM(f.SourceAirport) = ap.AirportCode WHERE ap.AirportName = 'Phillips AAF ';
result_summary: This query retrieves the airline and flight number for all flights departing from the Phillips AAF airport.

sql: SELECT sa.AirportName, COUNT(f.FlightNo) AS NumberOfDepartingFlights FROM flights AS f JOIN airports AS sa ON TRIM(f.SourceAirport) = sa.AirportCode WHERE TRIM(sa.Country) = 'United States' GROUP BY sa.AirportName;
result_summary: This query fetches the name of each airport in the United States along with the total number of flights that depart from it.

sql: WITH RankedFlights AS (SELECT a.Airline AS AirlineName, f.FlightNo, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY a.Airline ORDER BY f.FlightNo DESC) as rn FROM flights f JOIN airlines a ON f.Airline = a.uid) SELECT AirlineName, FlightNo FROM RankedFlights WHERE rn = 1;
result_summary: Retrieve the name of each airline and its single highest flight number.

sql: SELECT a.AirportCode, a.AirportName FROM airports a WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM flights f WHERE f.Airline = 1 AND TRIM(f.DestAirport) = a.AirportCode);
result_summary: This query retrieves the code and name of all airports that are not a destination for any flight operated by airline ID 1.

sql: SELECT AirportCode, AirportName FROM airports WHERE AirportCode IN (SELECT TRIM(SourceAirport) FROM flights WHERE Airline = 1 INTERSECT SELECT TRIM(DestAirport) FROM flights WHERE Airline = 1);
result_summary: Fetch the airport code and name for all airports that serve as both a source and a destination for flights operated by airline 1.

sql: WITH DepartureCounts AS (SELECT al.Airline AS AirlineName, ap.City, COUNT(f.FlightNo) AS NumDepartures, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY al.Airline ORDER BY COUNT(f.FlightNo) DESC) AS RankNum FROM flights AS f JOIN airlines AS al ON f.Airline = al.uid JOIN airports AS ap ON TRIM(f.SourceAirport) = ap.AirportCode WHERE al.Country = 'USA' GROUP BY al.Airline, ap.City) SELECT AirlineName, TRIM(City) AS TopDepartureCity, NumDepartures FROM DepartureCounts WHERE RankNum = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the top departure city and its corresponding number of departures for each individual airline in the USA.

sql: SELECT City, Hanzi, Hanyu_Pinyin, Regional_Population, GDP FROM city WHERE City = 'Shanghai';
result_summary: Fetch the city name, Hanzi, Hanyu Pinyin, regional population, and GDP for the city of Shanghai.

sql: SELECT Date, Venue, Score, Result FROM match WHERE Competition = 'Friendly match';
result_summary: This query retrieves the date, venue, score, and result for all matches that were 'Friendly match' competitions.

sql: SELECT Jan, Feb, Mar, Apr, Jun, Jul, Aug, Sep, Oct, Nov, Dec FROM temperature WHERE City_ID = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the monthly temperatures for a single, specific city identified by its ID.

sql: SELECT Match_ID, Host_City FROM hosting_city WHERE Year = 2008;
result_summary: Fetch the match ID and host city for all matches that took place in the year 2008.

sql: SELECT City, Regional_Population FROM city ORDER BY Regional_Population DESC LIMIT 3;
result_summary: Fetch the city and regional population for the top three most populous cities.

sql: SELECT T1.City, T1.Hanzi FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City WHERE T2.Year = 2009;
result_summary: Fetch the city name and its Hanzi representation for the city that hosted an event in the year 2009.

sql: SELECT T1.Date, T1.Result, T1.Competition FROM match AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.Match_ID = T2.Match_ID WHERE T2.Host_City = '2';
result_summary: Fetch the date, result, and competition for all matches that took place in the host city with an ID of 2.

sql: SELECT T1.City, T2.Jan, T2.Jul FROM city AS T1 JOIN temperature AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.City_ID WHERE T1.City = 'Shanghai';
result_summary: Fetch the city name and its January and July temperatures for the city of Shanghai.

sql: SELECT Host_City, COUNT(Match_ID) AS NumberOfMatches FROM hosting_city GROUP BY Host_City;
result_summary: Count the total number of matches that have been held in each host city.

sql: SELECT T1.Host_City FROM hosting_city AS T1 GROUP BY T1.Host_City HAVING COUNT(T1.Match_ID) > 1;
result_summary: Fetch the host cities that have hosted more than one match.

sql: SELECT AVG(Regional_Population) AS AveragePopulation, MAX(GDP) AS MaxGDP FROM city;
result_summary: Calculate the average regional population and the maximum Gross Domestic Product (GDP) across all cities.

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.GDP) FROM city AS T1 WHERE T1.City_ID IN (SELECT DISTINCT Host_City FROM hosting_city);
result_summary: Fetch the average GDP of all cities that have ever been a host city.

sql: SELECT T1.City, T3.Date, T3.Competition FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City JOIN match AS T3 ON T2.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T2.Year = 2010;
result_summary: Fetch the city, date, and competition for matches that were hosted in the year 2010.

sql: SELECT City, GDP, Regional_Population FROM city WHERE GDP > (SELECT AVG(GDP) FROM city);
result_summary: This query fetches the city, GDP, and regional population for all cities whose GDP is greater than the average GDP of all cities.

sql: WITH HostCityIDs AS (SELECT DISTINCT Host_City FROM hosting_city) SELECT c.City, (t.Jan+t.Feb+t.Mar+t.Apr+t.Jun+t.Jul+t.Aug+t.Sep+t.Oct+t.Nov+t.Dec)/11.0 AS Avg_Annual_Temp FROM city c JOIN temperature t ON c.City_ID = t.City_ID JOIN HostCityIDs hc ON c.City_ID = hc.Host_City;
result_summary: Fetch the name and average annual temperature for each city designated as a host city.

sql: SELECT c.City, (SELECT COUNT(m.Match_ID) FROM hosting_city hc JOIN match m ON hc.Match_ID = m.Match_ID WHERE hc.Host_City = c.City_ID AND m.Competition = '1994 FIFA World Cup qualification') AS QualificationMatchesHosted FROM city c WHERE c.City = 'Nanjing ( Jiangsu )';
result_summary: Fetch the city name 'Nanjing ( Jiangsu )' and the total number of '1994 FIFA World Cup qualification' matches it hosted.

sql: SELECT City, Regional_Population, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY Regional_Population DESC) as population_rank FROM city;
result_summary: Fetch the city name, its regional population, and a calculated population rank for each city, with the rank based on population in descending order.

sql: SELECT City, GDP FROM city WHERE Regional_Population > 20000000 UNION SELECT c.City, c.GDP FROM city c JOIN hosting_city hc ON c.City_ID = hc.Host_City WHERE hc.Year = 2008;
result_summary: This query retrieves the city and GDP for all cities that either have a regional population greater than 20 million or were a host city in the year 2008.

sql: SELECT T3.Competition, T3.Result FROM hosting_city AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.Host_City = T2.City_ID JOIN match AS T3 ON T1.Match_ID = T3.Match_ID WHERE T2.City_ID = (SELECT City_ID FROM city ORDER BY GDP DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Fetch the competition and result of matches that took place in the city with the highest Gross Domestic Product (GDP).

sql: SELECT AVG(T1.Regional_Population) FROM city AS T1 JOIN temperature AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.City_ID JOIN hosting_city AS T3 ON T1.City_ID = T3.Host_City JOIN match AS T4 ON T3.Match_ID = T4.Match_ID WHERE T4.Competition = 'Friendly match' AND T2.Jan < 18.0;
result_summary: Calculate the average regional population for all cities that hosted a 'Friendly match' and have a January temperature below 18.0.

sql: SELECT T1.City, T1.GDP, (SELECT AVG(T2.GDP) FROM city AS T2 WHERE T2.City_ID <> T1.City_ID) AS Avg_GDP_Of_Others FROM city AS T1 WHERE T1.City = 'Shanghai';
result_summary: Fetch the GDP for the city of Shanghai, along with the calculated average GDP of all other cities.

sql: SELECT T1.City, T1.Hanyu_Pinyin FROM city AS T1 WHERE T1.City_ID IN (SELECT T2.Host_City FROM hosting_city AS T2 WHERE T2.Year = 2009 INTERSECT SELECT T3.Host_City FROM hosting_city AS T3 WHERE T3.Year = 2010);
result_summary: Fetch the city name and Hanyu Pinyin for cities that hosted events in both the years 2009 and 2010.

sql: SELECT City, GDP FROM city c WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM hosting_city hc WHERE hc.Host_City = c.City_ID);
result_summary: Retrieve the city and GDP of all cities that have never been a host city.

sql: WITH MonthlyTemps AS (SELECT City_ID, 'Jan' as Month, Jan as Temp FROM temperature UNION ALL SELECT City_ID, 'Feb' as Month, Feb as Temp FROM temperature UNION ALL SELECT City_ID, 'Jul' as Month, Jul as Temp FROM temperature UNION ALL SELECT City_ID, 'Dec' as Month, Dec as Temp FROM temperature) SELECT T2.City, T1.Month, T1.Temp FROM MonthlyTemps AS T1 JOIN city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.City_ID WHERE T1.Temp = (SELECT MAX(Temp) FROM MonthlyTemps WHERE City_ID = T1.City_ID) AND T1.City_ID = 2;
result_summary: Find the month with the highest temperature for the city with ID 2, returning the city's name, that month, and the corresponding temperature.

sql: SELECT T2.Year, T1.City, T1.Regional_Population, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY T2.Year ORDER BY T1.Regional_Population DESC) as Population_Rank_For_Year FROM city AS T1 JOIN hosting_city AS T2 ON T1.City_ID = T2.Host_City WHERE T2.Year IN (2009, 2010);
result_summary: This query fetches the year, city, and regional population of hosting cities for the years 2009 and 2010, ranking them by population within each respective year.

sql: SELECT Name, Party FROM people WHERE Age > 40;
result_summary: Fetch the name and political party of people who are older than 40.

sql: SELECT Venue, Num_of_Audience FROM debate WHERE Date = 'October 21, 2011';
result_summary: Find the venue and number of audience members for the debate that occurred on October 21, 2011.

sql: SELECT d.Date, d.Venue, p.Name FROM debate AS d JOIN debate_people AS dp ON d.Debate_ID = dp.Debate_ID JOIN people AS p ON dp.Affirmative = p.People_ID WHERE p.Party = 'Democratic';
result_summary: This query retrieves the date, venue, and name of the person who argued the affirmative side in a debate, but only for people from the Democratic party.

sql: SELECT p.Name, p.District, d.Num_of_Audience FROM people AS p JOIN debate_people AS dp ON p.People_ID = dp.Negative JOIN debate AS d ON dp.Debate_ID = d.Debate_ID WHERE dp.If_Affirmative_Win = 'T';
result_summary: Fetch the name, district, and number of audience members for people who were on the losing (negative) side of a debate.

sql: SELECT p_aff.Name AS Affirmative_Participant, p_neg.Name AS Negative_Participant, d.Venue FROM debate_people AS dp JOIN people AS p_aff ON dp.Affirmative = p_aff.People_ID JOIN people AS p_neg ON dp.Negative = p_neg.People_ID JOIN debate AS d ON dp.Debate_ID = d.Debate_ID WHERE d.Num_of_Audience > 300;
result_summary: Fetch the names of the affirmative and negative participants and the debate venue for all debates with more than 300 audience members.

sql: SELECT p.Name, COUNT(p.People_ID) AS Number_of_Debates FROM people AS p JOIN (SELECT Affirmative AS Person_ID FROM debate_people UNION ALL SELECT Negative AS Person_ID FROM debate_people) AS participants ON p.People_ID = participants.Person_ID GROUP BY p.People_ID, p.Name ORDER BY Number_of_Debates DESC;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each person and the total number of debates they participated in, ordered from the most debates to the least.

sql: SELECT p_aff.Name AS Winner_Name, p_aff.Party AS Winner_Party, d.Date FROM debate_people AS dp JOIN people AS p_aff ON dp.Affirmative = p_aff.People_ID JOIN debate AS d ON dp.Debate_ID = d.Debate_ID WHERE dp.If_Affirmative_Win = 'T' UNION SELECT p_neg.Name, p_neg.Party, d.Date FROM debate_people AS dp JOIN people AS p_neg ON dp.Negative = p_neg.People_ID JOIN debate AS d ON dp.Debate_ID = d.Debate_ID WHERE dp.If_Affirmative_Win = 'F';
result_summary: Fetch the name, party, and debate date for the winner of each debate, regardless of whether they argued for the affirmative or negative position.

sql: SELECT Party, AVG(Age) AS Average_Age_of_Participants FROM people WHERE People_ID IN (SELECT Affirmative FROM debate_people WHERE Debate_ID IN (SELECT Debate_ID FROM debate WHERE Venue = 'Manama , Bahrain')) OR People_ID IN (SELECT Negative FROM debate_people WHERE Debate_ID IN (SELECT Debate_ID FROM debate WHERE Venue = 'Manama , Bahrain')) GROUP BY Party;
result_summary: This query retrieves the average age of participants for each political party, considering only those who took part in debates held in 'Manama, Bahrain'.

sql: WITH PartyWins AS (SELECT p.Party, SUM(CASE WHEN dp.Affirmative = p.People_ID AND dp.If_Affirmative_Win = 'T' THEN 1 WHEN dp.Negative = p.People_ID AND dp.If_Affirmative_Win = 'F' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Wins FROM people p JOIN debate_people dp ON p.People_ID = dp.Affirmative OR p.People_ID = dp.Negative GROUP BY p.Party) SELECT Party, Wins FROM PartyWins WHERE Wins = (SELECT MAX(Wins) FROM PartyWins);
result_summary: Retrieve the political party that has the highest number of debate wins, along with their total win count.

sql: SELECT p1.Name AS Person1, p2.Name AS Person2, d.Date, d.Venue FROM debate_people dp JOIN people p1 ON dp.Affirmative = p1.People_ID JOIN people p2 ON dp.Negative = p2.People_ID JOIN debate d ON dp.Debate_ID = d.Debate_ID WHERE p1.Party = p2.Party AND p1.Party = 'Republican';
result_summary: This query retrieves the names of two participants, the date, and the venue for any debate that occurred between two people from the Republican party.

sql: SELECT Participant_ID, Participant_Details FROM Participants WHERE Participant_Type_Code = 'Organizer';
result_summary: Fetch the ID and details of all participants who are classified as organizers.

sql: SELECT Service_ID FROM Services WHERE Service_Type_Code = 'Birth Proof';
result_summary: Fetch the service ID for the service with a service type code of 'Birth Proof'.

sql: SELECT e.Event_ID, s.Service_Type_Code, e.Event_Details FROM Events AS e JOIN Services AS s ON e.Service_ID = s.Service_ID WHERE e.Event_Details = 'Success';
result_summary: Fetch the event ID, service type code, and event details for all events that were successful by joining the Events and Services tables.

sql: SELECT p.Participant_Details FROM Participants AS p JOIN Participants_in_Events AS pie ON p.Participant_ID = pie.Participant_ID WHERE pie.Event_ID = 3;
result_summary: Fetches the details of participants who are part of the event with an ID of 3.

sql: SELECT Event_ID, COUNT(Participant_ID) AS NumberOfParticipants FROM Participants_in_Events GROUP BY Event_ID;
result_summary: This query retrieves the total number of participants for each event.

sql: SELECT s.Service_Type_Code, COUNT(CASE WHEN e.Event_Details = 'Success' THEN 1 END) AS successful_events, COUNT(CASE WHEN e.Event_Details = 'Fail' THEN 1 END) AS failed_events FROM Services AS s LEFT JOIN Events AS e ON s.Service_ID = e.Service_ID GROUP BY s.Service_Type_Code;
result_summary: Fetch the total count of successful and failed events for each type of service.

sql: SELECT p.Participant_Details FROM Participants AS p WHERE p.Participant_ID IN (SELECT pie.Participant_ID FROM Participants_in_Events AS pie JOIN Events AS e ON pie.Event_ID = e.Event_ID WHERE e.Service_ID = 5);
result_summary: Fetch the details of participants who took part in any event associated with service ID 5.

sql: SELECT Service_ID, Service_Type_Code FROM Services AS s WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM Events AS e WHERE e.Service_ID = s.Service_ID AND e.Event_Details = 'Fail');
result_summary: Fetch the ID and type code for all services that have a corresponding failed event.

sql: WITH EventParticipants AS (SELECT e.Event_ID, p.Participant_Details, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY e.Event_ID ORDER BY p.Participant_ID) as participant_rank FROM Events AS e JOIN Participants_in_Events AS pie ON e.Event_ID = pie.Event_ID JOIN Participants AS p ON pie.Participant_ID = p.Participant_ID WHERE e.Event_ID = 3) SELECT Event_ID, Participant_Details FROM EventParticipants WHERE participant_rank > 1;
result_summary: Fetch the event ID and details for all participants of event ID 3, excluding the one with the lowest participant ID.

sql: SELECT p.Participant_Details FROM Participants AS p JOIN Participants_in_Events AS pie ON p.Participant_ID = pie.Participant_ID WHERE pie.Event_ID = 3 EXCEPT SELECT p.Participant_Details FROM Participants AS p JOIN Participants_in_Events AS pie ON p.Participant_ID = pie.Participant_ID WHERE pie.Event_ID = 8;
result_summary: Fetch the details of participants who attended event 3 but did not attend event 8.

sql: SELECT * FROM county WHERE County_name = 'Howard';
result_summary: Fetch all data for the county named 'Howard'.

sql: SELECT Party, Governor, US_Senate FROM party WHERE Year = 1998.0;
result_summary: Fetch the political party, governor, and US Senate candidate for the year 1998.

sql: SELECT Delegate, First_Elected FROM election WHERE Committee = 'Appropriations';
result_summary: Fetch the delegate and the year they were first elected for all members of the 'Appropriations' committee.

sql: SELECT County_name, Population FROM county WHERE Zip_code = 'D08';
result_summary: Fetch the name and population of the county with the zip code 'D08'.

sql: SELECT Year, Party FROM party WHERE Governor = 'Carl McCall';
result_summary: Fetch the year and party for the governor named Carl McCall.

sql: SELECT e.Delegate, c.County_name FROM election AS e JOIN county AS c ON e.District = c.County_Id WHERE c.County_name = 'Baltimore County';
result_summary: Fetch the names of delegates and their corresponding county name, filtered to show only results for Baltimore County.

sql: SELECT e.Delegate, p.Party FROM election AS e JOIN party AS p ON e.Party = p.Party_ID WHERE e.First_Elected = 2002.0;
result_summary: Fetch the delegate and their political party for those first elected in the year 2002.

sql: SELECT Committee, COUNT(Delegate) AS Delegate_Count FROM election GROUP BY Committee;
result_summary: Fetch the total count of delegates for each committee from the election data.

sql: SELECT Zip_code, SUM(Population) AS Total_Population FROM county GROUP BY Zip_code;
result_summary: Fetch the sum of the population for each zip code by grouping the data from the county table.

sql: SELECT Attorney_General, COUNT(*) AS Times_Appeared FROM party WHERE Attorney_General = 'Eliot Spitzer' GROUP BY Attorney_General;
result_summary: Fetch the total count of appearances for the Attorney General named 'Eliot Spitzer'.

sql: SELECT c.County_name, MIN(e.First_Elected) AS Earliest_Election_Year FROM election AS e JOIN county AS c ON e.District = c.County_Id GROUP BY c.County_name;
result_summary: Fetch each county's name and its corresponding earliest election year.

sql: SELECT p.Party, COUNT(e.Election_ID) AS Number_Of_Delegates FROM party AS p JOIN election AS e ON p.Party_ID = e.Party WHERE p.Party_ID = 1 GROUP BY p.Party;
result_summary: Fetch the name of a specific political party and the total count of its delegates.

sql: SELECT e.Delegate, c.County_name, p.Governor FROM election AS e JOIN county AS c ON e.District = c.County_Id JOIN party AS p ON e.Party = p.Party_ID WHERE e.First_Elected = 2002.0 AND p.Party = 'Democratic';
result_summary: Fetch the delegate, county name, and governor for Democratic delegates who were first elected in 2002.

sql: SELECT Party, Governor FROM party WHERE Year IN (SELECT Year FROM party WHERE US_Senate = 'Charles Schumer') AND Party = 'Liberal';
result_summary: Fetch the party and governor for the 'Liberal' party during the same years that Charles Schumer served as a US Senator.

sql: WITH DelegateRanks AS (SELECT Delegate, Committee, First_Elected, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY Committee ORDER BY First_Elected ASC) as Election_Rank FROM election) SELECT Delegate, Committee, First_Elected FROM DelegateRanks WHERE Election_Rank = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the delegate, committee, and first election year for the earliest elected member or members within each committee.

sql: SELECT p.Party, p.Year FROM party AS p LEFT JOIN election AS e ON p.Party_ID = e.Party WHERE e.Election_ID IS NULL;
result_summary: Fetch the names and years for political parties that have not participated in any election.

sql: SELECT Governor AS Candidate, 'Governor' AS Office FROM party WHERE Year = 1998.0 UNION ALL SELECT Delegate, 'Delegate' AS Office FROM election WHERE First_Elected = 2002.0;
result_summary: Fetch a list of candidates and their office, combining governors from the year 1998 with delegates who were first elected in 2002.

sql: SELECT c.County_name FROM county AS c WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM election AS e WHERE e.District = c.County_Id AND e.Committee = 'Economic Matters');
result_summary: Find the name of the county that has the 'Economic Matters' committee.

sql: SELECT e1.Delegate, e2.Delegate AS Colleague_In_Same_District FROM election AS e1 JOIN election AS e2 ON e1.District = e2.District AND e1.Election_ID < e2.Election_ID WHERE e1.District = 1;
result_summary: Fetches the names of delegate pairs who serve in the same district, filtered to show only those from District 1.

sql: SELECT County_name FROM county WHERE Population > (SELECT AVG(Population) FROM county);
result_summary: Fetch the names of all counties that have a population greater than the average population.

sql: SELECT p1.Party, p2.Party, p1.Year, p1.US_Senate FROM party AS p1 JOIN party AS p2 ON p1.Year = p2.Year AND p1.US_Senate = p2.US_Senate AND p1.Party_ID < p2.Party_ID WHERE p1.US_Senate = 'Charles Schumer';
result_summary: Finds pairs of different political parties that were associated with US Senator 'Charles Schumer' in the same year, returning both party names, the year, and the senator's name.

sql: SELECT Delegate, Committee, First_Elected FROM election e WHERE First_Elected = (SELECT MIN(First_Elected) FROM election WHERE Committee = e.Committee);
result_summary: This query retrieves the delegate, committee, and their first election year for the member(s) who have served the longest in each respective committee.

sql: WITH PartyCommitteeCounts AS (SELECT p.Party, e.Committee, COUNT(e.Delegate) as DelegateCount FROM election AS e JOIN party AS p ON e.Party = p.Party_ID GROUP BY p.Party, e.Committee) SELECT Party, Committee, DelegateCount FROM PartyCommitteeCounts WHERE Party = 'Democratic';
result_summary: Retrieve the count of delegates for each committee within the Democratic party.

sql: SELECT p.Comptroller FROM party AS p WHERE p.Party = 'Democratic' INTERSECT SELECT p.Comptroller FROM party AS p WHERE p.Party = 'Liberal';
result_summary: Find the comptrollers who are members of both the Democratic and Liberal parties.

sql: SELECT Year, SUM(CASE WHEN Party = 'Democratic' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Democratic_Candidates, SUM(CASE WHEN Party = 'Liberal' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Liberal_Candidates FROM party WHERE Year = 1998.0 GROUP BY Year;
result_summary: Fetch the total number of candidates from the Democratic and Liberal parties for the year 1998.

sql: SELECT Name, Country FROM mountain;
result_summary: Fetch the name and country for every mountain.

sql: SELECT Name, Points FROM climber;
result_summary: Fetch the name and points of all climbers.

sql: SELECT Name, Height FROM mountain WHERE Country = 'Tanzania';
result_summary: Fetch the name and height of mountains located in Tanzania.

sql: SELECT Name, Time, Points FROM climber WHERE Country = 'West Germany';
result_summary: Fetch the name, time, and points for all climbers from West Germany.

sql: SELECT Name, Range FROM mountain WHERE Height > 5500.0;
result_summary: Fetch the name and range of mountains with a height greater than 5500.0.

sql: SELECT c.Name AS ClimberName, c.Time FROM climber AS c JOIN mountain AS m ON c.Mountain_ID = m.Mountain_ID WHERE m.Name = 'Kibo (Uhuru Pk)';
result_summary: Fetch the names and times of climbers who climbed the mountain named 'Kibo (Uhuru Pk)'.

sql: SELECT m.Name, COUNT(c.Climber_ID) AS NumberOfClimbers FROM mountain AS m JOIN climber AS c ON m.Mountain_ID = c.Mountain_ID GROUP BY m.Name;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each mountain and the corresponding total count of climbers for each one.

sql: SELECT AVG(Points) AS AveragePoints FROM climber WHERE Country = 'West Germany';
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Calculate the average points for all climbers from West Germany.

sql: SELECT m.Name, SUM(c.Points) AS TotalPointsAwarded FROM mountain AS m JOIN climber AS c ON m.Mountain_ID = c.Mountain_ID GROUP BY m.Name ORDER BY TotalPointsAwarded DESC;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each mountain and the total sum of points awarded for it, ordered by the highest total points awarded.

sql: SELECT Name, Prominence FROM mountain WHERE Range = 'Kilimanjaro' ORDER BY Prominence ASC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name and prominence of the single least prominent mountain from the 'Kilimanjaro' range.

sql: SELECT c.Name AS ClimberName, m.Name AS MountainName FROM climber AS c JOIN mountain AS m ON c.Mountain_ID = m.Mountain_ID WHERE m.Country = 'Kenya';
result_summary: List the names of climbers and the mountains they climbed, specifically for all mountains located in Kenya.

sql: SELECT Name, Points FROM climber ORDER BY Points DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name and points of the single climber with the most points.

sql: SELECT c.Name AS ClimberName, m.Name AS MountainName FROM climber AS c JOIN mountain AS m ON c.Mountain_ID = m.Mountain_ID WHERE m.Prominence > (SELECT Prominence FROM mountain WHERE Name = 'Mount Kenya (Batian)');
result_summary: Fetch the names of climbers and the mountains they are associated with, for all mountains that have a prominence greater than Mount Kenya (Batian).

sql: SELECT Country, COUNT(*) AS ClimberCount FROM climber GROUP BY Country ORDER BY ClimberCount DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Fetch the country with the highest count of climbers, along with that total count.

sql: SELECT m.Country, m.Name AS HighestMountain, m.Height FROM mountain m WHERE m.Height = (SELECT MAX(m2.Height) FROM mountain m2 WHERE m2.Country = m.Country);
result_summary: This query retrieves the country, name, and height of the tallest mountain for each country in the database.

sql: SELECT Name FROM climber WHERE Country = 'West Germany' AND Mountain_ID IN (SELECT Mountain_ID FROM mountain WHERE Range = 'Kilimanjaro');
result_summary: Fetch the names of climbers from West Germany who have climbed a mountain in the Kilimanjaro range.

sql: SELECT Name, Range FROM mountain m WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM climber c WHERE c.Mountain_ID = m.Mountain_ID);
result_summary: Fetch the name and range of all mountains that have no recorded climbers.

sql: SELECT c.Name, c.Points FROM climber c WHERE c.Mountain_ID = (SELECT Mountain_ID FROM mountain ORDER BY Height DESC LIMIT 1);
result_summary: Fetch the name and points of climbers who are associated with the highest mountain.

sql: SELECT Name, Points, Mountain_ID, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY Mountain_ID ORDER BY Points DESC) AS RankOnMountain FROM climber;
result_summary: Fetch the name, points, and mountain ID for each climber, along with their rank on each mountain based on points in descending order.

sql: SELECT c.Name, c.Points FROM climber c JOIN mountain m ON c.Mountain_ID = m.Mountain_ID WHERE m.Name = 'Mount Kenya (Batian)' AND c.Points > (SELECT AVG(Points) FROM climber);
result_summary: This query retrieves the name and points of climbers who have climbed 'Mount Kenya (Batian)' and whose points are greater than the average points of all climbers.

sql: SELECT m.Name, COUNT(c.Climber_ID) AS GermanClimbersCount FROM mountain m LEFT JOIN climber c ON m.Mountain_ID = c.Mountain_ID AND c.Country = 'West Germany' GROUP BY m.Name;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each mountain and the corresponding count of climbers from West Germany.

sql: SELECT c1.Name AS Climber1, c2.Name AS Climber2, c1.Country FROM climber c1 JOIN climber c2 ON c1.Country = c2.Country AND c1.Climber_ID < c2.Climber_ID;
result_summary: Fetch all unique pairs of climbers who are from the same country, returning both of their names and their shared country.

sql: SELECT m.Name, COUNT(c.Climber_ID) AS NumberOfClimbers FROM mountain m JOIN climber c ON m.Mountain_ID = c.Mountain_ID GROUP BY m.Mountain_ID, m.Name HAVING COUNT(c.Climber_ID) > 1;
result_summary: This query fetches the name of each mountain and the total number of climbers, but only for mountains that have been climbed by more than one person.

sql: SELECT m.Name, (SELECT c.Name FROM climber c WHERE c.Mountain_ID = m.Mountain_ID ORDER BY c.Points DESC LIMIT 1) AS TopClimber FROM mountain m WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM climber c WHERE c.Mountain_ID = m.Mountain_ID);
result_summary: This query lists each mountain that has been climbed and identifies the name of the top climber for that mountain based on their points.

sql: WITH TanzanianMountains AS (SELECT Mountain_ID, Name FROM mountain WHERE Country = 'Tanzania') SELECT c.Name AS ClimberName, tm.Name AS MountainName FROM climber c JOIN TanzanianMountains tm ON c.Mountain_ID = tm.Mountain_ID;
result_summary: Fetch a list of climbers and the specific Tanzanian mountains they have climbed.

sql: SELECT * FROM Person WHERE name = 'Alice';
result_summary: Fetch all personal details for a person from the Person table with the name 'Alice'.

sql: SELECT name, age FROM Person WHERE job = 'engineer' AND city = 'salt lake city';
result_summary: Fetch the name and age of all engineers who live in Salt Lake City.

sql: SELECT name, friend FROM PersonFriend WHERE year = 10;
result_summary: Fetch the name of a person and their friend from the records for year 10.

sql: SELECT name FROM Person WHERE gender = 'male';
result_summary: Fetch the names of all people who are identified as male.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Person WHERE age > 30;
result_summary: Count the total number of people whose age is greater than 30.

sql: SELECT T2.name, T2.age, T2.job FROM PersonFriend AS T1 JOIN Person AS T2 ON T1.friend = T2.name WHERE T1.name = 'Alice';
result_summary: Fetch the name, age, and job of all people who are friends with the person named 'Alice'.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name WHERE T2.year > 10;
result_summary: Fetch the names of people who have been friends for more than 10 years.

sql: SELECT city, COUNT(*) FROM Person GROUP BY city;
result_summary: Fetch the total count of people for each city.

sql: SELECT name, COUNT(friend) FROM PersonFriend GROUP BY name;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each person and the count of their friends.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.friend WHERE T2.name = 'Zach';
result_summary: Retrieve the names of all people who are friends with 'Zach'.

sql: SELECT gender, AVG(age) AS average_age FROM Person GROUP BY gender;
result_summary: Fetch the average age of people, grouped by gender.

sql: SELECT name, friend FROM PersonFriend ORDER BY year DESC LIMIT 2;
result_summary: Fetch the name and friend for the two most recent friendships, ordered by year in descending order.

sql: SELECT name, age FROM Person WHERE age > (SELECT AVG(age) FROM Person);
result_summary: Fetch the names and ages of people who are older than the average age.

sql: SELECT T2.friend FROM PersonFriend AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.friend = T2.name WHERE T1.name = 'Alice' AND T2.friend <> 'Alice' AND T2.friend NOT IN (SELECT friend FROM PersonFriend WHERE name = 'Alice');
result_summary: Fetch the names of 'friends of friends' for a person named Alice, excluding anyone who is already a direct friend or Alice herself.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Person AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM PersonFriend AS T2 WHERE T1.name = T2.name AND T2.year > 10);
result_summary: Fetch the names of people who have been friends for more than 10 years.

sql: WITH FriendCounts AS (SELECT name, COUNT(*) AS num_friends FROM PersonFriend GROUP BY name) SELECT p.name, p.age, fc.num_friends FROM Person AS p JOIN FriendCounts AS fc ON p.name = fc.name ORDER BY fc.num_friends DESC;
result_summary: Fetch the data containing each person's name, age, and their number of friends, ordered by the number of friends in descending order.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.age, T1.city FROM Person AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name JOIN Person AS T3 ON T2.friend = T3.name WHERE T1.gender = 'male' AND T3.gender = 'male';
result_summary: Fetch the name, age, and city of all males who have at least one male friend.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.name FROM PersonFriend AS T1 JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name WHERE T1.friend = 'Bob' OR T1.friend = 'Zach';
result_summary: Fetch the distinct names of people who are friends with either Bob or Zach.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.year FROM PersonFriend AS T1 JOIN Person AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name WHERE T2.age = (SELECT MIN(age) FROM Person);
result_summary: Find the name and year of the youngest person who is also listed as a friend.

sql: SELECT T1.name, T1.age FROM Person AS T1 JOIN (SELECT name, MAX(year) AS max_year FROM PersonFriend GROUP BY name) AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name WHERE T1.city = 'austin';
result_summary: Fetch the name and age of people from Austin who have at least one friend record.

sql: SELECT name FROM Person UNION SELECT friend FROM PersonFriend;
result_summary: Fetch a unique list of all names by combining the names of people and their friends.

sql: SELECT T1.name FROM Person AS T1 LEFT JOIN PersonFriend AS T2 ON T1.name = T2.name WHERE T2.name IS NULL;
result_summary: Find the names of people who do not have any friends.

sql: SELECT name, CASE WHEN age > 40 THEN 'Over 40' WHEN age > 30 THEN '31-40' ELSE '30 or Under' END AS age_group FROM Person WHERE city = 'salt lake city' OR city = 'austin';
result_summary: Fetch the name and a categorized age group for each person who lives in either Salt Lake City or Austin.

sql: SELECT Name, age, Rank FROM captain WHERE Class = 'Third-rate ship of the line';
result_summary: Fetch the name, age, and rank of all captains from the 'Third-rate ship of the line' class.

sql: SELECT Name, Type, Built_Year FROM ship WHERE Flag = 'Panama';
result_summary: Fetch the name, type, and built year for all ships that have the 'Panama' flag.

sql: SELECT T1.Name AS CaptainName, T2.Name AS ShipName, T2.Built_Year FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Name = 'HMS Gorgon';
result_summary: This query retrieves the name of the captain, the name of their ship, and the year the ship was built, specifically for the ship named 'HMS Gorgon'.

sql: SELECT Flag, COUNT(*) AS NumberOfShips FROM ship GROUP BY Flag;
result_summary: Fetch the total number of ships for each distinct flag.

sql: SELECT T2.Type, AVG(CAST(T1.age AS REAL)) AS AverageCaptainAge FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Type = 'Panamax' GROUP BY T2.Type;
result_summary: Fetch the ship type and the average age of captains for all ships of the 'Panamax' type.

sql: SELECT Name, age FROM captain WHERE CAST(age AS REAL) > (SELECT AVG(CAST(age AS REAL)) FROM captain);
result_summary: Fetch the name and age of captains whose age is greater than the average age of all captains.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Built_Year = (SELECT Built_Year FROM ship WHERE Name = 'HMS Manxman');
result_summary: Fetch the names of captains whose ships were built in the same year as the ship named 'HMS Manxman'.

sql: SELECT Ship_ID, Name, Flag FROM ship WHERE Ship_ID NOT IN (SELECT Ship_ID FROM captain);
result_summary: Fetch the ID, name, and flag for all ships that do not have an assigned captain.

sql: SELECT Name FROM captain WHERE Class = 'Cutter' UNION SELECT T1.Name FROM captain AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T2.Flag = 'Panama' AND T1.Rank = 'Midshipman';
result_summary: Retrieve the names of captains who are either of the 'Cutter' class or are Midshipmen on a ship with a 'Panama' flag.

sql: WITH RankedCaptains AS (SELECT Name, age, Ship_ID, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY Ship_ID ORDER BY CAST(age AS INTEGER) DESC) as rn FROM captain) SELECT T1.Name, T1.age, T2.Name AS ShipName FROM RankedCaptains AS T1 JOIN ship AS T2 ON T1.Ship_ID = T2.Ship_ID WHERE T1.rn = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name and age of the oldest captain for each ship, along with the name of their ship.

sql: SELECT personal_name, middle_name, family_name, address_line_1 FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors WHERE login_name = 'jmckenzie';
result_summary: Fetch the full name and primary address line of the course author or tutor with the login name 'jmckenzie'.

sql: SELECT student_id, login_name, date_of_registration FROM Students WHERE date_of_registration > '2015-10-01';
result_summary: Fetch the student ID, login name, and date of registration for students who registered after October 1st, 2015.

sql: SELECT subject_name FROM Subjects WHERE subject_id = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name of the subject with a subject ID of 1.

sql: SELECT course_name FROM Courses WHERE author_id = 8;
result_summary: Fetch the names of courses for the author with an ID of 8.

sql: SELECT registration_id, date_test_taken FROM Student_Tests_Taken WHERE test_result = 'Fail';
result_summary: Fetch the registration ID and the date a test was taken for all student tests that resulted in a 'Fail'.

sql: SELECT T1.course_name, T1.course_description, T2.subject_name FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Subjects AS T2 ON T1.subject_id = T2.subject_id WHERE T2.subject_name = 'Computer Science';
result_summary: Fetch the course name, course description, and subject name for all courses belonging to the 'Computer Science' subject.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name, T2.date_of_enrolment, T2.date_of_completion FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.course_id = 2;
result_summary: This query retrieves the personal and family names of students, along with their enrolment and completion dates, for the specific course with an ID of 2.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name, T2.course_name FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id WHERE T1.author_id = 6;
result_summary: Retrieve the first name, last name, and all associated course names for the author with an ID of 6.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name, count(T2.course_id) FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id GROUP BY T1.personal_name, T1.family_name;
result_summary: Fetch the personal and family name for each author along with a count of the total number of courses they have authored.

sql: SELECT T1.course_name FROM Courses AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.course_id = T2.course_id JOIN Student_Tests_Taken AS T3 ON T2.registration_id = T3.registration_id WHERE T3.test_result = 'Pass' GROUP BY T1.course_name;
result_summary: List the names of all courses in which at least one student has passed a test.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name, T3.course_name, T4.test_result FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id JOIN Student_Tests_Taken AS T4 ON T2.registration_id = T4.registration_id WHERE T3.subject_id = 1 AND T4.test_result = 'Pass';
result_summary: Fetch the full name, course name, and test result for all students who passed their tests for courses belonging to subject ID 1.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name, COUNT(T3.course_id) AS num_courses_enrolled FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id JOIN Courses AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id GROUP BY T1.personal_name, T1.family_name HAVING COUNT(T3.course_id) > 1;
result_summary: Fetch the full names of students and the number of courses they are enrolled in, for only those students enrolled in more than one course.

sql: SELECT T1.subject_name, COUNT(T3.student_id) AS num_students FROM Subjects AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.subject_id = T2.subject_id JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id GROUP BY T1.subject_name ORDER BY num_students DESC;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each subject and the total count of enrolled students, ordered by the student count in descending order.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T1 WHERE T1.author_id NOT IN (SELECT T2.author_id FROM Courses AS T2 JOIN Subjects AS T3 ON T2.subject_id = T3.subject_id WHERE T3.subject_name = 'Arts');
result_summary: Fetch the data containing the personal and family names of all course authors and tutors who have not created a course in the 'Arts' subject.

sql: SELECT T1.subject_name, AVG(CASE WHEN T4.test_result = 'Pass' THEN 100 ELSE 0 END) AS pass_rate FROM Subjects AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.subject_id = T2.subject_id JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id JOIN Student_Tests_Taken AS T4 ON T3.registration_id = T4.registration_id GROUP BY T1.subject_name;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each subject and its corresponding average test pass rate.

sql: SELECT T2.course_name FROM Student_Tests_Taken AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.registration_id = T2.course_id GROUP BY T2.course_name HAVING count(*)  >=  2
result_summary: Fetch the names of all courses that have been taken at least two times.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Students AS T1 EXCEPT SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Students AS T1 JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id WHERE T2.course_id = 4;
result_summary: Fetch the personal and family names of all students who are not enrolled in the course with an ID of 4.

sql: SELECT T1.personal_name, T1.family_name FROM Course_Authors_and_Tutors AS T1 JOIN Courses AS T2 ON T1.author_id = T2.author_id JOIN Student_Course_Enrolment AS T3 ON T2.course_id = T3.course_id JOIN Students AS T4 ON T3.student_id = T4.student_id ORDER BY T4.date_of_registration LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Fetch the personal and family name of the course author linked to the student with the earliest registration date.

sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tv_series;
result_summary: Fetch the total count of all TV series.

sql: SELECT SSN, Name FROM Scientists;
result_summary: This query retrieves the Social Security Number (SSN) and name of every scientist.

sql: SELECT Name, Code FROM Projects;
result_summary: Fetch the name and code for every project.

sql: SELECT Name, Hours FROM Projects WHERE Hours > 200;
result_summary: Fetch the name and hours of projects that have more than 200 hours.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Scientists WHERE SSN = 152934485;
result_summary: Fetch the name of a scientist identified by a specific Social Security Number.

sql: SELECT * FROM AssignedTo WHERE Project = 'AeH1';
result_summary: Fetch the scientist assigned to the project with the identifier 'AeH1'.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM AssignedTo AS T1 JOIN Projects AS T2 ON T1.Project = T2.Code WHERE T1.Scientist = 123234877;
result_summary: Fetch the name of the project assigned to the scientist with an ID of 123234877.

sql: SELECT T2.Name FROM AssignedTo AS T1 JOIN Scientists AS T2 ON T1.Scientist = T2.SSN WHERE T1.Project = 'AeH3';
result_summary: Fetch the names of all scientists assigned to the project with the code 'AeH3'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name AS ScientistName, T3.Name AS ProjectName FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist JOIN Projects AS T3 ON T2.Project = T3.Code;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each scientist and the name of the project they are assigned to.

sql: SELECT SUM(Hours) FROM Projects;
result_summary: Calculate the total sum of all hours from the Projects table.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM AssignedTo WHERE Project = 'Ast3';
result_summary: Fetch the total count of assignments for the project named 'Ast3'.
