sql: SELECT Name FROM Projects ORDER BY Name ASC;
result_summary: Fetch the names of all projects, sorted in alphabetical order.

sql: SELECT Name, Code FROM Projects WHERE Name LIKE '%Aerodynamics%';
result_summary: Fetch the name and code for all projects that have the term 'Aerodynamics' in their name.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Scientists WHERE SSN IN (SELECT Scientist FROM AssignedTo WHERE Project IN (SELECT Code FROM Projects WHERE Hours > 180));
result_summary: Fetch the names of scientists who are assigned to projects that have a duration of more than 180 hours.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Projects WHERE Hours = (SELECT MIN(Hours) FROM Projects);
result_summary: Fetch the name of the project with the minimum number of hours.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Projects WHERE Code NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT Project FROM AssignedTo);
result_summary: Fetch the names of all projects that are unassigned.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, COUNT(T2.Project) AS NumberOfProjects FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each scientist and the total number of projects they are assigned to.

sql: SELECT AVG(T3.Hours) FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist JOIN Projects AS T3 ON T2.Project = T3.Code WHERE T1.Name = 'Michael Rogers';
result_summary: Fetch the average number of hours for all projects assigned to the scientist named 'Michael Rogers'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist WHERE T2.Project IN ('AeH1', 'AeH3');
result_summary: Fetch the names of scientists who are assigned to projects 'AeH1' or 'AeH3'.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist GROUP BY T1.SSN, T1.Name ORDER BY COUNT(T2.Project) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name of the scientist who is assigned to the most projects.

sql: SELECT T3.Name, T4.Name FROM AssignedTo AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.Project = T2.Project AND T1.Scientist < T2.Scientist JOIN Scientists AS T3 ON T1.Scientist = T3.SSN JOIN Scientists AS T4 ON T2.Scientist = T4.SSN;
result_summary: Fetch the names of all pairs of scientists who are assigned to work on the same project.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, SUM(T3.Hours) AS TotalHours FROM Scientists AS T1 JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist JOIN Projects AS T3 ON T2.Project = T3.Code GROUP BY T1.Name;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each scientist and the total sum of hours they have worked on projects.

sql: WITH ProjectStats AS (SELECT AVG(Hours) AS AvgHours FROM Projects) SELECT p.Name, p.Hours FROM Projects p, ProjectStats ps WHERE p.Hours > ps.AvgHours;
result_summary: Fetch the name and hours for all projects that have a number of hours greater than the average hours across all projects.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Scientists s WHERE Name LIKE 'A%' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM AssignedTo WHERE Scientist = s.SSN);
result_summary: Fetch the names of scientists whose names start with 'A' and who have been assigned to a project.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, CASE WHEN T3.Name IS NULL THEN 'Not Assigned' ELSE T3.Name END AS ProjectName FROM Scientists AS T1 LEFT JOIN AssignedTo AS T2 ON T1.SSN = T2.Scientist LEFT JOIN Projects AS T3 ON T2.Project = T3.Code;
result_summary: Retrieves a list of all scientists and their assigned project's name, showing 'Not Assigned' for any scientist without a project.

sql: SELECT Name, Height, Weight FROM people WHERE Height > 190;
result_summary: Fetch the data containing the name, height, and weight of people who have a height greater than 190.

sql: SELECT Poll_Source, Support_rate, Consider_rate, Unsure_rate FROM candidate WHERE Poll_Source = 'WNBC/Marist Poll';
result_summary: Fetch the poll source, support rate, consider rate, and unsure rate for candidates where the poll source is 'WNBC/Marist Poll'.

sql: SELECT p.Name, p.Date_of_Birth, c.Poll_Source, c.Date FROM people p JOIN candidate c ON p.People_ID = c.People_ID WHERE c.Date = 'Feb 12–15, 2007';
result_summary: Fetch the name, date of birth, poll source, and date for candidates from a poll conducted on 'Feb 12–15, 2007'.

sql: SELECT Poll_Source, AVG(Support_rate) AS avg_support, AVG(Oppose_rate) AS avg_oppose FROM candidate GROUP BY Poll_Source;
result_summary: This query retrieves the average support and opposition rates for candidates, aggregated by each poll source.

sql: SELECT p.Name, p.Sex FROM people p JOIN candidate c ON p.People_ID = c.People_ID WHERE p.Sex = 'F';
result_summary: Fetches the name and sex of all candidates who are female.

sql: SELECT p.Name, c.Poll_Source, c.Support_rate FROM people p JOIN candidate c ON p.People_ID = c.People_ID WHERE c.Support_rate = (SELECT MAX(Support_rate) FROM candidate);
result_summary: Find the name, poll source, and support rate of the candidate who has the highest support rate.

sql: WITH RankedCandidates AS (SELECT p.Name, c.Poll_Source, c.Support_rate, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY c.Poll_Source ORDER BY c.Support_rate DESC) as rank_num FROM people p JOIN candidate c ON p.People_ID = c.People_ID) SELECT Name, Poll_Source, Support_rate FROM RankedCandidates WHERE rank_num = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name, poll source, and support rate for the candidate with the highest support rate from each distinct poll source.

sql: SELECT p.Name, c.Support_rate FROM people p JOIN candidate c ON p.People_ID = c.People_ID WHERE c.Support_rate > (SELECT AVG(Support_rate) FROM candidate);
result_summary: This query retrieves the names and support rates of candidates who have a support rate greater than the average support rate.

sql: SELECT p.Name, p.Sex, c.Support_rate, CASE WHEN c.Support_rate > 0.2 THEN 'High Support' WHEN c.Support_rate > 0.15 THEN 'Medium Support' ELSE 'Low Support' END AS Support_Category FROM people p JOIN candidate c ON p.People_ID = c.People_ID;
result_summary: This query retrieves the name, sex, and support rate for each candidate, along with a calculated support category ('High', 'Medium', or 'Low') based on their rate of support.

sql: SELECT p.Name, p.People_ID FROM people p LEFT JOIN candidate c ON p.People_ID = c.People_ID WHERE c.Candidate_ID IS NULL;
result_summary: Fetch the name and ID for all people who are not listed as candidates.

sql: SELECT Festival_ID, Festival_Name, Year FROM festival_detail WHERE Location = 'United States';
result_summary: Fetch the festival ID, name, and year for all festivals located in the United States.

sql: SELECT Name FROM artwork WHERE Type = 'Program Music/Variety Show';
result_summary: Fetch the names of artworks where the type is 'Program Music/Variety Show'.

sql: SELECT Artwork_ID, Result FROM nomination WHERE Festival_ID = 2;
result_summary: Fetch the artwork ID and result from the nomination table for entries corresponding to festival ID 2.

sql: SELECT Chair_Name, Num_of_Audience FROM festival_detail WHERE Year = 2006;
result_summary: Fetch the chair name and number of audience members from the festival details for the year 2006.

sql: SELECT * FROM artwork WHERE Artwork_ID = 3;
result_summary: Fetch all information for the specific artwork with an ID of 3.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T1.Type FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won';
result_summary: Fetch the name and type of all artworks that have won a nomination.

sql: SELECT T2.Festival_Name, T2.Year, T2.Location FROM nomination AS T1 JOIN festival_detail AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID WHERE T1.Artwork_ID = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name, year, and location of the festival where the artwork with an ID of 1 was nominated.

sql: SELECT T2.Festival_Name, COUNT(T1.Artwork_ID) AS NumberOfNominations FROM nomination AS T1 JOIN festival_detail AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T2.Festival_Name;
result_summary: Fetch the total count of artwork nominations for each festival, grouped by the festival's name.

sql: SELECT T1.Name FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T3.Festival_Name = 'Flower Awards' AND T2.Result = 'Nominated';
result_summary: Fetch the names of artworks that received a 'Nominated' result at the 'Flower Awards' festival.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T2.Result FROM artwork AS T1 LEFT JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T1.Name = 'Loving you';
result_summary: Fetch the name and nomination result for the specific artwork titled 'Loving you'.

sql: SELECT SUM(Num_of_Audience) AS TotalAudienceIn2007 FROM festival_detail WHERE Year = 2007;
result_summary: Fetch the total number of audience members for all festivals that occurred in the year 2007.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, T3.Festival_Name, T2.Result FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T3.Chair_Name = 'Raymond Floyd';
result_summary: Fetch the artwork's name, the festival's name, and the nomination result for all festivals chaired by Raymond Floyd.

sql: SELECT Name, Type FROM artwork WHERE Artwork_ID IN (SELECT Artwork_ID FROM nomination WHERE Festival_ID = (SELECT Festival_ID FROM festival_detail WHERE Festival_Name = 'Flower Awards'));
result_summary: Fetch the names and types of all artworks that were nominated for the 'Flower Awards' festival.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name FROM festival_detail AS T1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM nomination AS T2 WHERE T2.Festival_ID = T1.Festival_ID AND T2.Result = 'Won');
result_summary: Fetch the names of festivals that have at least one winning nomination.

sql: WITH FestivalNominations AS (SELECT Festival_ID, COUNT(Artwork_ID) as NominationCount FROM nomination GROUP BY Festival_ID) SELECT fd.Festival_Name, fn.NominationCount FROM FestivalNominations fn JOIN festival_detail fd ON fn.Festival_ID = fd.Festival_ID ORDER BY fn.NominationCount DESC;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each festival and its total number of nominations, ordered by the nomination count in descending order.

sql: SELECT T1.Type, COUNT(T1.Artwork_ID) AS WinCount FROM artwork AS T1 JOIN nomination AS T2 ON T1.Artwork_ID = T2.Artwork_ID WHERE T2.Result = 'Won' GROUP BY T1.Type HAVING COUNT(T1.Artwork_ID) > 0;
result_summary: Fetch the total win count for each artwork type, filtered to include only types with more than zero wins.

sql: SELECT T1.Name, (SELECT T2.Festival_Name FROM festival_detail AS T2 JOIN nomination AS T3 ON T2.Festival_ID = T3.Festival_ID WHERE T3.Artwork_ID = T1.Artwork_ID AND T3.Result = 'Won' LIMIT 1) AS WinningFestival FROM artwork AS T1 WHERE T1.Artwork_ID = 2;
result_summary: Fetch the name of the artwork with an ID of 2, along with the name of a single festival where it won an award.

sql: SELECT Name FROM artwork WHERE Artwork_ID NOT IN (SELECT Artwork_ID FROM nomination WHERE Festival_ID = 2);
result_summary: Fetch the names of artworks that have not been nominated for the festival with an ID of 2.

sql: SELECT T2.Festival_Name, SUM(CASE WHEN T1.Result = 'Won' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Wins, SUM(CASE WHEN T1.Result = 'Nominated' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Nominations FROM nomination AS T1 JOIN festival_detail AS T2 ON T1.Festival_ID = T2.Festival_ID GROUP BY T2.Festival_Name;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each festival along with the total count of its associated wins and nominations.

sql: SELECT A.Festival_Name AS Festival1, B.Festival_Name AS Festival2, A.Year FROM festival_detail A JOIN festival_detail B ON A.Year = B.Year AND A.Festival_ID < B.Festival_ID WHERE A.Year = 2007;
result_summary: Fetch pairs of different festival names that occurred in the same year, specifically for the year 2007.

sql: SELECT a.Name, fd.Festival_Name, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY fd.Festival_ID ORDER BY a.Artwork_ID DESC) as ArtworkRank FROM artwork AS a JOIN nomination AS n ON a.Artwork_ID = n.Artwork_ID JOIN festival_detail AS fd ON n.Festival_ID = fd.Festival_ID;
result_summary: Fetch the artwork name, festival name, and a rank of the artwork within each festival, ordered by the artwork's ID in descending order.

sql: SELECT Name FROM artwork WHERE Type = 'Program Music/Variety Show' UNION SELECT Festival_Name FROM festival_detail WHERE Chair_Name = 'Doug Ford';
result_summary: Fetch a list of names, combining artworks of the 'Program Music/Variety Show' type with festivals chaired by 'Doug Ford'.

sql: SELECT Festival_Name FROM festival_detail WHERE Festival_ID = (SELECT Festival_ID FROM (SELECT Festival_ID, COUNT(*) AS nomination_count FROM nomination GROUP BY Festival_ID ORDER BY nomination_count DESC LIMIT 1) AS T1);
result_summary: Fetch the name of the festival that has the highest number of nominations.

sql: SELECT * FROM Student WHERE city_code = 'BAL';
result_summary: Fetch all available information for students who are located in the city with the code 'BAL'.

sql: SELECT Allergy FROM Allergy_Type WHERE AllergyType = 'food';
result_summary: Fetch the list of allergies where the allergy type is 'food'.

sql: SELECT StuID FROM Has_Allergy WHERE Allergy = 'Cat';
result_summary: Fetch the student IDs of all students who have an allergy to cats.

sql: SELECT count(*) FROM Student WHERE Sex = 'F';
result_summary: Fetch the total count of students who are female.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student ORDER BY Age DESC;
result_summary: Fetch the first name, last name, and age of all students, ordered by age in descending order.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID WHERE T2.Allergy = 'Shellfish';
result_summary: Fetch the first and last names of all students who have a shellfish allergy.

sql: SELECT T1.StuID, T2.AllergyType FROM Has_Allergy AS T1 JOIN Allergy_Type AS T2 ON T1.Allergy = T2.Allergy;
result_summary: List each student's ID alongside the type of each allergy they have.

sql: SELECT Major, COUNT(StuID) FROM Student GROUP BY Major;
result_summary: Fetch the total count of students for each major.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.Allergy FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy WHERE T3.AllergyType = 'food';
result_summary: Fetches the first name, last name, and specific allergy for all students who have a food allergy.

sql: SELECT Sex, AVG(Age) FROM Student GROUP BY Sex;
result_summary: Fetch the average age of students, grouped by sex.

sql: SELECT Allergy, COUNT(StuID) AS NumberOfStudents FROM Has_Allergy GROUP BY Allergy;
result_summary: Fetch a list of all allergies and the total count of students who have each one.

sql: SELECT LName FROM Student WHERE Advisor = 7712;
result_summary: Fetch the last name of the student whose advisor has an ID of 7712.

sql: SELECT T1.LName, T1.Fname, COUNT(T2.Allergy) AS AllergyCount FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID GROUP BY T1.StuID HAVING COUNT(T2.Allergy) > 1;
result_summary: Fetch the first name, last name, and total allergy count for students who have more than one allergy.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName FROM Student WHERE StuID NOT IN (SELECT StuID FROM Has_Allergy);
result_summary: Fetch the first and last names of all students who do not have any recorded allergies.

sql: SELECT T1.AllergyType, COUNT(T2.StuID) AS StudentCount FROM Allergy_Type AS T1 LEFT JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.Allergy = T2.Allergy GROUP BY T1.AllergyType;
result_summary: Fetch a count of students for each allergy type.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Age FROM Student WHERE Age > (SELECT AVG(Age) FROM Student);
result_summary: Fetch the first name, last name, and age of students whose age is greater than the average age of all students.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Student AS T2 ON T1.city_code = T2.city_code WHERE T1.StuID < T2.StuID;
result_summary: Fetch the first and last names of all students who live in the same city as another student.

sql: SELECT Fname, LName, Major, Age FROM (SELECT *, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY Major ORDER BY Age ASC) as rn FROM Student) AS T WHERE rn = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the first name, last name, major, and age for the youngest student in each academic major.

sql: SELECT T1.StuID FROM Has_Allergy AS T1 WHERE T1.Allergy = 'Shellfish' INTERSECT SELECT T2.StuID FROM Has_Allergy AS T2 WHERE T2.Allergy = 'Tree Pollen';
result_summary: Fetch the student IDs of students who are allergic to both shellfish and tree pollen.

sql: SELECT T1.StuID FROM Has_Allergy AS T1 JOIN Allergy_Type AS T2 ON T1.Allergy = T2.Allergy WHERE T2.AllergyType = 'food' EXCEPT SELECT StuID FROM Has_Allergy WHERE Allergy = 'Eggs';
result_summary: Fetch the student IDs of those who have a food allergy but are not allergic to eggs.

sql: SELECT T3.AllergyType, avg(T1.Age) FROM Student AS T1 JOIN Has_Allergy AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID JOIN Allergy_Type AS T3 ON T2.Allergy = T3.Allergy GROUP BY T3.AllergyType;
result_summary: Find the average age of students for each type of allergy.

sql: WITH StudentAllergyCounts AS (SELECT StuID, COUNT(Allergy) as num_allergies FROM Has_Allergy GROUP BY StuID) SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, T2.num_allergies FROM Student AS T1 JOIN StudentAllergyCounts AS T2 ON T1.StuID = T2.StuID ORDER BY T2.num_allergies DESC;
result_summary: Fetch the first name, last name, and total number of allergies for each student, ordered in descending order by the allergy count.

sql: SELECT Advisor FROM Student WHERE StuID IN (SELECT StuID FROM Has_Allergy) GROUP BY Advisor ORDER BY COUNT(StuID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Find the advisor with the most students who have allergies.

sql: SELECT T1.Fname, T1.LName, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Has_Allergy AS T2 WHERE T2.StuID = T1.StuID) as allergy_count FROM Student AS T1 WHERE T1.city_code = 'HKG';
result_summary: Fetch the first name, last name, and total allergy count for each student from the city with the code 'HKG'.

sql: SELECT T2.AllergyType FROM Has_Allergy AS T1 JOIN Allergy_Type AS T2 ON T1.Allergy = T2.Allergy JOIN Student AS T3 ON T1.StuID = T3.StuID WHERE T3.Major = 600 GROUP BY T2.AllergyType ORDER BY COUNT(T1.StuID) DESC LIMIT 1;
result_summary: Fetch the most common allergy type for students with a major ID of 600.

sql: SELECT product_name, product_description, daily_hire_cost FROM Products_for_Hire WHERE product_type_code = 'Cutlery';
result_summary: Fetch the product name, description, and daily hire cost for all hireable products classified as cutlery.

sql: SELECT coupon_id, date_issued, coupon_amount FROM Discount_Coupons WHERE coupon_amount > 500;
result_summary: Fetch the coupon ID, date issued, and amount for all discount coupons with a value greater than 500.

sql: SELECT first_name, last_name, date_became_customer FROM Customers WHERE good_or_bad_customer = 'good' AND gender_mf = '0';
result_summary: Fetch the first name, last name, and the date they became a customer for all 'good' customers of a specific gender.

sql: SELECT booking_id, customer_id, amount_payable FROM Bookings WHERE booking_status_code = 'Provisional' AND returned_damaged_yn = '1';
result_summary: Fetch the booking ID, customer ID, and amount payable for provisional bookings that were returned damaged.

sql: SELECT payment_id, booking_id, amount_due, amount_paid FROM Payments WHERE payment_type_code = 'Check' AND amount_paid_in_full_yn = '1';
result_summary: Fetch the payment ID, booking ID, amount due, and amount paid for all payments that have been paid in full by check.

sql: SELECT c.first_name, c.last_name, b.booking_id, b.booking_status_code, b.booking_start_date FROM Customers AS c JOIN Bookings AS b ON c.customer_id = b.customer_id WHERE c.customer_id = 3;
result_summary: Fetch the first name, last name, and all booking details (ID, status code, and start date) for a specific customer with an ID of 3.

sql: SELECT b.booking_id, p.payment_id, p.payment_date, p.amount_paid FROM Bookings AS b JOIN Payments AS p ON b.booking_id = p.booking_id WHERE p.payment_id = 1;
result_summary: Retrieve booking and payment information, including the payment date and amount paid, for the specific payment with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT c.first_name, c.last_name, dc.coupon_amount, dc.date_issued FROM Customers AS c JOIN Discount_Coupons AS dc ON c.coupon_id = dc.coupon_id WHERE c.customer_id = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the first name, last name, coupon amount, and the date a coupon was issued for a specific customer with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT p.product_name, p.daily_hire_cost, pb.booked_count, pb.booked_amount FROM Products_for_Hire AS p JOIN Products_Booked AS pb ON p.product_id = pb.product_id WHERE pb.booking_id = 13 AND pb.returned_yn = '1';
result_summary: Fetch the product name, daily hire cost, booked quantity, and total amount for all returned items associated with booking ID 13.

sql: SELECT b.booking_id, b.customer_id, b.amount_payable, b.amount_of_discount FROM Bookings AS b JOIN Products_Booked AS pb ON b.booking_id = pb.booking_id WHERE pb.product_id = 1 AND pb.returned_late_yn = '1';
result_summary: Fetch the booking and payment details for all bookings where product ID 1 was returned late.

sql: SELECT p.product_name, v.status_date, v.available_yn FROM View_Product_Availability AS v JOIN Products_for_Hire AS p ON v.product_id = p.product_id WHERE v.available_yn = '0' AND v.booking_id = 5;
result_summary: Fetch the name, status date, and availability of products for hire that are currently unavailable for booking ID 5.

sql: SELECT c.good_or_bad_customer, COUNT(b.booking_id) AS total_bookings, AVG(b.amount_payable) AS average_booking_value FROM Customers AS c JOIN Bookings AS b ON c.customer_id = b.customer_id GROUP BY c.good_or_bad_customer;
result_summary: Retrieve the total booking count and average booking value, aggregated by the 'good' or 'bad' customer classification.

sql: WITH CustomerRank AS (SELECT first_name, last_name, gender_mf, date_became_customer, RANK() OVER(PARTITION BY gender_mf ORDER BY date_became_customer ASC) as rank_num FROM Customers) SELECT first_name, last_name, gender_mf, date_became_customer FROM CustomerRank WHERE rank_num <= 5;
result_summary: Retrieve the names, gender, and sign-up date for the five earliest customers to join from each gender group.

sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, SUM(b.amount_outstanding) AS total_outstanding, SUM(p.amount_paid) AS total_paid FROM Customers AS c JOIN Bookings AS b ON c.customer_id = b.customer_id JOIN Payments AS p ON c.customer_id = p.customer_id WHERE c.good_or_bad_customer = 'good' GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name HAVING SUM(p.amount_paid) < SUM(b.amount_payable);
result_summary: Fetch the ID, name, total outstanding amount, and total paid amount for 'good' customers who have paid less than their total payable amount.

sql: SELECT p.product_id, p.product_name, (SELECT c.first_name || ' ' || c.last_name FROM Customers c JOIN Bookings b ON c.customer_id = b.customer_id JOIN Products_Booked pb ON b.booking_id = pb.booking_id WHERE pb.product_id = p.product_id ORDER BY b.booking_end_date DESC LIMIT 1) AS last_customer FROM Products_for_Hire AS p;
result_summary: Fetch the ID and name for each product for hire, along with the name of the customer who most recently booked that product.

sql: SELECT customer_id, first_name, last_name, 'Good Customer Since 2017' AS description FROM Customers WHERE good_or_bad_customer = 'good' AND SUBSTR(date_became_customer, 1, 4) = '2017' UNION ALL SELECT customer_id, first_name, last_name, 'Good Customer Since 2015' AS description FROM Customers WHERE good_or_bad_customer = 'good' AND SUBSTR(date_became_customer, 1, 4) = '2015';
result_summary: Fetch the customer ID, first name, last name, and a descriptive label for all 'good' customers who joined in either 2017 or 2015.

sql: SELECT DISTINCT c.first_name, c.last_name FROM Customers AS c JOIN Bookings AS b ON c.customer_id = b.customer_id JOIN Products_Booked AS pb ON b.booking_id = pb.booking_id JOIN Products_for_Hire AS pfh ON pb.product_id = pfh.product_id JOIN Payments AS p ON b.booking_id = p.booking_id WHERE pfh.product_type_code = 'Cutlery' AND p.payment_type_code = 'Credit Card' AND b.booking_status_code = 'Confirmed';
result_summary: Fetch the unique first and last names of customers who have a confirmed booking for a 'Cutlery' product and paid for it with a 'Credit Card'.

sql: SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name FROM Customers AS c LEFT JOIN Payments AS p ON c.customer_id = p.customer_id WHERE p.payment_id IS NULL;
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Retrieve the ID, first name, and last name of all customers who have never made a payment.

sql: SELECT c1.first_name, c1.last_name, c2.first_name, c2.last_name, c1.date_became_customer FROM Customers AS c1 JOIN Customers AS c2 ON DATE(c1.date_became_customer) = DATE(c2.date_became_customer) AND c1.customer_id < c2.customer_id;
result_summary: Fetch the names of pairs of different customers who became customers on the same day, along with their shared join date.

sql: SELECT T1.customer_id, T1.first_name, T1.last_name FROM Customers AS T1 JOIN (SELECT customer_id FROM Bookings WHERE booking_status_code = 'Confirmed' INTERSECT SELECT customer_id FROM Bookings WHERE booking_status_code = 'Provisional') AS T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id;
result_summary: Fetch the ID, first name, and last name of customers who have bookings with both 'Confirmed' and 'Provisional' statuses.

sql: SELECT booking_status_code, AVG(products_per_booking) AS avg_products_count FROM (SELECT b.booking_id, b.booking_status_code, COUNT(pb.product_id) AS products_per_booking FROM Bookings AS b JOIN Products_Booked AS pb ON b.booking_id = pb.booking_id GROUP BY b.booking_id, b.booking_status_code) AS booking_details GROUP BY booking_status_code;
result_summary: Fetch the average number of products per booking, categorized by the booking's status code.

sql: SELECT booking_id, amount_payable, booking_status_code, CASE WHEN amount_payable > 350 THEN 'High Value' WHEN amount_payable BETWEEN 200 AND 350 THEN 'Medium Value' ELSE 'Low Value' END AS value_category, CASE WHEN returned_damaged_yn = '1' THEN 'Damaged' ELSE 'Not Damaged' END AS return_condition FROM Bookings WHERE booking_status_code = 'Confirmed';
result_summary: Fetch details for all confirmed bookings, categorizing each by its monetary value and its return condition.

sql: SELECT product_name, daily_hire_cost FROM Products_for_Hire WHERE daily_hire_cost > (SELECT AVG(daily_hire_cost) FROM Products_for_Hire);
result_summary: Fetch the product name and daily hire cost for all products with a daily hire cost greater than the average.

sql: SELECT CustomerId, FirstName, LastName, Company, Address, City FROM Customer WHERE City = 'São José dos Campos' AND Country = 'Brazil';
result_summary: Retrieve the ID, name, company, and address details for all customers located in the city of São José dos Campos, Brazil.

sql: SELECT EmployeeId, FirstName, LastName, Title, Email FROM Employee WHERE Title = 'Sales Support Agent';
result_summary: Fetch the employee ID, first name, last name, title, and email for all employees with the title 'Sales Support Agent'.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Genre WHERE Name = 'Rock';
result_summary: Fetch the name of the specific genre 'Rock' from the genre table.

sql: SELECT Name FROM MediaType WHERE MediaTypeId = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name of the media type with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Playlist WHERE PlaylistId = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the name of the playlist with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT T.Name AS TrackName, T.Composer, T.Milliseconds FROM Track AS T JOIN Album AS A ON T.AlbumId = A.AlbumId WHERE A.Title = 'Restless and Wild';
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Fetch the name, composer, and duration in milliseconds for all tracks from the album 'Restless and Wild'.

sql: SELECT I.InvoiceId, T.Name AS TrackName, IL.UnitPrice, IL.Quantity FROM InvoiceLine AS IL JOIN Invoice AS I ON IL.InvoiceId = I.InvoiceId JOIN Track AS T ON IL.TrackId = T.TrackId WHERE I.InvoiceId = 1;
result_summary: Fetch the invoice ID, track name, unit price, and quantity for all line items belonging to the invoice with an ID of 1.

sql: SELECT A.Title AS AlbumTitle FROM Album AS A JOIN Artist AS Ar ON A.ArtistId = Ar.ArtistId WHERE Ar.Name = 'Accept';
result_summary: Fetch the album titles for the artist named 'Accept'.

sql: SELECT C.FirstName, C.LastName, I.InvoiceId, I.InvoiceDate, I.Total FROM Customer AS C JOIN Invoice AS I ON C.CustomerId = I.CustomerId WHERE C.FirstName = 'Leonie' AND C.LastName = 'Köhler';
result_summary: Retrieve the first name, last name, and all associated invoice details, including ID, date, and total, for the specific customer named Leonie Köhler.

sql: SELECT T.Name AS TrackName, G.Name AS GenreName, M.Name AS MediaTypeName FROM Track AS T JOIN Genre AS G ON T.GenreId = G.GenreId JOIN MediaType AS M ON T.MediaTypeId = M.MediaTypeId WHERE G.Name = 'Rock' AND M.Name = 'MPEG audio file';
result_summary: This query fetches the track name, genre name, and media type for all tracks that belong to the 'Rock' genre and are in the 'MPEG audio file' format.

sql: SELECT C.Email, C.Country, E.FirstName AS SupportRepFirstName, E.LastName AS SupportRepLastName FROM Customer AS C JOIN Employee AS E ON C.SupportRepId = E.EmployeeId WHERE C.Country = 'Brazil';
result_summary: Fetch the email and country of customers from Brazil, along with the first and last name of their assigned support representative.

sql: SELECT T.Name AS TrackName FROM Track AS T JOIN PlaylistTrack AS PT ON T.TrackId = PT.TrackId WHERE PT.PlaylistId = 8;
result_summary: Fetch the names of all tracks that belong to the playlist with an ID of 8.

sql: SELECT Name FROM Artist WHERE ArtistId IN (SELECT ArtistId FROM Album WHERE Title = 'For Those About To Rock We Salute You');
result_summary: Fetch the name of the artist who released the album titled 'For Those About To Rock We Salute You'.

sql: WITH SalesAgentSales AS (SELECT E.EmployeeId, E.FirstName, E.LastName, I.Total FROM Employee AS E JOIN Customer AS C ON E.EmployeeId = C.SupportRepId JOIN Invoice AS I ON C.CustomerId = I.CustomerId WHERE E.Title = 'Sales Support Agent') SELECT FirstName, LastName, COUNT(Total) AS TotalInvoices, SUM(Total) AS TotalSalesValue FROM SalesAgentSales GROUP BY EmployeeId, FirstName, LastName ORDER BY TotalSalesValue DESC;
result_summary: This query retrieves the name, total number of invoices, and total sales value for each sales support agent, ordered by the highest total sales value.

sql: SELECT A.Title AS AlbumTitle, T.Name AS TrackName, T.Milliseconds, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY T.AlbumId ORDER BY T.Milliseconds DESC) AS LengthRank FROM Track AS T JOIN Album AS A ON T.AlbumId = A.AlbumId WHERE A.ArtistId = (SELECT ArtistId FROM Artist WHERE Name = 'Accept');
result_summary: Fetch the album title, track name, and length for all tracks by the artist 'Accept', ranking each track by its length from longest to shortest within its respective album.

sql: SELECT T.TrackId, T.Name AS TrackName, G.Name AS GenreName FROM Track AS T JOIN Genre AS G ON T.GenreId = G.GenreId WHERE T.TrackId IN (SELECT PT.TrackId FROM PlaylistTrack AS PT JOIN Playlist AS P ON PT.PlaylistId = P.PlaylistId WHERE P.Name = 'Music') AND T.TrackId NOT IN (SELECT T2.TrackId FROM Track AS T2 JOIN Genre AS G2 ON T2.GenreId = G2.GenreId WHERE G2.Name = 'Rock');
result_summary: Fetch the ID, name, and genre for all tracks that are part of the 'Music' playlist, excluding those of the 'Rock' genre.

sql: SELECT c.FirstName, c.LastName, i.InvoiceId, i.Total, (SELECT AVG(Total) FROM Invoice WHERE CustomerId = c.CustomerId) AS CustomerAverageInvoice FROM Customer AS c JOIN Invoice AS i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId WHERE c.Country = 'Germany' ORDER BY c.CustomerId, i.InvoiceDate;
result_summary: This query fetches the first name, last name, invoice ID, and total for each invoice, alongside the customer's overall average invoice total, specifically for customers in Germany, ordered by customer and then by invoice date.

sql: SELECT Genre.Name, COUNT(Track.TrackId) AS NumberOfTracks FROM Genre JOIN Track ON Genre.GenreId = Track.GenreId JOIN InvoiceLine ON Track.TrackId = InvoiceLine.TrackId GROUP BY Genre.Name HAVING COUNT(Track.TrackId) > 100 ORDER BY NumberOfTracks DESC;
result_summary: Fetch the name of each music genre and the count of its sold tracks for genres with more than 100 sales, ordered by the track count in descending order.

sql: SELECT E1.FirstName || ' ' || E1.LastName AS Employee, E2.FirstName || ' ' || E2.LastName AS Manager FROM Employee AS E1 LEFT JOIN Employee AS E2 ON E1.ReportsTo = E2.EmployeeId WHERE E1.City = 'Calgary';
result_summary: Fetch a list of employees and their direct managers for all employees based in the city of Calgary.

sql: SELECT A.Name AS ArtistName, COUNT(DISTINCT G.GenreId) AS NumberOfGenres FROM Artist A JOIN Album AL ON A.ArtistId = AL.ArtistId JOIN Track T ON AL.AlbumId = T.AlbumId JOIN Genre G ON T.GenreId = G.GenreId GROUP BY A.Name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT G.GenreId) > 1 ORDER BY NumberOfGenres DESC;
result_summary: Retrieve the names of artists with more than one genre and the count of their genres, ordered by the genre count in descending order.

sql: WITH CustomerSpending AS (SELECT CustomerId, SUM(Total) AS TotalSpent FROM Invoice GROUP BY CustomerId) SELECT C.FirstName, C.LastName, CS.TotalSpent FROM Customer C JOIN CustomerSpending CS ON C.CustomerId = CS.CustomerId WHERE CS.TotalSpent > (SELECT AVG(TotalSpent) FROM CustomerSpending) AND C.Country = 'USA';
result_summary: Fetch the first name, last name, and total spending for customers from the USA whose spending is greater than the average spending of all customers.

sql: SELECT P.Name AS PlaylistName, MT.Name AS MediaTypeName, COUNT(T.TrackId) AS TrackCount FROM Playlist P JOIN PlaylistTrack PT ON P.PlaylistId = PT.PlaylistId JOIN Track T ON PT.TrackId = T.TrackId JOIN MediaType MT ON T.MediaTypeId = MT.MediaTypeId GROUP BY P.Name, MT.Name ORDER BY P.Name, TrackCount DESC;
result_summary:
RESULT SUMMARY:
Fetch the count of tracks for each media type within every playlist, ordered by playlist name and then by the track count in descending order.

sql: SELECT C.Country, G.Name as TopGenre, MAX(T.PurchaseCount) AS Purchases FROM (SELECT IL.TrackId, COUNT(*) AS PurchaseCount FROM InvoiceLine IL GROUP BY IL.TrackId) T JOIN Track TR ON T.TrackId = TR.TrackId JOIN Genre G ON TR.GenreId = G.GenreId JOIN Invoice I ON T.TrackId = (SELECT TrackId FROM InvoiceLine WHERE InvoiceId = I.InvoiceId LIMIT 1) JOIN Customer C ON I.CustomerId = C.CustomerId GROUP BY C.Country, G.Name ORDER BY C.Country, Purchases DESC;
result_summary: Fetches a list of music genres for each country, ranked by the sales count of the most-purchased track within that genre.
